Analytics Cloud
===============

Analytics empowers business analysts and consumers with modern, AI-powered, self-service analytics capabilities for data preparation, visualization, enterprise reporting, augmented analysis, and natural language processing.

# Data Labeling

### [Oracle Cloud Infrastructure Documentation](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/home.htm)

* * *

All Pages

[Skip to main content](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/data-labeling/using/home.htm#dcoc-content-body)

Data Labeling
=============

Data Labeling is the process of identifying properties of documents, text, and images, and annotating them with those properties. The topic of a news article, the sentiment of a tweet, the caption of an image, important words spoken in an audio recording, the genre of a video are all examples of a data label.

[What's new](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/releasenotes/services/data-labeling/)

#### Get Started

[Learn about data labeling](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/data-labeling/using/about.htm "Find out about Data Labeling, what it is and how to use it.")

[Learn about datasets](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/data-labeling/using/datasets.htm "Learn about datasets and how you create and use them in Data Labeling.")

[Learn about work requests](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/data-labeling/using/work-requests.htm "Work requests are long-running operations on a dataset started by Data Labeling.")

[Data Labeling overview video](https://apexapps.oracle.com/pls/apex/f?p=44785:265:0:::265:P265_CONTENT_ID:31668)

[Product page](https://www.oracle.com/artificial-intelligence/data-labeling/)

#### Developer Tools

[Data Labeling API](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/api/#/en/datalabeling-dp/latest/)

[OCI SDKs and the CLI](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/API/Concepts/sdks.htm)

[Cloud Shell](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/API/Concepts/devcloudshellintro.htm)

[Data Labeling CLI](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/tools/oci-cli/3.2.0/oci_cli_docs/cmdref/data-labeling-service.html)

[Oracle Accelerated Data Science (ADS) SDK](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/tools/ads-sdk/latest/user_guide/data_labeling/index.html)

[Data Labeling samples in Github](https://github.com/oracle-samples/oci-data-science-ai-samples/tree/master/data_labeling_examples)

#### Prerequisites

[Before you begin](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/data-labeling/using/getting-started.htm#before-you-begin "Find out what you need in place before you can start using Data Labeling.")

[Create buckets](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/data-labeling/using/set-up-admin.htm#set-up-object-store "You must create buckets in a compartment in Object Storage before you can upload documents, images or text to Object Storage for use with Data Labeling.")

[Create IAM policies](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/data-labeling/using/set-up-identity-policies.htm "Data Labeling requires policies to be set in IAM to access resources to manage datasets and label records.")

#### Community

[Oracle Cloud Infrastructure blog](https://blogs.oracle.com/cloud-infrastructure/)

[Oracle Cloud Infrastructure community forum](https://community.oracle.com/tech/apps-infra/categories/18430-cloud-infrastructure)

[Oracle Machine Learning Blog](https://blogs.oracle.com/machinelearning/)

[Oracle AI and Data Science Blog](https://blogs.oracle.com/ai-and-datascience/)

[Videos](https://www.youtube.com/playlist?list=PLKCk3OyNwIzsgkJ4CKrkyCx334Veo6SSP)

#### Troubleshooting

[Known Issues](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/knownissues.htm#data-labeling)

[Troubleshooting Data Labeling](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/data-labeling/using/troubleshooting.htm "Use troubleshooting information to identify and address common issues that can occur while working with Data Labeling.")

#### Support

[Support Requests](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/GSG/Tasks/contactingsupport.htm)

[Create a service request](https://support.oracle.com/)

[Service end of life announcement](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/servicechanges.htm#DLS)

# Speech

* * *

All Pages

[Skip to main content](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/speech/home.htm#dcoc-content-body)

Speech
======

Speech can transcribe customer service calls, automate subtitling, and generate metadata for media assets to create a fully searchable archive.

[What's new](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/releasenotes/services/speech)

#### Get Started

[Learn about the service](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/speech/using/speech.htm#overview "You can use the Speech service to convert media files to readable text that's stored in JSON and SRT format.")

[Review key concepts](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/speech/using/speech.htm#concepts "These are the key Speech service concepts:")

[Learn about data handling](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/speech/using/data-handle.htm#faq_data "Learn how Speech handles your data.")

[Try Speech with the LiveLabs workshop](https://apexapps.oracle.com/pls/apex/dbpm/r/livelabs/view-workshop?wid=3135)

[Product page](https://www.oracle.com/artificial-intelligence/speech/)

#### Prerequisites

[Create the required policies](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/speech/using/policies.htm#policies "Learn about the resource policies including API permissions.")

#### Support

[Create a service request](https://support.oracle.com/)

[Using Live Transcribe](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/speech/using/using-live-transcribe.htm#using-live-transcribe "Create and manage live transcribe jobs in the Speech service.")

[Using Customization](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/speech/using/using-customizations.htm#using-customizations "Learn how to customize jobs.")

[Using Text to Speech](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/speech/using/using-tts.htm#using-tts "Learn how to use Text to speech.")

# Vision

### [Oracle Cloud Infrastructure Documentation](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/home.htm)

* * *

All Pages

[Skip to main content](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/vision/using/home.htm#dcoc-content-body)

Vision
======

Oracle Cloud Infrastructure Vision is a serverless, multi-tenant service, accessible using the Console, or over REST APIs. You can upload images to detect and classify objects in them. If you have lots of images, you can process them in batch using asynchronous API endpoints.

[What's new](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/releasenotes/services/data-flow/)

#### Get Started

[Overview of Vision](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/vision/using/overview.htm "Oracle Cloud Infrastructure Vision is a serverless, multi-tenant service, accessible using the Console, REST APIs, SDK, or CLI.")

[About Vision Policies](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/vision/using/about_vision_policies.htm "Learn about Vision's resource policies including API permissions.")

[Live Lab](https://apexapps.oracle.com/pls/apex/dbpm/r/livelabs/view-workshop?wid=931)

[Product page](https://www.oracle.com/artificial-intelligence/vision/)

#### Prerequisites

[Vision Policy Quick Set Up](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/vision/using/policies_quick_set_up.htm "You can use a template to quickly set up Vision policies to control who has access to the service, and the type of access for each group of users.")

#### Support

[Getting help](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/GSG/support/getting-help.htm)

[Create a service request](https://support.oracle.com/)

[Contact us using Cloud Customer Connect](https://cloudcustomerconnect.oracle.com/pages/home)

#### Developer Tools

[Vision API](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/api/#/en/vision/)

[SDKs and the CLI](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/API/Concepts/sdks.htm)

[Cloud Shell](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/API/Concepts/devcloudshellintro.htm)

#### How To

[Analyzing with the API](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/vision/using/api_models.htm "You can use the Vision API to analyze images and documents individually or images in batch.")

[Building a Custom Model](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/vision/using/custom_model_calling_stored_video.htm#custom_model_build "Build custom models in Vision to extract insights from images without needing data scientists.")

#### Community

[Oracle AI and Data Science Blog](https://blogs.oracle.com/ai-and-datascience/)

[Oracle Cloud Infrastructure blog](https://blogs.oracle.com/cloud-infrastructure/)

[Cloud infrastructure community forum](https://community.oracle.com/tech/apps-infra/categories/18430-cloud-infrastructure)

#### Tutorials

[Tutorials](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/vision/tutorials/index.htm "This set of tutorials guides you through a specific, simple use case to get you started with Vision quickly and easily.")

[Live Lab](https://apexapps.oracle.com/pls/apex/dbpm/r/livelabs/view-workshop?wid=931)

#### Concepts

[Batch Processing](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/vision/using/batch_processing.htm "When dealing with archival media, or analyzing many images that don't require an immediate response to a user, you might want to process the images asynchronously in Vision.")

[Custom Image Analysis Models](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/vision/using/custom_image_analysis_models.htm "Vision provides custom image analysis models that let you find and tag objects, text, and entire scenes in images, that are specific to your scenario.")

[Pretrained Document AI Models](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/vision/using/pretrained_doc_ai_models.htm "Vision provides pretrained document AI models that let you organize and extract text and structure from business documents.")

[Pretrained Image Analysis Models](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/vision/using/pretrained_image_analysis_models.htm "Vision provides pretrained image analysis AI models that let you to find and tag objects, text, and entire scenes in images.")

[Data Handling](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/vision/using/data-handling.htm "Vision's data handling.")

[Face Detection](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/vision/using/pretrained_image_analysis_models.htm#pretrained_image_analysis_face "Vision can detect and recognize faces in an image.")

[Stored Video Analysis](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/vision/using/stored_video_analysis.htm "Vision lets you process stored videos to identify labels, classify images, and detect objects and faces in each frame.")

[Stream Video Analysis](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/vision/using/video-stream-processing-top.htm "Stream Video Analysis is a fully managed, GPU-quickened real-time video analytics service on Oracle Cloud Infrastructure (OCI). It process live RTSP streams to deliver real-time insights using AI capabilities.")
  **New**

# Document Understanding

### [Oracle Cloud Infrastructure Documentation](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/home.htm)

* * *

All Pages

[Skip to main content](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/document-understanding/using/home.htm#dcoc-content-body)

Document Understanding
======================

Oracle Cloud Infrastructure Document Understanding is an AI service that lets developers extract text, tables, and other key data from document files through APIs and CLI tools. With Document Understanding, you can automate tedious business processing tasks with prebuilt AI models, and customize document extraction to fit your industry-specific needs.

[What's new](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/releasenotes/services/document-understanding/index.htm)

#### Get Started

[Service Overview](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/document-understanding/using/overview.htm "Document Understanding is a serverless, multitenant service, that you can use to detect and classify text, tables, and other key data from document files that you upload.")

[Getting Started](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/document-understanding/using/getting_started.htm "Learn how to get started with using Document Understanding.")

[IAM Policies for User Permission in Document Understanding](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/document-understanding/using/about_document-understanding_policies.htm "Learn about Document Understanding's resource policies including API permissions.")

[Product page](https://www.oracle.com/artificial-intelligence/document-understanding/)

#### Concepts

[Pretrained Document AI Models](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/document-understanding/using/pretrained_doc_ai_models.htm "Document Understanding provides pretrained models that let you organize and extract text and structure from business documents.")

[Custom Generative Models](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/document-understanding/using/custom-kv-generative-extraction.htm "The OCI Document Service features Key-Value Extraction powered by Large Multimodal Models (LMMs), offering enhanced accuracy and adaptability over conventional model-driven methods.")
  **New**

[Custom Classic Models](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/document-understanding/using/custom-models-about.htm "In Document Understanding, you can leverage custom models for extracting key-value pairs and classifying documents, tailoring the process to your specific needs.")

#### Support

[Support Requests](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/GSG/Tasks/contactingsupport.htm)

[Create a service request](https://support.oracle.com/)

#### Developer Tools

[Document Understanding API](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/api/#/en/document-understanding/latest/)

[SDKs and the CLI](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/API/Concepts/sdks.htm)

[Cloud Shell](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/API/Concepts/devcloudshellintro.htm)

#### How To

[Analyzing with the API](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/document-understanding/using/api_models.htm "You can use the Document Understanding API to analyze documents.")

[Using Pretrained Models in the Console](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/document-understanding/using/pretrained-doc-using.htm#custom_model_using_console)

#### Community

[Oracle AI and Data Science Blog](https://blogs.oracle.com/ai-and-datascience/)

# Support Requests

### [Oracle Cloud Infrastructure Documentation](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/home.htm)

* * *

All Pages

[Skip to main content](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/GSG/Tasks/contactingsupport.htm#dcoc-content-body)

Support Requests
================

Visit the Support Center to browse and create support requests for technical and billing requests, including service limit increases.

To visit the Support Center, [list support requests](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/GSG/support/list-incidents.htm "List support requests in Support Management. Visibility of support requests is controlled by the support request's associated user group and the privileges assigned to the current user account.")
.

**Note**  
  

*   To get help signing in, or to contact support when not signed in, see [Troubleshooting Signing In to the Console](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/GSG/Tasks/signinginIdentityDomain.htm "Get help signing in to the Oracle Cloud Console.")
    .
*   Support requests are available to paid accounts only. Customers using only Always Free resources and customers using Free Tier accounts aren't eligible for Oracle Support. If you need support, use [Support Chat](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/GSG/support/getting-help.htm#livechat)
     and [Cloud Customer Connect](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/GSG/support/getting-help.htm#Post_a_question_to_our_forums__CloudCustomerConnect)
    .

If the available resources didn't resolve your issue and you need to talk to someone, then you can create a support request. In addition to support for technical issues, you can open support requests if you need to:

*   Reset the password or unlock the account for the tenancy administrator
*   Add or change a tenancy administrator
*   Ask a question about billing and payments
*   Request a [service limit](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/General/service-limits/default.htm)
     increase
*   Request a root cause analysis (RCA)

**Note**  
  
Support requests (`incidents`) don't have **OCIDs** .

Open the **Help** menu ![Help menu](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/libraries/global-images/help-menu.png) and then select **Create a Support request**. For more guidance, see [Creating a Support Request](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/GSG/support/create-incident.htm "Create a support request in Support Management.")
.

The first time you open a support request, you're automatically taken through a series of steps to provision your support account. To make changes, or if you run into problems, then see [Configuring Your Oracle Support Account](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/GSG/Tasks/usingsupport.htm "Configure your Oracle support account by adding an email address to your IAM user account, or perform administrative tasks.")
.

You can perform the following support request tasks:

[Enable and validate a user to submit support requests.](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/GSG/support/validate-user.htm "Confirm that you have a support account and retrieve a list of user groups that you have access to.")

[List the OCI products that you can create technical support requests for.](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/GSG/support/list-incident-resource-types-taxonomy.htm "List products that you can create technical support requests for in Support Management. Products include service groups, services, service categories, and subcategories.")

[List the limits and current usage of your OCI services.](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/GSG/support/list-incident-resource-types-limit.htm "This page has moved.")

[List](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/GSG/support/list-incidents.htm "List support requests in Support Management. Visibility of support requests is controlled by the support request's associated user group and the privileges assigned to the current user account.")
, [create](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/GSG/support/create-incident.htm "Create a support request in Support Management.")
, [view details](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/GSG/support/get-incident.htm "Get the details for a support request in Support Management. Visibility of support requests is controlled by the support request's associated user group and the privileges assigned to the current user account.")
, and [update](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/GSG/support/update-incident.htm "Update support requests in Support Management.")
 your Oracle support requests.

[Configure your Oracle support account.](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/GSG/Tasks/usingsupport.htm "Configure your Oracle support account by adding an email address to your IAM user account, or perform administrative tasks.")

[Locate your OCI IDs.](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/GSG/Tasks/contactingsupport_topic-Locating_Oracle_Cloud_Infrastructure_IDs.htm "Find identifiers to include in your support requests.")

---

# Language

### [Oracle Cloud Infrastructure Documentation](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/home.htm)

* * *

All Pages

[Skip to main content](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/language/using/home.htm#dcoc-content-body)

Language
========

Language offers REST API for powerful text analytics and translations. Analyze vast amounts of unstructured text with ease, use pretrained and custom models. Perform sentiment analysis, key phrase extraction, text classification, named entity recognition and detect PII data in text. Tailor models for domain-specific tasks and effortlessly translate text across various languages. Language also supports document translation and asynchronous jobs for efficiently processing large-volume workloads.

[What's new](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/releasenotes/services/language/)

#### Get Started

[Learn about the Language service](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/language/using/overview.htm "Language is server-less and multi-tenant service that's accessible using REST API calls. With pretrained and custom models, you can process unstructured text and extract insights without data science expertise.")

[Try Language with the LiveLabs workshop](https://apexapps.oracle.com/pls/apex/dbpm/r/livelabs/view-workshop?wid=887)

[Product page](https://www.oracle.com/artificial-intelligence/language/)

# Compute Cloud@Customer Isolated

### [Oracle Cloud Infrastructure Documentation](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/home.htm)

* * *

All Pages

[Skip to main content](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/compute-cloud-at-customer-isolated/c3i/home.htm#dcoc-content-body)

Compute Cloud@Customer Isolated
===============================

Use Compute Cloud@Customer Isolated to deploy OCI services in locations disconnected from the internet. Build cloud workloads in environments where control, security and residency of confidential data are strictly regulated. Take advantage of OCI performance and efficiency while maintaining sovereignty of your data and the operation of underlying infrastructure.

[What's new](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/releasenotes/services/compute-cloud-at-customer-isolated/)

#### Get Started

[Overview](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/compute-cloud-at-customer-isolated/c3i/overview-compute-cloud-customer-isolated.htm#overview-ccatc-isolated "Use Compute Cloud@Customer Isolated to deploy Oracle Cloud Infrastructure (OCI) services in locations disconnected from the internet. Build cloud workloads in environments where control, security and residency of confidential data are strictly regulated.")

[Installation](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/compute-cloud-at-customer-isolated/c3i/installation.htm#installation "Before you can use Compute Cloud@Customer Isolated, you must prepare your site, install the infrastructure rack, and complete the initial configuration.")

[Infrastructure Administration](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/compute-cloud-at-customer-isolated/c3i/admin-infra.htm#admin-infrastructure "The Compute Cloud@Customer Isolated administrators manage and configure the infrastructure from the Service Enclave. This highly restricted part of the system provides tools for hardware and capacity management, tenancy control, and centralized monitoring of components at all system layers.")

[Identity and Access Management](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/compute-cloud-at-customer-isolated/c3i/identity.htm#iam-local "The Identity and Access Management Service (IAM) on Oracle Compute Cloud@Customer Isolated provides control over which users have what access to which resources in your local infrastructure tenancy.")

[Working with Cloud Resources](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/compute-cloud-at-customer-isolated/c3i/cloud-services.htm#cloud-resources "Compute Cloud@Customer Isolated can be considered a rack-scale deployment unit of OCI, built on the same APIs. The Compute Enclave is the user environment where cloud resources are created and managed. It's logically isolated from the infrastructure administration (Service Enclave) interfaces.")

#### Troubleshooting

[Limits](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/compute-cloud-at-customer-isolated/c3i/limits.htm#limits "The Compute Cloud@Customer Isolated limits presented here have been tested and are fully supported by Oracle.")

[Known issues](https://docs.oracle.com/en/engineered-systems/private-cloud-appliance/3.0-latest/relnotes/relnotes-known-issues.html)

#### Support

[Billing](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/compute-cloud-at-customer-isolated/c3i/where-to-find-billing-information.htm#where-to-find-billing-information "Find information about billing and cost management for Compute Cloud@Customer Isolated.")

[Support Bundles](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/compute-cloud-at-customer-isolated/c3i/admin-support-bundles.htm#support-bundles "Support bundles are files of diagnostic data collected from Compute Cloud@Customer Isolated, which are used to evaluate and fix problems.")

# Data Flow

Data Flow
=========

Oracle Cloud Infrastructure Data Flow is a fully managed service for running Apache Spark ™ applications. It lets developers to focus on their applications and provides an easy runtime environment to run them. It has an easy and simple user interface with API support for integration with applications and workflows. You don't need to spend any time on the underlying infrastructure, cluster provisioning, or software installation.

[What's new](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/releasenotes/services/data-flow/)

#### Get Started

[Get started with Data Flow](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/data-flow/using/dfs_getting_started.htm "Learn about Oracle Cloud Infrastructure Data Flow Engineering, what it is, what you need to do before you begin using it, including setting up policies and storage, loading data, and how to import and bundle Spark applications.")

[Learn about Spark streaming](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/data-flow/using/streaming-get-started.htm "Before you can use Spark streaming with Data Flow, you must set it up.")

[Product page](https://www.oracle.com/big-data/data-flow/)

[Use Pools with Applications and Runs](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/data-flow/using/pools.htm#pool_config "Use pools with Data Flow Applications and Runs.")

#### SQL Endpoints

# Oracle Multicloud Hub

### [Oracle Cloud Infrastructure Documentation](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/home.htm)

* * *

All Pages

[Skip to main content](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/multicloud-hub/home.htm#dcoc-content-body)

Oracle Multicloud Hub
=====================

Oracle Multicloud Hub (Multicloud Hub) is a single pane of glass in the OCI Console that provides an insight into your Oracle database services across one or more partner cloud service providers (CSPs).

You can use Multicloud Hub to:

*   **Answer FinOps questions** – List your OCI Multicloud subscription, the partner CSP account details, and if applicable, understand your private offer commitment details such as committed amount and timeframe.
*   **Quickly identify Multicloud resources** – You can list the Multicloud resources and navigate to the resource details in OCI and the partner CSP.
*   **Understand interconnectivity** – You can understand the mapping between:
    *   OCI compartments and partner cloud account constructs such as Microsoft Azure Subscription ID, Microsoft Azure Resource Group, or Amazon Web Services (AWS) Account ID through resource anchors.
    *   OCI virtual cloud networks and partner CSP networking constructs such as Microsoft Azure Virtual Networks (VNET), AWS Virtual Private Cloud (VPC), or Google Cloud Platform (GCP) VPC through network anchors.
*   **Plan for HA/DR** (high availability / disaster recovery) – Design your HA/DR strategy based on the mapping of OCI availability domains to partner CSP availability zones (physical and logical).

---

# Overview of Service Catalog

### [Oracle Cloud Infrastructure Documentation](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/home.htm)

* * *

All Pages

[Skip to main content](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Marketplace/overview_of_service_catalog.htm#dcoc-content-body)

Overview of Service Catalog
===========================

A Service Catalog enables your organization to create and manage catalogs of applications that are approved for use in your tenancy.

These applications are offered through marketplace in the form of image and stack listings. Applications can be a public application available on marketplace or a private application offered within the organization. Service Catalog allows organizations to centrally manage applications and helps achieve consistent governance and compliance requirements. Approved or restricted sets of applications can reduce risks of misuse or overspending by end users, giving administrators peace of mind. End users can quickly deploy only approved applications that follow constraints set by the organization.

Required IAM Policy
-------------------

To use Oracle Cloud Infrastructure, you must be granted security access in a **policy** by an administrator.

This access is required whether you're using the Console or the REST API with an SDK, CLI, or other tool. If you get a message that you don't have permission or are unauthorized, verify with your administrator what type of access you have and which **compartment** to work in.

If you're new to policies, see [Getting Started with Policies](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/Identity/Concepts/policygetstarted.htm#Getting_Started_with_Policies)
 and [Common Policies](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/Identity/Concepts/commonpolicies.htm#top)
.

For administrators, the following policies enable the creation of applications in a Service Catalogand provide access to those applications to users. For any policies, if you want to reduce the scope of access to a particular compartment, specify the compartment instead of the tenancy.

*   The following policy gives the specified example group the ability to list, view, create, update, delete, or move private applications in all compartments.
    
        allow group CatalogAdmins to manage private-applications in tenancy
    
*   The following policy gives the specified example group the ability to list, view, create, update, delete, or move Service Catalogs in all compartments.
    
        allow group CatalogAdmins to manage service-catalogs in tenancy
    
*   The following policy gives the specified example group the ability to browse and launch Service Catalog applications in all compartments:
    
        allow group CatalogUsers to inspect service-catalog-contents in tenancy
    
*   The following policy gives the specified example group the ability to browse and launch applications only from within a specific service catalog:
    
        allow group CatalogUsers to inspect service-catalog-contents in tenancy where service-catalog.id='ocid1.servicecatalog.oc1.iad.aaaaaaaaexampleocid'
    
*   The following policy gives the specified example group the ability to browse and launch applications only from within those service catalogs which are hosted in the compartment Project-A:
    
        allow group CatalogUsers to inspect service-catalog-contents in compartment Project-A
    

If you need to write more restrictive policies, see the policy reference on which these policies were based: [Details for the Marketplace Service](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/Identity/Reference/marketplacepolicyreference.htm#Details_for_the_Marketplace_Service)
 and [Details for the Core Services](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/Identity/Reference/corepolicyreference.htm#Details_for_the_Core_Services)
, as needed.

Resource Identifiers
--------------------

Most types of Oracle Cloud Infrastructure resources have a unique, Oracle-assigned identifier called an Oracle Cloud ID (OCID).

For information about the OCID format and other ways to identify your resources, see [Resource Identifiers](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/General/Concepts/identifiers.htm#Resource_Identifiers)
. While the resources created from Marketplace images and stacks have an OCID to identify them, the listings themselves have a listing ID and a package version ID for every package version in the listing. Listing IDs are numeric values. Package version IDs are string values. These identifiers are unique to Marketplace and unrelated to OCIDs.

Ways to Access Oracle Cloud Infrastructure
------------------------------------------

You can access Oracle Cloud Infrastructure using the Console (a browser-based interface) or the REST API. Instructions for the Console and API are included in topics throughout this guide.

For a list of available SDKs, see [Software Development Kits and Command Line Interface](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/API/Concepts/sdks.htm#Software_Development_Kits_and_Command_Line_Interface)
.

To access the Console, you must use a [supported browser](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/GSG/Tasks/signinginIdentityDomain.htm#Supporte)
. You can use the **Console** link at the top of this page to go to the sign-in page. You are prompted to enter your cloud tenant, your user name, and your password.

Authentication and Authorization
--------------------------------

Each service in Oracle Cloud Infrastructure integrates with IAM for authentication and authorization, for all interfaces (the Console, SDK or CLI, and REST API).

An administrator in your organization needs to set up **groups**, **compartments**, and **policies** that control which users can access which services, which resources, and the type of access. For example, the policies control who can create new users, create and manage the cloud network, launch instances, create buckets, download objects, etc. For more information, see [Getting Started with Policies](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/Identity/Concepts/policygetstarted.htm#Getting_Started_with_Policies)
. For specific details about writing policies for each of the different services, see [Policy Reference](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/Identity/policyreference/policyreference.htm)
.

If you're a regular user (not an administrator) who needs to use the Oracle Cloud Infrastructure resources that your company owns, contact your administrator to set up a user ID for you. The administrator can confirm which compartment or compartments you should be using.

For the actual policy statements required to perform tasks related to Marketplace, see the topic specific to the task.

*   [Overview of Service Catalog](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Marketplace/overview_of_service_catalog.htm#overview_of_service_catalog)
    
*   [Required IAM Policy](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Marketplace/overview_of_service_catalog.htm#servicecatalog_topic_Required_IAM_Policy)
    
*   [Resource Identifiers](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Marketplace/overview_of_service_catalog.htm#servicecatalogidentifiers)
    
*   [Ways to Access Oracle Cloud Infrastructure](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Marketplace/overview_of_service_catalog.htm#servicecatalogaccess)
    
*   [Authentication and Authorization](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Marketplace/overview_of_service_catalog.htm#servicecatalogauth)

---

# Oracle AI Database@AWS

### [Oracle Cloud Infrastructure Documentation](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/home.htm)

* * *

All Pages

[Skip to main content](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/database-at-aws/oaaws.htm#dcoc-content-body)

Oracle AI Database@AWS
======================

Oracle AI Database@AWS is a cloud database service that lets you to access OCI managed Oracle Exadata infrastructure inside AWS data centers.

The service lets you to migrate on-premises Oracle workloads to AWS, giving your business-critical applications low-latency connectivity to your Oracle databases. With Oracle AI Database@AWS, you also have access to Oracle Real Application Clusters (RAC) and Oracle Exadata performance for mission-critical applications in AWS. The service integrates with other AWS services, and you get a single invoice through AWS Marketplace that includes Oracle AI Database@AWS billing. Oracle AI Database@AWS usage counts toward AWS commitments.

The following high-level architecture diagram shows how an OCI region (parent site) is connected with an AWS region's Availability Zone (child site) through an OCI managed network as part of Oracle AI Database@AWS. An application server deployed on Amazon EC2 connects to the Oracle databases deployed in an Oracle Database (ODB) network through ODB peering. The control plane, which is highly available and fault-tolerant, is deployed in the OCI Region and offers a seamless database management and administration experience.

[![A diagram of the relationship between an OCI parent region and an AWS region offering Oracle AI Database@AWS.](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/database-at-aws/images/odbaws-region-architecture-diagram.png "Click to expand")](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/database-at-aws/images/odbaws-region-architecture-diagram.png)

Benefits of Using Oracle AI Database@AWS
----------------------------------------

Oracle AI Database@AWS offers a powerful and flexible solution for running Oracle workloads directly within AWS. Key benefits include:

*   [Simplified Migration](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/database-at-aws/oaaws.htm#simplified-migration)
    
*   [Low-Latency Connectivity](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/database-at-aws/oaaws.htm#low-latency-connectivity)
    
*   [Unified Management and Operations](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/database-at-aws/oaaws.htm#unified-management-ops)
    
*   [Seamless Integration](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/database-at-aws/oaaws.htm#seamless-integration)
    

### Simplified Migration

Oracle AI Database@AWS lets you seamlessly migrate your Oracle workloads to either Oracle Exadata Database Service on Dedicated Infrastructure or Oracle Autonomous AI Database on Dedicated Exadata Infrastructure, deployed within AWS. These migrations require minimal database changes, retain full Oracle feature sets, maintain architectural compatibility, and deliver performance equal to on-premises environments. Supported migration tools include RMAN, Oracle Data Guard, transportable tablespaces, Oracle Data Pump, Oracle GoldenGate, and Oracle Zero Downtime Migration (ZDM).

### Low-Latency Connectivity

Hosting Oracle Database close to your AWS applications reduces network latency, which ensures faster response times and improved application performance. This proximity minimizes data transfer delays and optimizes system efficiency.

### Unified Management and Operations

Oracle AI Database@AWS offers a cohesive operational experience with integrated purchasing, support, and lifecycle management across Oracle and AWS. It supports your existing AWS commitments and Oracle license entitlements. You can manage Oracle resources using AWS's APIs, CLI, and SDKs, which internally invoke the necessary OCI APIs for provisioning and management.

### Seamless Integration

Oracle AI Database@AWS seamlessly integrates with core AWS services like Amazon EC2, VPC, and IAM, enabling cohesive security and infrastructure management. It also supports AWS-native tools such as Amazon CloudWatch for monitoring, Amazon EventBridge for automation, and Amazon S3 for highly durable backups.

Available Database Services
---------------------------

Oracle AI Database@AWS offers the following Oracle database services:

| Service name | Available versions | Private Offer eligible? | Public Offer eligible? |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| [Oracle Exadata Database Service on Dedicated Infrastructure](https://docs.oracle.com/en/engineered-systems/exadata-cloud-service/ecscm/exadata-cloud-infrastructure-overview.html) | 19c, 26ai | Yes | No  |
| [Oracle Autonomous AI Database on Dedicated Exadata Infrastructure](https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/paas/autonomous-database/dedicated/adbaa/index.html) | 19c, 26ai | Yes | No  |
| [Oracle Database Autonomous Recovery Service](https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/paas/recovery-service/dbrsu/overview-recovery-service.html) | NA  | Yes | No  |
| [Oracle Autonomous AI Database Serverless](https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/paas/autonomous-database/serverless/adbsb/autonomous-intro-adb.html) | 19c, 26ai | Yes | Yes |

Both Oracle Exadata Database Service on Dedicated Infrastructure and Oracle Autonomous AI Database on Dedicated Exadata Infrastructure use the same underlying high-performance hardware optimized for database workloads, but they each offer distinct advantages to meet different customer needs.

| Characteristic | Oracle Exadata Database Service | Oracle Autonomous AI Database on Dedicated Exadata Infrastructure | Oracle Autonomous AI Database |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Usage | Fully managed dedicated infrastructure for running Oracle databases. Suitable for mission-critical, latency-sensitive workloads requiring predictable performance where operational monitoring of the database belongs to the customer. | Fully managed database service (Infrastructure, OS, Database) for running Oracle databases. Suitable for mission-critical, latency-sensitive workloads requiring predictable performance where operational monitoring is delegated to Oracle. | Fully managed, elastic, and scalable database service. Designed for variable workloads, cloud native applications, and flexible usage, automatically scaling resources up and down as needed. Minimal admin effort needed. |
| Performance | Offers consistent and predictable high performance with low latency because of optimized hardware and software integration. | Offers consistent and predictable high performance with low latency because of optimized hardware and software integration. | Provides high performance with automatic scaling. Delivers low latency and high throughput, leveraging the elasticity of the cloud. |
| Management | Requires manual management and administration, including database configuration, tuning, and maintenance. | Fully managed with optional self-tuning features and automated admin tasks such as provisioning, scaling, patching, cloning and backups. | Self-driving and self-managing, automating tasks such as provisioning, scaling, tuning, patching, and backups. |
| Operations | Cloud automation provides the creation, maintenance updates, and basic monitoring for databases. Database administrators need to tune the database, apply maintenance updates, and configure additional database monitoring. Strong Oracle support problem resolution model. | Reduced operational overhead, as Oracle manages administrative tasks. Customers only need to focus on application development and data management. Moves to a DevOps problem resolution model. | Reduces operational overhead, as Oracle manages most administrative tasks. Customers only need to focus on application development and data management. |
| Cost | Upfront subscription cost for dedicated infrastructure and pay-per-use for CPUs allocated to VM Cluster resources. Provides predictable pricing. | Upfront subscription cost for dedicated infrastructure and pay-per-use for CPUs actively running Autonomous AI Databases. Provides lowest total-cost-of-ownership. | Uses a pay-as-you-go model, charging based on actual resource consumption. Costs scale up and down with usage, providing flexibility and potential cost savings. |
| Scaling | Provides elastic scaling for infrastructure so you can vertically and horizontally scale when you need more resources for your workloads. | Provides the same elastic scaling for infrastructure resources, but also provides auto-scaling at the database level, no need to scale at infrastructure to optimize cost. | Offers automatic and elastic scaling. Can scale up and down based on workload demands without manual intervention, providing true serverless scalability. |
| Security | Robust security features including encryption, security patches, and operator access controls for infrastructure. Customers are responsible for security configuration and management. | Mandatory security configuration by default. Database runs in Database Vault realms, customers can bring encryption keys and certificates. Operator access control for infrastructure and virtualization resources. | Built-in security with automatic data encryption, user authentication, and threat detection. Oracle manages security updates and patches. |
| Configuration | Requires initial configuration and setup, including hardware provisioning and software installation. Full control over configuration but default values suggested in provisioning. | Requires initial configuration and setup, including hardware provisioning and software installation. Full control over configuration but default values suggested in provisioning and specialized best practice defaults for OLTP / Data Warehouse workload. | Pre-configured and instantly provisioned, requiring minimal setup and configuration. |
| Patching | Infrastructure security and maintenance updates applied automatically on configured schedule. You receive Database, Grid Infrastructure, and Guest OS patches, which you apply based on your schedule. | Policy defined, hands free patching for entire service stack: Infrastructure, OS, Grid and database. Automation for one-off patches and ability to set custom governance workflows to control software change from Dev to Prod. | Automatic patching with no downtime, managed by Oracle. |
| Isolation | Complete isolation as customers has their own dedicated infrastructure. | Complete isolation as customers has their own dedicated infrastructure. | Data isolation through dedicated database instances, ensuring customer data separation. |
| Flexibility | Offers flexibility in customization within the VM for existing app scripts and dependent libraries, so easiest lift and shift to cloud. | Fully managed and monitored by Oracle, so no in VM changes. Provides most flexibility in usage and scaling, adapting to dynamic workload demands. | Fully managed and monitored by Oracle. Provides flexibility in usage and scaling, adapting to dynamic workload demands. |
| Third-party integrations | Integrates with third-party tools and applications through Oracle's extensive partner ecosystem and APIs. | Integrates with third-party tools and applications through Oracle's extensive partner ecosystem and APIs. | Integrates with third-party tools and applications through Oracle's extensive partner ecosystem and APIs. |

Use Cases
---------

*   [Oracle Exadata Database Service](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/database-at-aws/oaaws.htm#oracle-exadata-database-service)
    *   [Small Enterprises](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/database-at-aws/oaaws.htm#exadata-small-enterprises)
        
    *   [Mid-Sized Enterprises](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/database-at-aws/oaaws.htm#exadata-mid-sized-enterprises)
        
    *   [Large Enterprises](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/database-at-aws/oaaws.htm#exadata-large-enterprises)
        
*   [Oracle Autonomous AI Database](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/database-at-aws/oaaws.htm#oracle-autonomous-ai-database)
    *   [Small Enterprises](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/database-at-aws/oaaws.htm#autonomous-small-enterprises)
        
    *   [Mid-Sized Enterprises](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/database-at-aws/oaaws.htm#autonomous-mid-sized-enterprises)
        
    *   [Large Enterprises](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/database-at-aws/oaaws.htm#autonomous-large-enterprises)
        

### Oracle Exadata Database Service

Oracle Exadata Database Service offers a predictable performance, scalable, and reliable database solution for enterprises with demanding workloads and specific compliance requirements.

#### Small Enterprises

While Exadata Database Service is typically chosen by larger enterprises, small businesses with specific compliance or performance requirements can also benefit. For example, a small financial services firm dealing with sensitive data might require the dedicated infrastructure and extreme performance that Exadata offers.

_Example:_ A boutique investment firm handling sensitive financial data uses Exadata Database Service because it provides them with a dedicated, highly secure, and high performance database environment, and lets them meet strict regulatory compliance standards.

#### Mid-Sized Enterprises

Mid-sized enterprises with high-volume, mission-critical workloads can benefit from the power and scalability of Exadata Database Service. It provides them with the performance, reliability, and availability needed to support their critical applications.

_Example:_ A mid-sized online gaming company with millions of concurrent users uses Exadata Database Service because the platform's extreme performance and scalability can handle the high-volume transactional workload, ensuring a seamless and responsive user experience.

#### Large Enterprises

Large enterprises with demanding enterprise applications, data warehouses, or consolidation projects can use Exadata Database Service to handle massive data volumes, high concurrency, and complex queries with ease.

_Example:_ A large telecommunications company with terabytes of data and complex analytical queries can use Exadata Database Service because the platform's parallel processing and in-memory capabilities speed up query performance, enabling faster decision-making and improved operational efficiency.

### Oracle Autonomous AI Database

Oracle Autonomous AI Database is an excellent choice for enterprises of all sizes, offering high performance, flexibility and economical database solutions.

#### Small Enterprises

Small businesses often have dynamic and unpredictable workloads. Autonomous AI Database is ideal for this scenario. It lets small enterprises focus on their core business without the overhead of managing database infrastructure.

_Example:_ A small e-commerce startup uses Autonomous AI Database as the foundation of its online store because the database can scale automatically based on demand, ensuring the best performance during peak sales periods and lowering costs when demand is low.

#### Mid-Sized Enterprises

Mid-sized enterprises often have growing data needs that require a cost-effective and scalable solution. Autonomous AI Database lets them to pay for only what they use, making it a flexible and economical choice.

_Example:_ A mid-sized SaaS company offering customer relationship management (CRM) solutions uses Autonomous AI Database for their service. As their customer base grows, the database can scale seamlessly, ensuring they only pay for the resources consumed during that period. This helps them manage costs effectively while delivering a reliable service to their clients.

#### Large Enterprises

Large enterprises often have diverse database requirements, including handling critical and unpredictable workloads. Autonomous AI Database provides the flexibility to accommodate such needs without the complexity of managing the infrastructure.

_Example:_ A large retail chain use Autonomous AI Database for its inventory management system. During seasonal sales or promotional events, the database can automatically scale to handle increased transaction volumes, ensuring smooth operations. The autonomous capabilities also reduce the burden on the IT team, letting them to focus on strategic initiatives.

*   [Oracle AI Database@AWS](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/database-at-aws/oaaws.htm#top)
    
*   [Benefits of Using Oracle AI Database@AWS](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/database-at-aws/oaaws.htm#benefits)

*   [Use Cases](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/database-at-aws/oaaws.htm#use-cases)

Application Dependency Management
=================================

Application Dependency Management is a developer service in Oracle Cloud Infrastructure that detects security vulnerabilities in application dependencies.

[What's new](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/releasenotes/services/adm/)

#### Get Started

[Learn about Application Dependency Management](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/application-dependency-management/concepts/adm_overview.htm "Application Dependency Management (ADM) detects security vulnerabilities in application dependencies.")

[Set up Application Dependency Management](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/application-dependency-management/tasks/using-adm-with-devops.htm "This section describes the steps to set up Oracle Cloud Infrastructure for Application Dependency Management.")

[Create IAM Policies](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/application-dependency-management/concepts/policies.htm "You use the Oracle Cloud Infrastructure Identity and Access Management (IAM) service to create policies.")

#### Remediation Recipes

[List Remediation Recipes](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/application-dependency-management/tasks/list_remediation_recipes.htm "List remediation recipes.")

[Create Remediation Recipe](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/application-dependency-management/tasks/create-remediation-recipe.htm "Create a remediation recipe.")

[Update Remediation Recipe](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/application-dependency-management/tasks/editing_remediation_recipe.htm "Edit remediation recipe details.")

[Delete Remediation Recipe](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/application-dependency-management/tasks/deleting_remediation_recipe.htm "When you no longer require a remediation recipe, you can delete it.")

#### Remediation Runs

[List Remediation Runs](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/application-dependency-management/tasks/list_run.htm "View the list of remediation runs in the selected compartment. For every remediation run, you can view the last stage details, status, and associated remediation recipe.")

[Create Remediation Run](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/application-dependency-management/tasks/create_remediation_run.htm "Running a remediation recipe.")

[Delete Remediation Run](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/application-dependency-management/tasks/delete_run.htm "Delete a remediation run from your tenancy.")

[Cancel Remediation Run](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/application-dependency-management/tasks/cancel_run.htm "Cancel a remediation run which is in progress.")

#### Knowledge Bases

[List Knowledge Bases](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/application-dependency-management/tasks/KnowledgeBases/list-knowledge-base.htm "Describes how to list the knowledge bases in your tenancy.")

[Delete Knowledge Base](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/application-dependency-management/tasks/KnowledgeBases/delete-knowledge-base.htm "Delete a knowledge base using the Console, CLI, or API.")

#### Developer Tools

[API for Application Dependency Management](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/api/#/en/adm/latest/)

[CLI for Application Dependency Management](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/tools/oci-cli/latest/oci_cli_docs/cmdref/adm.html)

#### Support

[Create a service request](https://support.oracle.com/)

#### Vulnerability Audits

[List Vulnerability Audits](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/application-dependency-management/tasks/VulnerabilityAudits/list-vulnerability-audit.htm "Describes how to list the vulnerability audits in your tenancy.")

[Create Vulnerability Audit](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/application-dependency-management/tasks/VulnerabilityAudits/create-vulnerability-audit.htm "Describes how to create a vulnerability audit.")

Cloud Advisor
=============

Cloud Advisor finds potential inefficiencies in your tenancy and offers guided solutions that explain how to address them. The recommendations help you maximize cost savings and improve the security of your tenancy.

[What's new](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/releasenotes/services/cloud-advisor/)

#### Getting Started

[Cloud Advisor Overview](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/CloudAdvisor/Concepts/cloudadvisoroverview.htm "Cloud Advisor finds potential inefficiencies in the tenancy and offers guided solutions to help you address them.")

[Secure Cloud Advisor](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/Security/Reference/cloudadvisor-security.htm#cloudadvisor-security)

[Product Page](https://www.oracle.com/manageability/cloud-advisor/)

#### Community

[Oracle Cloud Infrastructure blog](https://blogs.oracle.com/cloud-infrastructure/)

[Cloud infrastructure community forum](https://community.oracle.com/tech/apps-infra/categories/18430-cloud-infrastructure)

#### Using Cloud Advisor

[Categories and Recommendations](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/CloudAdvisor/Concepts/recommendations.htm "Cloud Advisor recommendations are divided into three categories: cost management, performance, and high availability. These categories and the recommendations they include are described in this section.")

[View Categories and Recommendations](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/CloudAdvisor/Concepts/view-recommendations.htm "This section explains how to view Cloud Advisor categories and recommendations. Recommendations help you reduce costs by finding and adjusting resources that are underutilized. For example, they find underutilized compute instances, overprovisioned Autonomous AI Lakehouse and Autonomous AI Transaction Processing instances, and unattached block volumes and boot volumes.")

[Implement Recommendations](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/CloudAdvisor/Tasks/implementing_cloud_advisor_recommendations.htm "This section describes how to implement recommendations using fix-it flows in Cloud Advisor. It contains procedures to complete the following tasks:")

[Manage Work Requests](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/CloudAdvisor/Tasks/work-requests.htm "This section of Implementing Recommendations describes how to view work requests, including details, errors, and logs.")

[View Recommendation History](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/CloudAdvisor/Tasks/history.htm "This section of Implementing Recommendations describes the history table and how to view the recommendation status changes that it contains.")

[Cloud Advisor Settings](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/CloudAdvisor/Tasks/cloudadvisor-cloud_advisor_settings.htm "You can customize Cloud Advisor by postponing or dismissing recommendations and by customizing the logic that Cloud Advisor uses to make recommendations. Modify the recommendations based on your preferences and what works best for your workloads.")

[Create Cloud Advisor Queries](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/CloudAdvisor/Reference/query-syntax.htm "Review the basics of the query language for Cloud Advisor, including an explanation of syntax and rules so you can create your own queries.")

[Create Cloud Advisor Policies](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/CloudAdvisor/Reference/cloudadvisorpolicyreference.htm "This section describes the advanced details of writing policies for Cloud Advisor. Use policies to control access to Cloud Advisor.")

# API Gateway

### [Oracle Cloud Infrastructure Documentation](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/home.htm)

* * *

All Pages

[Skip to main content](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/APIGateway/home.htm#dcoc-content-body)

API Gateway
===========

The API Gateway service enables you to create governed HTTP/S interfaces for other services, including OCI Functions, Kubernetes Engine, and Container Registry. API Gateway also provides policy enforcement such as authentication and rate-limiting to HTTP/S endpoints.

[What's new](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/releasenotes/services/api-gateway/)

#### Get Started

[QuickStart](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/APIGateway/Tasks/apigatewayquickstartsetupcreatedeploy.htm "Find out how to get started quickly with API Gateway.")

[Learn about API Gateway](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/APIGateway/Concepts/apigatewayoverview.htm)

[Review key concepts](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/APIGateway/Concepts/apigatewayconcepts.htm "Find out how about key concepts you need to understand when using API Gateway.")

[Prepare for API Gateway](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/APIGateway/Concepts/apigatewayprerequisites.htm "Find out about the high-level prerequisites for using API Gateway.")

[Product page](https://www.oracle.com/cloud-native/api-management/)

#### Create APIs

[Create API gateways](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/APIGateway/Tasks/apigatewaycreatinggateway.htm "Find out how to create an API gateway with the API Gateway service to process traffic from front-end clients and route it to back-end services.")

[Create API resources and descriptions](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/APIGateway/Tasks/apigatewaycreatingapiobject.htm "Find out how to create an API resource with the API Gateway service that you can use to deploy an API on an API gateway.")

[Create API deployment specs](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/APIGateway/Tasks/apigatewaycreatingspecification.htm "Find out how to create an API specification (a machine-readable definition of an API in JSON format) for use with API Gateway.")

[Create API deployment](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/APIGateway/Tasks/apigatewaycreatingdeployment.htm "Find out how to deploy an API on an API gateway by creating an API deployment with the API Gateway service.")

#### Manage APIs

[List API gateways and deployments](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/APIGateway/Tasks/apigatewaylisting.htm "Find out how to list existing API gateways with the API Gateway service.")

[Update API gateways and deployments](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/APIGateway/Tasks/apigatewayupdating.htm "Find out how to modify API gateways that you previously created with the API Gateway service.")

[Move API gateways and deployments](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/APIGateway/Tasks/apigatewaymovingresources.htm "Find out how to move API gateways between compartments with the API Gateway service.")

[Delete API gateways and deployments](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/APIGateway/Tasks/apigatewaydeleting.htm "Find out how to delete API gateways that you previously created with the API Gateway service.")

#### Secure APIs

[Add CORS support](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/APIGateway/Tasks/apigatewayaddingcorssupport.htm "Find out how to use a request policy to add CORS support to API deployments with API Gateway.")

[Customize trust stores](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/APIGateway/Tasks/apigatewaysettingupcustomcertificateauthorities.htm "Find out how to add Certificate Authorities (CAs) and CA bundles to custom trust stores with API Gateway.")

[Add mTLS support](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/APIGateway/Tasks/apigatewayaddingmtlssupport.htm "Find out how to use a request policy to add mTLS support to API deployments with API Gateway.")

#### Authenticate and Authorize

[Add authentication and authorization](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/APIGateway/Tasks/apigatewayaddingauthzauthn.htm "Find out how to add authentication and authorization functionality to API gateways with the API Gateway service.")

[Use authorizer functions](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/APIGateway/Tasks/apigatewayusingauthorizerfunction.htm "Find out how to use single-argument authorizer functions and access tokens to add authentication and authorization functionality to API gateways with the API Gateway service.")

[Use OAuth 2.0 (and OpenID Connect), Remote JWKS, or Static keys](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/APIGateway/Tasks/apigatewayusingjwttokens.htm)

[API Gateway CLI](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/tools/oci-cli/latest/oci_cli_docs/cmdref/api-gateway.html)

[SDKs and the CLI](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/API/Concepts/sdks.htm)

[Cloud Shell](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/API/Concepts/devcloudshellintro.htm)

#### Add Back Ends

[Add HTTP/HTTPS URL back ends](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/APIGateway/Tasks/apigatewayusinghttpbackend.htm "Find out how to create an API deployment to access HTTP and HTTPS URLs with API Gateway.")

[Add Function back ends](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/APIGateway/Tasks/apigatewayusingfunctionsbackend.htm "Find out how to create an API deployment with API Gateway that exposes serverless functions defined in OCI Functions.")

[Add stock response back ends](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/APIGateway/Tasks/apigatewayaddingstockresponses.htm "Find out how to define a path to a stock response back end with API Gateway.")

[Add logout back ends](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/APIGateway/Tasks/apigatewayaddinglogoutbackends.htm "Find out how to define a logout back end with API Gateway.")

[Add login back ends](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/APIGateway/Tasks/apigatewayaddingloginbackends.htm "Find out how to define an OAuth 2.0 login back end with API Gateway.")

[Add request and response policies](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/APIGateway/Tasks/apigatewayaddingrequestpolicies.htm "Find out how to control the behavior of API deployments by adding request and response policies to API specifications that you previously created with API Gateway.")

[Add request validation](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/APIGateway/Tasks/apigatewayaddingrequestvalidation.htm "Find out how to prevent invalid requests being sent to back-end services, by validating incoming requests against a validation request policy with API Gateway.")

[Limit request numbers](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/APIGateway/Tasks/apigatewaylimitingbackendaccess.htm "Find out how to use a request policy to limit the number of requests sent to back-end services with API Gateway.")

[Cache responses](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/APIGateway/Tasks/apigatewayresponsecaching.htm "Find out how to use response caching request and response policies to reduce the number of requests sent to back-end services with API Gateway.")

[Transform requests and responses](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/APIGateway/Tasks/apigatewaymodifyingresponsesrequests.htm "Find out how to modify incoming requests and outgoing responses being sent to and from back-end services with API Gateway.")

#### Troubleshooting

[Troubleshoot API Gateway](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/APIGateway/Tasks/apigatewaytroubleshooting.htm "Find out how to troubleshoot problems with API Gateway, and possible solutions to common issues.")

# Managed Cloud Self Service Platform

### [Oracle Cloud Infrastructure Documentation](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/home.htm)

* * *

All Pages

[Skip to main content](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/managed-cloud-self-service/home.htm#dcoc-content-body)

Managed Cloud Self Service Platform
===================================

Oracle Managed Cloud Self-Service Platform provides a range of tools and services designed to manage CEMLIs (Configurations, Extensions, Modifications, Localizations, and Integrations) associated with your Oracle E-Business Suite environment.

[What's new](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/releasenotes/services/managed-cloud-self-service/)

#### Get Started

[About Oracle Managed Cloud Self-Service Platform](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/managed-cloud-self-service/mgdcloudss_about_oracle_managed_cloud_self_service_platform.htm#top "The Oracle Managed Cloud Self-Service Platform initiative enables a modernized experience by offering self-service capabilities to MCS customers at OCI. The Self-Service Platform delivers capabilities and automation used by MCS Operations directly for use by the customer. It improves service agility while delivering a modernized managed application lifecycle experience.")

[Requirements](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/managed-cloud-self-service/mgdcloudss_requirements.htm#top "Learn about the minimum requirements to use Oracle Managed Cloud Self-Service Platform.")

[Working with Instances](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/managed-cloud-self-service/mgdcloudss_working_with_instances.htm#top "Learn how to use the Instances tab to add new instances, edit existing instances, and select an instance to associate your CEMLIs with.")

[Using Packager](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/managed-cloud-self-service/mgdcloudss_using_packager.htm#top "Learn how to use the Packager option, the core tool in Oracle Automated CEMLI Execution (ACE).")

[Setting Preferences](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/managed-cloud-self-service/mgdcloudss_setting_preferences.htm#top "Learn how to set preferences.")

#### Key Features

[Using Reports](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/managed-cloud-self-service/mgdcloudss_using_reports.htm#top "Learn how to use the Reports tab to search CEMLI data and export such data to Excel.")

[Using Configurations](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/managed-cloud-self-service/mgdcloudss_using_configurations.htm#top "Learn how to use the configurations.")

[Using Bounce](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/managed-cloud-self-service/mgdcloudss_using_bounce.htm#top "Learn how the OCI users can work with self-service Bounce to shut down, start up, and bounce DB Tier and MidTiers and/or MidTier components (individual or combination of components).")

[CEMLI File Object Types](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/managed-cloud-self-service/mgdcloudss_cemli_file_object_types.htm#top "Learn about all the CEMLI file objects that are supported in Oracle Automated CEMLI Execution Release 18.1.")

[CEMLI and Data Fix File Header Format](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/managed-cloud-self-service/mgdcloudss_cemli_file_header_format.htm#top "Learn the correct format for CEMLI file headers.")

#### **Working with Oracle Managed Cloud Self-Service Platform**

[Using CEMLI](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/managed-cloud-self-service/mgdcloudss_using_cemli.htm#top "Learn about the actions that you can take on CEMLI files using Oracle Managed Cloud Self-Service Platform.")

[Building Patches](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/managed-cloud-self-service/mgdcloudss_building_patches.htm#top "You can now use the files you have uploaded to create custom patches which can be applied to your environments.")

[Scheduling Custom Patches for Deployment (Execution)](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/managed-cloud-self-service/mgdcloudss_scheduling_custom_patches_for_deployment_execution.htm#top "Learn how to schedule custom patches for deployment.")

[Using Data Fix](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/managed-cloud-self-service/mgdcloudss_using_datafix.htm#top "Learn about the actions you can take on Data fix files using Oracle Managed Cloud Self-Service Platform.")

[Scheduling Data Fixes for Deployment (Execution)](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/managed-cloud-self-service/mgdcloudss_scheduling_datafixes_for_deployment_execution.htm#top "Learn how to schedule data fixes for deployment.")

#### Licensing

[Licensing Information](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/managed-cloud-self-service/mgdcloudss_licensing_information.htm#top "Learn about the licensing information on third-party products included with Oracle Managed Cloud Self-Service Platform.")

#### Community

[Oracle Cloud Infrastructure blog](https://blogs.oracle.com/cloud-infrastructure/)

[Cloud infrastructure community forum](https://community.oracle.com/tech/apps-infra/categories/18430-cloud-infrastructure)

#### Developer Tools

[SDKs and the CLI](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/API/Concepts/sdks.htm)

[Cloud Shell](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/API/Concepts/devcloudshellintro.htm)

#### Support

[Support Requests](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/GSG/Tasks/contactingsupport.htm)

[Create a service request](https://support.oracle.com/)

# Pulse

### [Oracle Cloud Infrastructure Documentation](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/home.htm)

* * *

All Pages

[Skip to main content](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/pulse/home.htm#dcoc-content-body)

Pulse
=====

Oracle Pulse is a monitoring service for administrators to track Oracle Cloud service performance in areas of availability, storage, incident management, and change management across environments.

[What's new](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/releasenotes/services/pulse/)

#### Get Started

[Learn about Oracle Pulse](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/pulse/pulse_intro_introduction.htm#pulse_intro_introduction "Oracle Pulse enables you to truly take the 'Pulse' of your business, understanding the health of all your Oracle services.")

[Key concepts](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/pulse/pulse_key-concepts_key_concepts.htm#pulse_key_concepts_key_concepts "Review key concepts of Oracle Pulse.")

[Access Oracle Pulse](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/pulse/pulse_intro_introduction.htm#pulse_intro_accessing_oracle_pulse)

#### Community

[Oracle Cloud Infrastructure blog](https://blogs.oracle.com/cloud-infrastructure/)

[Cloud infrastructure community forum](https://community.oracle.com/tech/apps-infra/categories/18430-cloud-infrastructure)

#### Key Features

[Understand the Pulse dashboard](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/pulse/pulse_work-pulse_working_with_oracle_pulse.htm#pulse_work_pulse_working_with_oracle_pulse "Host and database metrics are tracked in Oracle Pulse using information drawn from Oracle® Enterprise Manager. Available at Customer Level, these metrics allow you to see the load on different hosts and databases at a particular point in time.")

[Display charts and environments](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/pulse/pulse_work-pulse_working_with_oracle_pulse.htm#pulse_work_pulse_my_charts_and_environments)

[Check service performance](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/pulse/pulse_check-perf_checking_the_performance_of_your_services.htm#pulse_check_perf_checking_the_performance_of_your_services "Use the Pulse Dashboard to quickly check the performance of all your Oracle production services in several widgets.")

[Export data](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/pulse/pulse_work-pulse_working_with_oracle_pulse.htm#pulse_work_pulse_exporting_data)

#### Troubleshooting

[Troubleshoot Oracle Pulse](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/pulse/pulse_trshoot_troubleshooting.htm#troubleshooting "Use troubleshooting information to identify and address common issues that can occur while working with Pulse.")

#### Oracle Pulse Reports

[Learn about reports](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/pulse/pulse_intro_features.htm#pulse_intro_what_s_new_in_this_release "Review a list of features and reports available in Oracle Pulse.")

[Storage reports](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/pulse/pulse_storage_using_the_storage_reports.htm#pulse_storage_using_the_storage_reports "Use the Storage menu in the navigation menu to see storage usage across all your organization's services and associated environments.")

[Availability reports](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/pulse/pulse_unit-reports_using_the_availability_reports.htm#pulse_unit_reports_using_the_availability_reports "Use the Availability link in the navigation menu to see availability reports across all services and environments.")

[Incident management reports](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/pulse/pulse_incidents_using_the_incident_management_reports.htm#pulse_incidents_using_the_incident_management_reports "View reports of service requests across all services and environments for all your services.")

[Change management reports](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/pulse/pulse_chng-mgt_using_the_change_management_reports.htm#pulse_chng_mgt_using_the_change_management_reports "View reports of change requests across all services and environments for all your services.")

#### Support

[Get help and contact Support](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/GSG/Tasks/contactingsupport.htm)

[Create a service request](https://support.oracle.com/)

# Artifact Registry

### [Oracle Cloud Infrastructure Documentation](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/home.htm)

* * *

All Pages

[Skip to main content](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/artifacts/home.htm#dcoc-content-body)

Artifact Registry
=================

Artifact Registry is a repository service for storing, sharing, and managing software development packages.

[What's new](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/releasenotes/services/artifacts/)

#### Get Started

[Learn about Artifact Registry](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/artifacts/overview.htm#overview "Oracle Cloud Infrastructure (OCI) Artifact Registry is a repository service for storing, sharing, and managing software development packages.")

[Review key concepts](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/artifacts/concepts.htm#concepts "Review concepts and terms related to Artifact Registry.")

[Access Artifact Registry](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/artifacts/overview.htm#ways-to-access "Use any of the following options to access Artifact Registry.")

#### Prerequisites

[Create IAM policies](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/artifacts/iam-policies.htm#policies "Create Identity and Access Management (IAM) policies to control who has access to Artifact Registry resources and to control the type of access for each group of users.")

#### Community

[Oracle Cloud Infrastructure blog](https://blogs.oracle.com/cloud-infrastructure/)

[Oracle communities](https://community.oracle.com/tech/apps-infra/categories/18430-cloud-infrastructure)

#### Manage Repositories

[Create repositories](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/artifacts/create-repo.htm#create-repo "Create a repository in Artifact Registry to store non-container generic artifacts or blobs.")

[Delete repositories](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/artifacts/delete-repo.htm#delete-repo "Delete an empty repository in Artifact Registry.")

[Get repository events](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/artifacts/events.htm#repository-events "When you create, update, list, delete or change compartment of a repository, Artifact Registry creates an event.")

#### Developer Tools

[Artifacts and Container Images API](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/api/#/en/registry/)

[Generic Artifacts Content API](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/api/#/en/generic/)

[Artifacts CLI](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/tools/oci-cli/latest/oci_cli_docs/cmdref/artifacts.html)

[Cloud Shell](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/API/Concepts/devcloudshellintro.htm)

#### Manage Artifacts

[Upload artifacts](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/artifacts/upload-artifact.htm#upload-artifact "The method that you use to upload an artifact to Artifact Registry depends on the size of the artifact file.")

[Download artifacts](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/artifacts/download-artifact.htm#download-artifact "Download an artifact to a local environment.")

[Delete artifacts](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/artifacts/delete-artifact.htm#delete-artifact "Delete artifacts from an Artifact Registry repository.")

[Search for artifacts](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/artifacts/search-artifacts.htm#search-artifacts "Search for artifacts in Artifact Registry by entering the first few characters of the artifact name or version in the search field.")

[Get artifact events](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/artifacts/events.htm#artifact-events "When you upload, update, delete, or get details of an artifact, or when you list the artifacts of a repository, Artifact Registry creates an event.")

#### Support

[Get help and contact support](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/GSG/Tasks/contactingsupport.htm)

[Create a service request](https://support.oracle.com/epmos/faces/MosIndex.jspx)
