# Logging

### [Oracle Cloud Infrastructure Documentation](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/home.htm)

* * *

All Pages

[Skip to main content](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Logging/home.htm#dcoc-content-body)

Logging
=======

The Logging service provides a highly scalable and fully managed single interface for all the logs in your tenancy. Use Logging to access logs from all your Oracle Cloud Infrastructure resources, and enable, manage, and search them.

[What's new](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/releasenotes/services/logging/)

#### Get Started

[Logging overview video](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=JrFAHLbajXg)

[How Logging works](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Logging/Concepts/loggingoverview.htm#how_logging_works)

[Logging concepts](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Logging/Concepts/loggingoverview.htm#logging_concepts)

[Required permissions](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Logging/Task/managinglogs.htm#required_permissions_logs_groups)

[Product page](https://www.oracle.com/devops/logging/)

#### Search Logs

[Learn about log search](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Logging/Concepts/searchinglogs.htm "Use the Logging Search page to search logs.")

[Basic searches](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Logging/Concepts/searchinglogs.htm#basic_search_queries)

[Advanced searches](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Logging/Concepts/searchinglogs.htm#advanced_search_queries)

[Save a search](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Logging/Task/log-saved-search-management.htm "Learn about how to create and manage log saved searches.")

[Logging query language](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Logging/Reference/query_language_specification.htm "Use query syntax components with Advanced mode custom searches on the Logging Search page.")

#### Developer Tools: CLI

[SDKs and the CLI](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/API/Concepts/sdks.htm)

[Cloud Shell](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/API/Concepts/devcloudshellintro.htm)

[CLI for Logging Ingestion](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/tools/oci-cli/latest/oci_cli_docs/cmdref/logging-ingestion.html)

[CLI for Logging Management](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/tools/oci-cli/latest/oci_cli_docs/cmdref/logging.html)

[CLI for Logging Search](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/tools/oci-cli/latest/oci_cli_docs/cmdref/logging-search.html)

#### Work with Custom Logs

[Learn about custom logs](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Logging/Concepts/custom_logs.htm "Custom logs are logs that contain diagnostic information from custom applications, other cloud providers, or an on-premise environment.")

[Create a custom log](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Logging/Task/create-logging-log.htm#console)

[Learn about agent management](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Logging/Concepts/agent_management.htm "To ingest events from your applications into your custom log, you can install the Oracle fluentd-based agent. This agent allows you to control exactly which logs you want to collect, how to parse them, and more.")

[Install the agent](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Logging/Task/installing_the_agent.htm "Learn about how to install the Unified Monitoring Agent, whether for new instances, existing instances, or instances created from custom images, and non-Oracle Cloud Infrastructure instances.")

[Manage agent configurations](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Logging/Task/agent-configuration-management.htm "Learn about how to create and manage agent configurations.")

#### Work with Service and Audit Logs

[Learn about service logs](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Logging/Concepts/service_logs.htm "Oracle Cloud Infrastructure services emit service logs. Each of these supported services has a Logs resource you can use to enable or disable logging for that service.")

[Enable logging for a resource](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Logging/Task/enabling_logging.htm "Service logs can be enabled directly on the resource itself, on the Logs page, or on a log group details page.")

[Service logs reference](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Logging/Reference/service_log_reference.htm "Logging details for each service supported by Oracle Cloud Infrastructure Logging.")

[Learn about audit logs](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Logging/Concepts/audit_logs.htm "On the Audit page, you can explore audit logs. Audit logs are also searchable on the Search page, and you can view Audit logs in every compartment by selecting the /_Audit log group on the Search page.")

#### Developer Tools: APIs

[API for Logging Ingestion](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/api/#/en/logging-dataplane/)

[API for Logging Management](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/api/#/en/logging-management/)

[API for Logging Search](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/api/#/en/logging-search/)

#### Support

[Support Requests](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/GSG/Tasks/contactingsupport.htm)

[Create a service request](https://support.oracle.com/)

#### Manage Logs and Log Groups

[Learn about logs and log groups](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Logging/Task/managinglogs.htm "Use Oracle Cloud Infrastructure Logging to manage logs and log groups.")

[Move log groups to a different compartment](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Logging/Task/change-compartment-logging-log-group.htm "Move a log group to a different compartment.")

[Work with custom logs and service logs](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Logging/Task/log-group-management.htm "Learn about how to create and manage log groups.")

#### Troubleshooting

[Troubleshooting Logging](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Logging/troubleshooting.htm "Use troubleshooting information to identify and address common issues that can occur while working with Logging.")

#### Community

[Oracle Cloud Infrastructure blog](https://blogs.oracle.com/cloud-infrastructure/)

[Cloud infrastructure community forum](https://forums.oracle.com/ords/apexds/domain/dev-community/category/15686-platform?tags=cloud-applications,cloud-infrastructure)

# Monitoring

### [Oracle Cloud Infrastructure Documentation](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/home.htm)

* * *

All Pages

[Skip to main content](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Monitoring/home.htm#dcoc-content-body)

Monitoring
==========

Use Monitoring to query metrics and manage alarms. Metrics and alarms help monitor the health, capacity, and performance of your cloud resources.

[What's new](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/releasenotes/services/monitoring/)

#### Get Started

[Video: Overview](https://apexapps.oracle.com/pls/apex/f?p=44785:265:0:::265:P265_CONTENT_ID:31981)

[View service dashboards](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Monitoring/Tasks/view-chart-namespace.htm "Go to the Service Metrics page in the Console to view metric charts that use predefined service queries for a selected metric namespace. The charts show metric data for all resources in the selected metric namespace, compartment, and region.")

[Services that emit metrics](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Monitoring/Concepts/monitoringoverview.htm#SupportedServices)

[Secure the Monitoring service](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/Security/Reference/monitoring_security.htm)

[Product page](https://www.oracle.com/devops/monitoring/)

#### Manage Alarms

[View firing alarms](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Monitoring/Tasks/list-alarm-status.htm "List status levels for alarms in Monitoring.")

[Create a basic alarm](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Monitoring/Tasks/create-alarm-basic.htm "Create a basic alarm in Monitoring to notify you when metrics meet specified triggers.")

[Split messages by metric stream](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Monitoring/Tasks/split-messages.htm "Walk through setting up an alarm to send a message for each metric stream. In this example, you want to be notified whenever a server exceeds a threshold. With this setup, you receive server-specific messages.")

[List metric stream status](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Monitoring/Tasks/list-alarm-status-metric-stream.htm "List the status of each metric stream in an alarm in Monitoring. A metric stream corresponds to a set of dimension key-value pairs.")

[Suppress an alarm](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Monitoring/Tasks/create-alarm-suppression.htm "Temporarily stop notifications from an alarm by applying a suppression. For example, use a suppression to suspend alarm notifications during system maintenance.")

#### Query Metrics

[View default metric charts](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Monitoring/Tasks/view-chart-namespace.htm "Go to the Service Metrics page in the Console to view metric charts that use predefined service queries for a selected metric namespace. The charts show metric data for all resources in the selected metric namespace, compartment, and region.")

[Create a basic query](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Monitoring/Tasks/metrics-explorer-basic-query.htm "Select the minimum required query configuration on the Metrics Explorer page in the Console to view a custom metric chart.")

[Select dimensions for a query](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Monitoring/Tasks/query-metric-dimension.htm "Limit returned metric data by selecting dimensions when querying metric data in Monitoring. A dimension is a qualifier provided in a metric definition. In MQL, the dimension selection component specifies name-value pairs for dimensions, surrounded by curly brackets.")

[Aggregate metric streams](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Monitoring/Tasks/query-metric-grouping.htm "Aggregating metric streams returns the combined value of all metric streams for the selected statistic. For example, aggregate all metric streams for CPU Utilization to return the combined value across all resources.")

[Edit the MQL expression](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Monitoring/Tasks/query-metric-mql.htm "Directly edit the MQL expression used to query metric data in Monitoring.")

#### Developer Tools

[API for Monitoring](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/api/#/en/monitoring/latest/)

[CLI for Monitoring](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/tools/oci-cli/latest/oci_cli_docs/cmdref/monitoring/alarm/change-compartment.html)

[SDKs and the CLI](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/API/Concepts/sdks.htm)

[Cloud Shell](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/API/Concepts/devcloudshellintro.htm)

#### Community

[Oracle Cloud Infrastructure blog](https://blogs.oracle.com/cloud-infrastructure/)

[Cloud infrastructure community forum](https://forums.oracle.com/ords/apexds/domain/dev-community/category/15686-platform?tags=cloud-applications,cloud-infrastructure)

[Monitoring learning videos](https://www.youtube.com/playlist?list=PLKCk3OyNwIzvhV2er2b2W0MgdhO8uvIrW)

#### Publish Custom Metrics

[Steps to publish custom metrics](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Monitoring/Tasks/publishingcustommetrics.htm "Publish custom metrics to the Monitoring service.")

[Walk through example custom metrics](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Monitoring/Tasks/custom-metrics-walkthrough.htm "Walk through common use cases with custom metrics: publishing, querying, creating an alarm, and triggering the alarm to observe its Firing status.")

#### Troubleshooting

[Investigate missing resources or metrics](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Monitoring/troubleshooting-queries.htm#missing-resources "Troubleshoot missing resources or metrics when querying metric data.")

[Troubleshoot query limits](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Monitoring/Concepts/monitoringoverview.htm#TroubleshootingLimits)

[Confirm validity of alarm intervals](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Monitoring/Concepts/alarmsbestpractices.htm#interval)

4.  [Big Data Preparation Cloud Service](https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/paas/big-data-prep-cloud/index.html)
    

Oracle Big Data Preparation Cloud Service
=========================================

Get Started
-----------

Oracle Big Data Preparation Cloud Service is a managed Platform as a Service (PaaS) cloud-based offering that enables you to rapidly ingest, repair, enrich, and publish large data sets with end-to-end visibility in an interactive environment. You can integrate your data with other Oracle Cloud Services, such as Oracle Business Intelligence Cloud Service, for down-stream analysis.

*   [Get Acquainted with Product Features](https://www.oracle.com/pls/topic/lookup?ctx=en/cloud/paas/big-data-prep-cloud&id=BDPUG-GUID-197CFCD6-9582-4283-B188-E9CF36C85074)

# Kubernetes Engine (OKE)

### [Oracle Cloud Infrastructure Documentation](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/home.htm)

* * *

All Pages

[Skip to main content](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/ContEng/home.htm#dcoc-content-body)

Kubernetes Engine (OKE)
=======================

Kubernetes Engine (OKE) helps you define and create Kubernetes clusters to enable the deployment, scaling, and management of containerized applications.

[What's new](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/releasenotes/services/conteng/)

#### Get Started

[Learn about OKE](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/ContEng/Concepts/contengoverview.htm "Find out about Kubernetes Engine (OKE), which enables you to deploy, manage, and scale containerized applications on Oracle Cloud Infrastructure with flexible node choices, automation, and integrated security.")

[Review key concepts](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/ContEng/Concepts/contengclustersnodes.htm "Find out about the key concepts you need to understand before using Kubernetes Engine (OKE).")

[Prepare for OKE](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/ContEng/Concepts/contengprerequisites.htm "Find out about the prerequisites you have to meet before you can use Kubernetes Engine (OKE).")

[Product page](https://www.oracle.com/cloud/cloud-native/container-engine-kubernetes/)

#### Create Clusters

[Quick Create workflow](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/ContEng/Tasks/contengcreatingclusterusingoke_topic-Using_the_Console_to_create_a_Quick_Cluster_with_Default_Settings.htm "Find out how to use the 'Quick Create' workflow to create a Kubernetes cluster with default settings and new network resources using Kubernetes Engine (OKE).")

[Custom Create workflow](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/ContEng/Tasks/contengcreatingclusterusingoke_topic-Using_the_Console_to_create_a_Custom_Cluster_with_Explicitly_Defined_Settings.htm "Find out how to use the 'Custom Create' workflow to create a Kubernetes cluster with explicitly defined settings and existing network resources using Kubernetes Engine (OKE).")

[Enhanced clusters](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/ContEng/Tasks/contengworkingwithenhancedclusters.htm "Find out about enhanced clusters and basic clusters, the differences between them, and how to create them using Kubernetes Engine (OKE).")

[Managed nodes](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/ContEng/Tasks/contengworkingwithmanagednodes.htm "Find out about managed nodes, the differences between managed nodes and virtual nodes, and how to create managed nodes using Kubernetes Engine (OKE).")

[Virtual nodes](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/ContEng/Tasks/contengworkingwithvirtualnodes.htm "Find out about virtual nodes, the differences between virtual nodes and managed nodes, and how to create virtual nodes using Kubernetes Engine (OKE).")

[Self-managed nodes](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/ContEng/Tasks/contengworkingwithselfmanagednodes.htm "Find out how to set up and use self-managed nodes with Kubernetes Engine.")

#### Access Clusters

[Set up access](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/ContEng/Tasks/contengdownloadkubeconfigfile.htm "Find out about the steps to set up access to the clusters you create using Kubernetes Engine (OKE). Having completed the steps, you can start using kubectl to manage the cluster.")

[Add auth token to kubeconfig](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/ContEng/Tasks/contengaddingserviceaccttoken.htm "Find out how to add a service account authentication token to the kubeconfig file of a Kubernetes cluster you've created using Kubernetes Engine (OKE).")

[IAM and Kubernetes](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/ContEng/Concepts/contengaboutaccesscontrol.htm "Find out about the permissions required to access clusters you've created using Kubernetes Engine (OKE).")

[SSH to worker nodes](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/ContEng/Tasks/contengconnectingworkernodesusingssh.htm "Find out how to use SSH to connect to worker nodes in node pools in clusters you've created using Kubernetes Engine (OKE).")

#### Support

[Support Requests](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/GSG/Tasks/contactingsupport.htm)

[Create a service request](https://support.oracle.com/)

#### Manage Clusters

[Edit clusters](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/ContEng/Tasks/contengmodifyingcluster.htm "Find out how to modify properties of existing Kubernetes clusters you've created using Kubernetes Engine (OKE).")

[Edit nodes and node pools](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/ContEng/Tasks/contengmodifyingnodepool.htm "Find out how to modify properties of existing node pools and worker nodes you've created using Kubernetes Engine (OKE).")

[Configure cluster add-ons](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/ContEng/Tasks/contengconfiguringclusteraddons.htm "Find out about configuring cluster add-ons in clusters you create using Kubernetes Engine (OKE).")

[Encrypt Kubernetes secrets](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/ContEng/Tasks/contengencryptingdata.htm "Find out how to encrypt configuration data stored as Kubernetes secrets in etcd when using Kubernetes Engine (OKE).")

[Configure DNS servers](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/ContEng/Tasks/contengconfiguringdnsserver.htm "Find out how to configure DNS servers for Kubernetes clusters you've created using Kubernetes Engine (OKE).")

#### Manage Deployments

[Deploy sample Nginx app](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/ContEng/Tasks/contengdeployingsamplenginx.htm "Find out how to use kubectl to deploy an Nginx app on a cluster you've created using Kubernetes Engine (OKE).")

[Pull images from Registry](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/ContEng/Tasks/contengpullingimagesfromocir.htm "Find out how to create a Docker registry secret, and how to specify the image to pull from Oracle Cloud Infrastructure Registry (along with the Docker secret to use) during deployment of an application to a cluster you've created using Kubernetes Engine (OKE).")

[Create load balancers](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/ContEng/Tasks/contengcreatingloadbalancer.htm "Find out how to create different types of load balancer to distribute traffic between the nodes of a cluster you've created using Kubernetes Engine (OKE).")

[Set up storage](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/ContEng/Tasks/contengcreatingpersistentvolumeclaim.htm "Find out how to define and apply persistent volume claims to clusters you've created using Kubernetes Engine (OKE). With Oracle Cloud Infrastructure as the underlying IaaS provider, you can provision persistent volume claims by attaching volumes from the Block Volume service, or by mounting file systems from the File Storage service or the File Storage with Lustre service.")

[Set up disaster recovery](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/ContEng/Tasks/contengfullstackdisasterrecovery.htm "Find out more about setting up Full Stack Disaster Recovery for clusters created with Kubernetes Engine (OKE).")

#### Observe Clusters

[Monitor clusters](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/ContEng/Tasks/contengmonitoringclusters.htm "Find out how to monitor the clusters, node pools, and nodes you've created using Kubernetes Engine (OKE).")

[View work requests](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/ContEng/Tasks/contengviewingworkrequests.htm "Find out how to view the operations of Kubernetes Engine (OKE) as work requests.")

[View audit logs](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/ContEng/Tasks/contengmonitoringoke.htm "Find out how to view operations of both Kubernetes Engine (OKE) and the Kubernetes API server as log events in the Oracle Cloud Infrastructure Audit.")

[View application logs](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/ContEng/Tasks/contengviewingworkernodelogs.htm "Find out how to view the logs of applications running on managed nodes and self-managed nodes in a Kubernetes cluster you've created using Kubernetes Engine (OKE).")

[Metrics](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/ContEng/Reference/contengmetrics.htm "Find out about the metrics emitted by Kubernetes Engine (OKE).")

#### Developer Tools

[OKE API](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/api/#/en/containerengine/)

[OKE CLI](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/tools/oci-cli/latest/oci_cli_docs/cmdref/ce.html)

[SDKs and the CLI](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/API/Concepts/sdks.htm)

[Cloud Shell](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/API/Concepts/devcloudshellintro.htm)

[Cluster API Provider for OCI](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/API/SDKDocs/cluster-api-provider.htm)

[Crossplane Provider for OCI](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/API/SDKDocs/crossplane-provider.htm)

[OCI Service Operator for Kubernetes](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/API/SDKDocs/operator-kubernetes.htm)

#### Scale Clusters

[Add and remove node pools](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/ContEng/Tasks/contengscalingclusters.htm "Find out how to add and remove node pools to scale up and scale down the Kubernetes clusters you've created using Kubernetes Engine (OKE).")

[Scale node pools](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/ContEng/Tasks/contengscalingnodepools.htm "Find out how to scale up and scale down the node pools you've created using Kubernetes Engine (OKE).")

[Autoscale node pools and pods](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/ContEng/Tasks/contengautoscalingclusters.htm "Find out about autoscaling Kubernetes node pools and pods you've created using Kubernetes Engine (OKE).")

#### Admission Controllers

[Supported admission controllers](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/ContEng/Reference/contengadmissioncontrollers.htm "Find out about the admission controllers that are turned on in Kubernetes clusters you create using Kubernetes Engine (OKE).")

[Pod security policies](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/ContEng/Tasks/contengusingpspswithoke.htm "Find out how to use pod security policies with Kubernetes clusters you've created using Kubernetes Engine (OKE).")

#### Upgrade Clusters

[Upgrade to new Kubernetes versions](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/ContEng/Concepts/contengaboutupgradingclusters.htm "Find out about the different ways to upgrade control plane nodes and worker nodes to newer Kubernetes versions using Kubernetes Engine (OKE).")

[Supported Kubernetes versions](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/ContEng/Concepts/contengaboutk8sversions.htm "Find out about the Kubernetes versions that Kubernetes Engine (OKE) currently supports, along with details of previously supported versions and planned support for future versions.")

[Kubernetes support policy](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/ContEng/Concepts/contengupgradeoverview.htm "Find out about the Kubernetes versioning scheme and Kubernetes Engine (OKE) support for different Kubernetes versions.")

#### Troubleshooting and FAQs

[Troubleshooting](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/ContEng/Tasks/contengtroubleshooting.htm "Find out about troubleshooting information for Kubernetes Engine (OKE).")

[Frequently Asked Questions](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/ContEng/Tasks/contengfaqs.htm "Find out answers to frequently asked questions about Kubernetes Engine (OKE).")

#### Community

[Oracle Cloud Infrastructure blog](https://blogs.oracle.com/cloud-infrastructure/)

[Cloud infrastructure community forum](https://forums.oracle.com/ords/apexds/domain/dev-community/category/15686-platform?tags=cloud-applications,cloud-infrastructure)

[AI solutions hub](https://www.oracle.com/artificial-intelligence/solutions/)

Oracle Data Integrator 12.2.1.4.0
=================================

Get Started
-----------

Use Oracle Data Integrator on Oracle Fusion Middleware 12c (12.2.1.4). Oracle Data Integrator is a comprehensive data integration platform that covers all data integration requirements: from high-volume, high-performance batch loads, to event-driven, trickle-feed integration processes, to SOA-enabled data services.

### Release Notes

*   [Release notes for Oracle Data Integrator](https://www.oracle.com/pls/topic/lookup?ctx=en/middleware/fusion-middleware/data-integrator/12.2.1.4&id=ODIRN)
    
*   [All Fusion Middleware release notes](https://www.oracle.com/pls/topic/lookup?ctx=en/middleware/fusion-middleware/data-integrator/12.2.1.4&id=relnotehome)
    

### Learn About Oracle Data Integrator

*   [What's new in Oracle Data Integrator?](https://www.oracle.com/pls/topic/lookup?ctx=en/middleware/fusion-middleware/data-integrator/12.2.1.4&id=ODIAD1924)
    
*   [Understanding Oracle Data Integrator Concepts](https://www.oracle.com/pls/topic/lookup?ctx=en/middleware/fusion-middleware/data-integrator/12.2.1.4&id=ODIUN165)
    
*   [Setting Up an Integration Project](https://www.oracle.com/pls/topic/lookup?ctx=en/middleware/fusion-middleware/data-integrator/12.2.1.4&id=ODIDG150)
    
*   [Integrating with Different Technologies](https://www.oracle.com/pls/topic/lookup?ctx=en/middleware/fusion-middleware/data-integrator/12.2.1.4&id=ODIKM)
    
*   [Integrating with Big Data](https://www.oracle.com/pls/topic/lookup?ctx=en/middleware/fusion-middleware/data-integrator/12.2.1.4&id=ODIBD)
    
*   [Working with Application Adapters](https://www.oracle.com/pls/topic/lookup?ctx=en/middleware/fusion-middleware/data-integrator/12.2.1.4&id=ODIAA)
    

### Install, Configure, and Upgrade

*   [Install and configure Oracle Data Integrator](https://www.oracle.com/pls/topic/lookup?ctx=en/middleware/fusion-middleware/data-integrator/12.2.1.4&id=ODING)
    
*   [Upgrade Oracle Data Integrator](https://www.oracle.com/pls/topic/lookup?ctx=en/middleware/fusion-middleware/data-integrator/12.2.1.4&id=ODIUG)
    

### Use Oracle Data Integrator on Oracle Cloud Marketplace

*   [Getting Started with Oracle Data Integrator on Oracle Cloud Marketplace](https://www.oracle.com/pls/topic/lookup?ctx=en/middleware/fusion-middleware/data-integrator/12.2.1.4&id=DIMGS-GUID-1793F6A6-8581-465D-BBE3-8F0C8ADD6536)
    
*   [Migrating from Oracle Data Integrator Cloud Services to Oracle Data Integrator Marketplace](https://www.oracle.com/pls/topic/lookup?ctx=en/middleware/fusion-middleware/data-integrator/12.2.1.4&id=DIMGS-GUID-255219D4-9E6A-4AF7-8BC9-BF56BC8270D4)

# Data Science

### [Oracle Cloud Infrastructure Documentation](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/home.htm)

* * *

All Pages

[Skip to main content](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/data-science/using/home.htm#dcoc-content-body)

Data Science
============

Data Science is a fully managed and serverless platform for data science teams to build, train, and manage machine learning models in Oracle Cloud Infrastructure.

[What's new](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/releasenotes/services/data-science/)

#### Get Started

[Overview of the Data Science service](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/data-science/using/overview.htm "Oracle Cloud Infrastructure (OCI) Data Science is a fully managed and serverless platform for data science teams to build, train, and manage machine learning models.")

[Data Scientists guide to OCI](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/GSG/Reference/getting-started-as-data-scientist.htm)

[What's new in ADS](https://accelerated-data-science.readthedocs.io/en/latest/release_notes.html)

[Provisioning and pricing](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/data-science/using/overview.htm#provisioning-pricing)

[Configure a tenancy with one-click Resource Manager](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/data-science/using/orm-configure-tenancy.htm "The Data Science service tenancy can be automatically configured, including OCI resources, using the OCI Resource Manager.")

[Create projects](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/data-science/using/create-projects.htm "Create a Data Science project to organize Data Science resources.")

[Using GPUs](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/data-science/using/gpu-using.htm "To work with GPUs in Data Science, the tenancy needs a limit for that shape and the shape must be available in the region you want to use it in.")

[Product page](https://www.oracle.com/data-science/data-science-service/)

#### Build Models in Notebooks

[Authentication](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/data-science/using/use-notebook-sessions.htm#signing-oci-apis)

[Use code files](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/data-science/using/use-notebook-sessions.htm#code-files)

[Configure a private endpoint](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/data-science/using/pe-network.htm "Configure private access for notebook sessions and model deployments in Data Science using private endpoints.")

[Configure runtime options](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/data-science/using/notebook-runtime.htm "When you create a notebook session in Data Science, you can add custom environment variable keys and Git repository URLs to be used in notebook sessions.")

[Create notebooks](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/data-science/using/create-notebook-sessions.htm "Create a Data Science notebook session to access a JupyterLab interface that uses a customizable compute, storage, and network configuration.")

[View notebook metrics](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/data-science/using/metrics-view-notebook.htm "View the default metric charts using a notebook session's detail page in the Data Science service in the Console.")

[Connect to data](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/data-science/using/connect-to-data.htm "You can connect to data in several different ways.")

[Connect to Data Flow](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/data-science/using/connect-to-data-flow-studio.htm "You can connect to Data Flow and run an Apache Spark application from a Data Science notebook session. These sessions let you run interactive Spark workloads on a long lasting Data Flow cluster through an Apache Livy integration.")

#### Use AI Operators

[Overview](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/data-science/using/operators.htm "Operators are a suite of prepackaged solutions designed to address a wide range of data science needs")

[Quick Start](https://accelerated-data-science.readthedocs.io/en/latest/user_guide/quick_start/quick_start.html)

[AI Forecasting](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/data-science/using/operators-ai-forecast.htm "The AI Forecast Operator uses historical time series data to generate forecasts for future trends.")

[Anomaly Detection](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/data-science/using/operators-anomaly-detection.htm "The Anomaly Detection Operator is a low code tool for integrating anomaly detection into any enterprise application.")

#### ML Applications

[Overview](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/data-science/using/ml-apps-about.htm "ML Applications is a self-contained representation of ML use cases in Data Science.")

[Policies](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/data-science/using/ml-apps-policy-setup.htm "Setting up ML Applications policies.")

[Project Setup](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/data-science/using/ml-apps-project-setup.htm "After creating the infrastructure for the sample ML Application, you can use the sample ML Application project as a template to start building, deploying, and operating your own ML Applications.")

#### Support

[Increase Data Science service limits](https://youtu.be/I_oeFppwybg)

[Support Requests](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/GSG/Tasks/contactingsupport.htm)

[Create a service request](https://support.oracle.com/)

#### Create and Save Models

[Construct a model artifact](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/data-science/using/models-prepare-artifact.htm "After a model is trained, you must create a model artifact to save with the model in a model catalog.")

[Create and save models](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/data-science/using/models_saving_catalog.htm "Create a model and save it to the model catalog by using the ADS SDK, OCI Python SDK, or the Console.")

[Use a large model artifact](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/data-science/using/model-large-artifacts.htm "The maximum size of a model artifact is 6 GB.")

#### Use Jobs

[Use your own container](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/data-science/using/jobs-byoc.htm "You can build and use a custom container (Bring Your Own Container or BYOC) for use when you create a job and job runs.")

[Build a job artifact](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/data-science/using/jobs-artifact.htm "Learn how create a job artifact to use in your jobs and job runs.")

[Create a job](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/data-science/using/jobs-create.htm "Create and run a job in Data Science.")

[Start a job run](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/data-science/using/job-runs.htm "Use Data Science job runs to apply various use cases to created jobs.")

[View job run metrics](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/data-science/using/metrics-jobs.htm "Use job metrics to improve performance.")

#### Use Pipelines

[Create a pipeline](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/data-science/using/pipelines-create.htm "Create a Data Science pipeline to run a task. You can create pipelines by using the ADS SDK, OCI Console, or the OCI SDK. Using ADS for creating pipelines can make developing the pipeline, the steps, and the dependencies easier. ADS supports reading and writing the pipeline to and from a YAML file. You can use ADS to view a visual representation of the pipeline. We recommend that you use ADS to create and manage pipeline using code.")

[Start a pipeline run](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/data-science/using/pipelines-runs-start.htm "Use a Data Science pipeline run to run the code of the pipeline steps according to the workflow defined in a pipeline.")

[Delete a pipeline run](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/data-science/using/pipelines-delete.htm "Delete a pipeline that's in a Succeeded, Failed, or Canceled state.")

[Bring Your Own Container](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/data-science/using/pipelines-v2-byoc-about.htm "Apart from defining pipeline steps based on jobs and scripts, you can use custom container images to define the step runtime.")

#### Developer Tools

[ADS SDK](https://accelerated-data-science.readthedocs.io/en/latest/index.html)

[ADS classes](https://accelerated-data-science.readthedocs.io/en/latest/modules.html)

[OCIFS SDK](https://ocifs.readthedocs.io/en/latest/)

[AutoMLx](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/tools/automlx/latest/latest/index.html)

[ML Insights](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/tools/ml-insights-docs/latest/ml-insights-documentation/html/index.html)

[ML Monitoring](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/data-science/using/ml-monitoring-about.htm "Learn about ML Monitoring in Data Science.")

[Feature Store](https://feature-store-accelerated-data-science.readthedocs.io/en/latest/overview.html)

[Data Science API](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/api/#/en/data-science/)

[Data Science CLI](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/tools/oci-cli/3.37.14/oci_cli_docs/cmdref/data-science.html)

#### Community

[AI and Data Science blog](https://blogs.oracle.com/datascience/)

[Data Science newsletter subscription](https://go.oracle.com/LP=84731?elqCampaignId=109966&bn=odsn)

[Data Science learning videos](https://www.youtube.com/playlist?list=PLKCk3OyNwIzv6CWMhvqSB_8MLJIZdO80L)

[Become an OCI Data Science Professional](https://mylearn.oracle.com/ou/learning-path/become-an-oci-data-science-professional-2023/121944)

#### Discover Model Deployments

[Set up IAM policies](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/data-science/using/model-dep-policies-auth.htm "You must create a group of users that are authorized to work with model deployments before you can deploy a model with a model deployment resource.")

[Use your own container](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/data-science/using/mod-dep-byoc.htm "Build and use a custom container (Bring Your Own Container or BYOC) as the runtime dependency when you create a model deployment.")

[Create model deployments](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/data-science/using/model_dep_create.htm "After you store a Data Science model in the model catalog, it can be deployed as an HTTP endpoint as a model deployment.")

[Set up predict endpoints](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/data-science/using/model-dep-invoke.htm "Invoking a model deployment means that you can pass feature vectors or data samples to the inference endpoint, and then the model returns predictions for those data samples.")

[View model deployment metrics](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/data-science/using/metrics-model-deploy.htm#metrics-view-ds)

[Functions samples repository and Cloud Shell quick start](https://blogs.oracle.com/cloud-infrastructure/oracle-functions-announcing-samples-repository-and-quick-start-with-cloud-shell)

#### Explore Conda Environments

[Learn about condas](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/data-science/using/conda_understand_environments.htm "We recommend that you use conda environment to package Python dependencies inside notebook sessions.")

[Install condas](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/data-science/using/conda_installing.htm "To use conda environments in notebook sessions, you must install them.")

[Publish a conda to a bucket](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/data-science/using/conda_publishs_object.htm "Publish conda environments to Object Storage for use notebook sessions.")

[Select a kernel](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/data-science/using/conda_kernel.htm "Console kernels are selected in JupyterLab.")

[Copy a conda to another region](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/data-science/using/tutorial/move-conda-env/summary.htm)

[Conda notebook examples](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/data-science/using/conda_examples.htm "The notebook session environment includes various interactive example notebooks. You can use them to learn how to use the Data Science service. These examples are designed to help you understand its various features and solve business problems.")

#### Use AI Quick Actions

[Prerequisites](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/data-science/using/ai-quick-actions-set-up.htm "The prerequisites required to set yourself up for using AI Quick Actions.")

[About AI Quick Actions](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/data-science/using/ai-quick-actions-about.htm "AI Quick Actions makes it easy for you to browse foundation models, and deploy, fine-tune, and evaluate them inside Data Science notebooks.")

[Models](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/data-science/using/ai-quick-actions-models.htm "Learn about Model Explorer in AI Quick Actions.")

[Model Deployment](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/data-science/using/ai-quick-actions-model-deploy.htm "Follow these steps to deploy models with AI Quick Actions.")

[Fine-Tuning](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/data-science/using/ai-quick-actions-fine-tuning.htm "Fine-tuning is the process of taking a pretrained model and further training it on a domain-specific dataset to improve its knowledge and provide better responses in that domain.")

[Evaluations](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/data-science/using/ai-quick-actions-evaluations.htm "Evaluating the model performance with AI Quick Actions")

# Functions

Functions
=========

Functions is a serverless platform that enables you to create, run, and scale business logic without managing any infrastructure.

[What's new](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/releasenotes/services/functions/)

#### Get Started

[QuickStart on Cloud Shell](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Functions/Tasks/functionsquickstartcloudshell.htm "Find out how to get set up and running quickly on Cloud Shell using this OCI Functions QuickStart.")

[QuickStart on local host](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Functions/Tasks/functionsquickstartlocalhost.htm "Find out how to get set up and running quickly on a local host using this OCI Functions QuickStart.")

[Learn about Functions](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Functions/Concepts/functionsoverview.htm "Learn how the Functions service lets you create, run, and scale business logic without managing any infrastructure .")

[Review key concepts](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Functions/Concepts/functionsconcepts.htm "Find out about the key concepts you need to understand before using OCI Functions.")

[Product page](https://www.oracle.com/cloud/cloud-native/functions/)

#### Create Functions

[Create applications](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Functions/Tasks/functionscreatingapps.htm "Find out how to create applications with OCI Functions.")

[Create functions](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Functions/Tasks/functionsuploading.htm "Find out how to create and deploy functions to OCI Functions using Fn Project CLI commands.")

[Use existing Docker images](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Functions/Tasks/functionscreatingfunctions.htm "Find out how to create functions from existing Docker images with OCI Functions.")

[Use custom Dockerfiles](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Functions/Tasks/functionsusingcustomdockerfiles.htm "Find out how to use your own custom Dockerfile with OCI Functions.")

#### Manage Functions

[List functions](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Functions/Tasks/functionsviewingfunctionsapps.htm "Find out how to list functions with OCI Functions.")

[Update functions](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Functions/Tasks/functionsupdatingfunctions.htm "Find out how to update existing functions in the OCI Functions server.")

[Scan function images](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Functions/Tasks/functionsimagescanning.htm "Find out how to enable and disable scans of function images pushed to Container Registry using OCI Functions, and how to check scan results for vulnerabilities found in those function images.")

[Sign function images](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Functions/Tasks/functionsenforcingsignedimagesfromocir.htm "Find out how to sign function images, and how to enforce the use of signed images from Oracle Cloud Infrastructure Registry when deploying when deploying and invoking functions using OCI Functions.")

# Oracle Cloud Infrastructure Console Dashboards

### [Oracle Cloud Infrastructure Documentation](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/home.htm)

* * *

All Pages

[Skip to main content](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Dashboards/home.htm#dcoc-content-body)

Oracle Cloud Infrastructure Console Dashboards
==============================================

The Console Dashboards service allows you to create custom dashboards in the Oracle Cloud Infrastructure Console to monitor resources, diagnostics, and key metrics for your tenancy.

[What's new](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/releasenotes/services/dashboards/)

#### Get Started

[Learn about Console Dashboards](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Dashboards/Concepts/dashboardsoverview.htm#overview "The Console Dashboards service allows you to create custom dashboards in the Oracle Cloud Infrastructure Console to monitor resources, diagnostics, and key metrics for your tenancy.")

[Enable Console Dashboards](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Dashboards/Tasks/dashboards-getting-started.htm#top "Learn how to start using the Console Dashboards service.")

[Securing Console Dashboards](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/Security/Reference/dashboards-security.htm)

#### Community

[Oracle Cloud Infrastructure Blog](https://blogs.oracle.com/cloud-infrastructure/)

[Cloud infrastructure community forum](https://community.oracle.com/tech/apps-infra/categories/18430-cloud-infrastructure)

#### Using Console Dashboards

[Managing Console Dashboards](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Dashboards/Tasks/dashboards.htm#top "Dashboards let you monitor resources, diagnostics, and key metrics for your tenancy in the Oracle Cloud Infrastructure Console.")

[Working with Console Dashboards Groups](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Dashboards/Tasks/dashboardgroups.htm#top "Dashboard groups let you share collections of dashboards with designated groups of people.")

[Configuring Widgets](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Dashboards/Tasks/widgetmanagement.htm#top "Configure Console Dashboards widgets so that they give you insight into your resource usage, billing, and system health. Each dashboard can contain multiple widgets.")

[Support Requests](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/GSG/Tasks/contactingsupport.htm)

[Post a question to the forum](https://community.oracle.com/customerconnect/categories/oracle-cloud-infrastructure-and-platform)

# Document Conventions and Tools

### [Oracle Cloud Infrastructure Documentation](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/home.htm)

* * *

All Pages

[Skip to main content](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/General/Reference/docconventions.htm#dcoc-content-body)

Document Conventions and Tools
==============================

Oracle Cloud Infrastructure documentation employs various visual cues to enhance the clarity and usefulness of the information it provides. This page explains those font and presentation conventions. It also provides tips for effective searches in the online Help and describes other features and tools.

Font Conventions
----------------

The following font conventions have specific meaning within this document.

### User Interface Labels

Text and labels that appear in the user interface appear in bold text. For example:

In the Console, click **Compute**, and then choose your **Compartment**.

### Commands

Commands that you enter from your keyboard appear in a monospace typeface. For example:

    sudo su

### Variables

Placeholder text for required values appears in red text surrounded by angle brackets. For example:

    useradd <new_user>
                        

### Code and Response Examples

Code and system response examples appear in a monospace typeface. For example:

    # UPDATEDEFAULT specifies if new-kernel-pkg should make
    # new kernels the default
    UPDATEDEFAULT=yes
    
    # DEFAULTKERNEL specifies the default kernel package type
    DEFAULTKERNEL=kernel-uek

### Notes, Tips, and Cautions

The documentation applies the following styles to draw your attention to important information:

**Note**  
  
"Note" boxes provide information of special interest or importance to help you understand the behavior of the system.

**Important**  
  
"Important" boxes address essential points to complete a task or understand a topic.

**Tip**  
  
"Tips" provide helpful suggestions that can improve your experience using Oracle Cloud Infrastructure, or alternative ways of doing things.

**Caution**  
  
"Cautions" provide important information to help you prevent data loss or damage.

Reading Railroad Diagrams
-------------------------

Oracle Cloud Infrastructure uses railroad diagrams to express defined grammars, showing syntax for policy statements, queries, and other items.

When reading these diagrams, consider the following conventions:

*   Vertical bars indicate the beginning and end of an expression line.
    
*   The expression line reads from left to right (and sometimes down), following the line or any of the possible branches.
    
*   Literal values or commands are depicted as lozenge-shaped boxes.
    
    For example, `1m` is a literal value for "Interval" in the following absent alarm query.
    
*   Variable values are depicted as rectangular boxes.
    
    For example, `metric` is a variable value in the following absent alarm query.
    
*   Concatenation loops are indicated by either a comma or special symbols.
    
    For example, a comma indicates possible concatenation loops for name-value pairs in the dimension component of the following absent alarm query.
    
*   An optional component is indicated by an empty branch (no value or command) above or below it.
    
    For example, the dimension and group components are optional in the following absent alarm query.
    

Example railroad diagram from [Monitoring Query Language (MQL) Reference](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/Monitoring/Reference/mql.htm)
:

[![MQL expression.](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Monitoring/images/monitoring-mql.svg "Click to expand")](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Monitoring/images/monitoring-mql.svg)

Document Tools
--------------

Oracle Cloud Infrastructure documentation provides several tools to help you find the information you need. Chief among these tools is a powerful full-text search engine that features phrase, and partial-word searching.

The following tools do not apply to PDF documentation.

### Online Search

When you search the Oracle Cloud Infrastructure documentation, results appear in a ranked-order list. The ranking favors topics with search terms in page titles, followed by headings, and pages with multiple instances of the search terms. After titles and headings, search evaluates metadata descriptions, and then body text last. Search results include the topic title and an excerpt from the topic's first paragraph. When you click a topic to open it, your search terms are highlighted in the topic.

**Full-text searches** are case-insensitive. A search for the word "availability" returns results with both "availability" and "Availability". Searches also return variant endings. For example, a search for the word "run" also returns matches for words like "runner", "running", and "runs".

When you search by using **multiple terms**, a Boolean AND is inferred. (However, Boolean search is not supported.) You can search for precise matches on terms and phrases by enclosing the terms in quotation marks. For example, you can use quotation marks to search for the phrase "availability domain" and get results for topics in which those terms appear in that exact order.

Your search results can include topics that contain synonyms for the search term you specified. If the results include topics that do not appear to have your search term in them, be sure to scan those topics for relevant information. To avoid seeing results that contain synonyms, enclose your search term in quotation marks.

You can search for **partial words** and number strings with a minimum of three characters. For example, a search for "345" returns results such as "345" and "34567". A partially matching result always begins with the same first letter as the search term it matches.

To compensate for misspellings, searches apply **fuzzy matching** to search terms with a minimum of four characters. For example, a search for "victual network" returns results for "virtual network", replacing the letter "c" with the letter "r" to match "victual" to "virtual". The degree to which letter substitutions are allowed depends on the length of the search term. Search does not attempt the replacement of any letters in words that are four characters or less. If a word has between five and nine characters, search attempts to replace up to one letter when matching results. If a word has 10 characters or more, search allows up to two substitutions.

To determine the relevancy of a result, a search begins by matching against each word independently. Then, it treats the search input as a single phrase. Lastly, the location of matching terms, publication date, and popularity are considered. Popularity is determined by traffic, and an older topic might rank more highly in results than a newer topic if it is more popular.

By default, results are limited to content in the Oracle Cloud Infrastructure product documentation. If you want to expand a search to the rest of the Oracle Help Center, close the scope chip by hovering over the **Oracle Cloud Infrastructure** scope chip, and then clicking the **X**.

**Tip**  
  

The search engine does NOT recognize the asterisk (\*) as a wildcard character.

Searching in PDF documents conforms to the search capabilities of your PDF reader.

### Glossary Terms

A full [glossary page](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/libraries/glossary/glossary-intro.htm#nav-intro "You can learn about Oracle Cloud Infrastructure terms and concepts in this glossary.")
 provides definitions for all special terms used in this document. On many pages, glossary terms also appear as links upon their first use within the topic. To see the definition, hover your cursor over the term. For example:

Volumes are only accessible to instances in the same **availability domain** .

Supported Browsers
------------------

Oracle Cloud Infrastructure supports the following browsers and versions:

| Browser | Version | Notes |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Microsoft Edge | Latest three major versions only | None |
| Firefox | Latest three major versions only | Private Browsing mode isn't supported |
| Google Chrome | Latest three major versions only | None |
| Safari | Latest three major versions only | None |

*   [Document Conventions and Tools](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/General/Reference/docconventions.htm#Document_Conventions_and_Tools)
    
*   [Font Conventions](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/General/Reference/docconventions.htm#one)
    
*   [Reading Railroad Diagrams](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/General/Reference/docconventions.htm#railroad-diagrams)
    
*   [Document Tools](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/General/Reference/docconventions.htm#two)
    
*   [Supported Browsers](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/General/Reference/docconventions.htm#three)

# Health Checks

### [Oracle Cloud Infrastructure Documentation](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/home.htm)

* * *

All Pages

[Skip to main content](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/HealthChecks/home.htm#dcoc-content-body)

Health Checks
=============

The Health Checks service provides external monitoring to help you determine the availability and performance of any public-facing service, load balancer, or API endpoint.

[What's new](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/releasenotes/services/health-checks/)

#### Get Started

[Learn about the Health Checks service](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/HealthChecks/Concepts/healthchecks.htm#top "The Oracle Cloud Infrastructure Health Checks service provides users with high frequency external monitoring to determine the availability and performance of any publicly facing service, including hosted websites, API endpoints, or externally facing load balancers. By using Health Checks, users can ensure that they're immediately aware of any availability issue affecting their customers.")

[Managing health checks](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/HealthChecks/Tasks/managinghealthchecks.htm#top "Monitor the health of IP addresses and hosts using HTTP and ping monitors and on-demand probes.")

[Health checks metrics](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/HealthChecks/Reference/metricsalarms.htm#top "View metric charts, create queries, and review details about Health Checks service metrics.")

[Securing Health Checks](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/Security/Reference/healthchecks_security.htm)

[Product page](https://www.oracle.com/cloud/networking/health-checks/)

#### Support

[Support Requests](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/GSG/Tasks/contactingsupport.htm)

[Create a service request](https://support.oracle.com/)

#### Manage Health Checks

[Create an HTTP monitor](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/HealthChecks/Tasks/create-http-monitor.htm#top "Create an HTTP monitor in Health Checks.")

[Create a ping monitor](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/HealthChecks/Tasks/create-ping-monitor.htm#top "Create a ping monitor in Health Checks.")

[Create an HTTP on-demand probe](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/HealthChecks/Tasks/create-http-probe.htm#top "Create an HTTP on-demand probe in Health Checks.")

[Create a ping on-demand probe](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/HealthChecks/Tasks/create-ping-probe.htm#top "Create a ping on-demand probe in Health Checks.")

[List vantage points](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/HealthChecks/Tasks/list-vantage-point.htm#top "List vantage points in Health Checks.")

#### Community

[Oracle Cloud Infrastructure blog](https://blogs.oracle.com/cloud-infrastructure/)

[A-Team Chronicles](https://www.ateam-oracle.com/)

[Cloud infrastructure community forum](https://community.oracle.com/tech/apps-infra/categories/18430-cloud-infrastructure)

#### Troubleshooting

[Troubleshoot a continuously failing health check](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/HealthChecks/troubleshooting.htm#continually-fail "Troubleshoot health checks that are continually returning non-healthy results.")

# Oracle Linux

### [Oracle Cloud Infrastructure Documentation](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/home.htm)

* * *

All Pages

[Skip to main content](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/oracle-linux/home.htm#dcoc-content-body)

Oracle Linux
============

Use Oracle Linux for the most cost-effective and integrated operating environment for Oracle Cloud Infrastructure.

[What's new](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/images/)

#### Get Started

[Review the latest general notices](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/oracle-linux/oci/general-notices.htm#general-notices "These notices apply to operating system updates that fall outside of standard release cycles or to updates that apply to images on Oracle Cloud Infrastructure.")

[Get started with Oracle Linux](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/oracle-linux/oci/gettings-started.htm#get-started-oracle-linux "Oracle Cloud Infrastructure (OCI) has a set of Oracle Linux platform images that you can select when creating an instance.")

[Review the latest image release information](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/oracle-linux/oci/release-info.htm#release-info "Oracle Linux images are released for Oracle Cloud Infrastructure regularly to use the most recent packages for important security and bug fixes.")

[Product page](https://www.oracle.com/linux/)

#### Learning Tracks

[Introduction to Oracle Linux](https://www.oracle.com/goto/linuxintrolearning)

[Launch a Linux instance tutorial](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/Compute/tutorials/first-linux-instance/overview.htm)

[Grow your Oracle Linux skill set](https://www.oracle.com/goto/oraclelinuxlearning)

[Use Oracle Linux on Cloud](https://www.oracle.com/goto/linuxcloudlearning)

#### Security

[Oracle Linux Security](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/oracle-linux/oci/security.htm#security-on-cloud "Follow these security best practices to securely deploy and use Oracle Linux instances in a cloud environment. Oracle Linux also provides several cloud-relevant services that perform automatic software updates, install bug fixes, and monitor deployed instances for critical events.")

[Oracle Autonomous Linux image](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/oracle-linux/oci/autonomous-linux.htm#autonomous-linux "Use the Oracle Autonomous Linux image to reduce the complexity and overhead of common operating system management tasks, including automatic daily updates and monitoring of critical events.")

[Oracle Ksplice](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/oracle-linux/oci/ol-ksplice.htm#security_ksplice "Use Oracle Ksplice to apply critical security patches to Linux kernels on Oracle Cloud Infrastructure instances without requiring a reboot. On Oracle Linux, Ksplice also updates the glibc and OpenSSL user space libraries, applying critical security patches without disrupting workloads.")

[Oracle Linux STIG image](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/oracle-linux/oci/stig-image.htm#top "The Oracle Linux STIG Image is an implementation of Oracle Linux that follows the Security Technical Implementation Guide (STIG).")

#### Support

[Get help and contact Support](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/GSG/Tasks/contactingsupport.htm)

[Create a service request](https://support.oracle.com/epmos/faces/MosIndex.jspx)

#### Create an Instance

[Create a Linux instance](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/Compute/Tasks/launchinginstance.htm#linux__linux-create)

[Enhance security with Oracle Linux STIG](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/oracle-linux/oci/stig-image.htm#top "The Oracle Linux STIG Image is an implementation of Oracle Linux that follows the Security Technical Implementation Guide (STIG).")

[Deploy KVM hosts in Oracle Cloud](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/oracle-linux/oci/index.htm#kvm "KVM provides a set of modules that enable you to use the Oracle Linux kernel as a hypervisor to run virtual machines. KVM is built into the Unbreakable Enterprise Kernel (UEK) by default.")

[Build NFS and Samba shared storage in Oracle Cloud](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/oracle-linux/oci/storage-appliance-image.htm#oracle-linux-storage-appliance "The Oracle Linux Storage Appliance provides a fast and easy way to build a shared storage system on Oracle Cloud Infrastructure. It enables you to export files by using the protocols Network File System (NFS) v3 and v4, and Windows Server Message Block (SMB) v3 (Samba).")

[Optimize boot performance with Oracle Linux Minimal](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/oracle-linux/oci/minimal-image.htm#top "The Oracle Linux 9 Minimal platform image has been optimized to provide a minimum footprint with an accelerated boot time.")

#### Utilities

[OCI Utilities](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/Compute/References/ociutilities.htm)

[OCIFS Utility](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/oracle-linux/oci/ocifs.htm#index "The OCIFS utility lets you mount Oracle Cloud Infrastructure Object Storage buckets as file systems. You can then manage the objects in the buckets as regular files and directories.")

[OCI Image Migrate Utilities](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/oracle-linux/oci/migrate-utils.htm#index "You can migrate on-premises Oracle Linux images to Oracle Cloud Infrastructure with the oci-image-migrate utilities.")

[OCI-KVM Utilities](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/oracle-linux/oci/oci-kvm-utility.htm#oci-kvm-utility "The OCI-KVM (oci-kvm) utilities allow you to create and configure KVM guests on Oracle Cloud Infrastructure instances.")

#### Developer Tools

[Oracle Linux Cloud Developer Image](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/oracle-linux/oci/developer-image.htm#top "The Oracle Linux Cloud Developer image provides the latest development tools, languages, and Oracle Cloud Infrastructure Software Development Kits (SDKs) to rapidly deploy a comprehensive development environment. ")

[SDKs and the CLI](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/API/Concepts/sdks.htm)

[Cloud Shell](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/API/Concepts/devcloudshellintro.htm)

[developer tutorials](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/developer/home.htm)

#### Services

[OS Management Hub](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/osmh/doc/home.htm)

[Autonomous Linux](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/autonomous-linux/doc/home.htm)

#### Clustering

[Install Pacemaker](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/oracle-linux/oci/ha-clustering-overview.htm#ha-installing-pmaker "To create a high availability (HA) cluster with Oracle Cloud Infrastructure (OCI) instances, you must first install the Pacemaker and Corosync packages on each instance, or node, that you want in the cluster. You can then configure each cluster node, and ensure that the Pacemaker service automatically starts and runs on each node at boot time.")

[Create a cluster](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/oracle-linux/oci/ha-clustering-overview.htm#ha-create-cluster "With the Pacemaker and Corosync software, you can create a high availability (HA) cluster with Linux instances running on Oracle Cloud Infrastructure (OCI).")

[Configure fencing](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/oracle-linux/oci/ha-clustering-overview.htm#ha-configure-sbd-fencing "STONITH Block Device (SBD) fencing works with the Pacemaker software to protect data when a node in a high availability (HA) cluster becomes unresponsive. Fencing prevents the live nodes in the HA cluster from accessing data on the unresponsive node until the Pacemaker software takes that unresponsive node offline.")

#### Learn More

[General Oracle Linux documentation](https://docs.oracle.com/en/operating-systems/oracle-linux)

[Unbreakable Enterprise Kernel documentation](https://docs.oracle.com/en/operating-systems/uek)

#### Community

[Oracle Cloud Infrastructure blog](https://blogs.oracle.com/cloud-infrastructure/)

[Cloud infrastructure community forum](https://community.oracle.com/tech/apps-infra/categories/18430-cloud-infrastructure)

# Email Delivery

### [Oracle Cloud Infrastructure Documentation](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/home.htm)

* * *

All Pages

[Skip to main content](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Email/home.htm#dcoc-content-body)

Email Delivery
==============

Email Delivery is an email sending service and SMTP relay that provides a fast and reliable managed solution for sending both high volume bulk and transactional emails that need to reach the inbox.

[What's new](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/releasenotes/services/email/)

#### Getting Started

[Learn about Email Delivery service](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Email/Concepts/overview.htm "The Oracle Cloud Infrastructure Email Delivery service provides a fast and reliable managed solution for sending secured, high-volume marketing and transactional emails.")

[Video: Overview](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=-hWI_wlojKA)

[First steps with Email Delivery](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Email/Reference/gettingstarted.htm "Configure Oracle Cloud Infrastructure Email Delivery to use the functionalities of the Email Delivery service. You can configure using the Console, REST API, SDKs, CLI, or Terraform.")

[Best practices for email deliverability](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Email/Reference/deliverabilitybestpractices.htm "Learn about the best practices to deliver bulk, marketing, or transactional emails to opt-in recipients.")

[Email Delivery service limits](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/General/service-limits/default.htm#Email_Delivery_Limits)

[Product page](https://www.oracle.com/cloud/networking/email-delivery/)

#### Reporting

[Log details for Email Delivery](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/Logging/Reference/details_for_emaildelivery.htm)

[Email Delivery metrics](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Email/Reference/metricsalarms.htm "Monitor the health, capacity, and performance of the Email Delivery service by using metrics, alarms, and notifications.")

[Manage the suppression list](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Email/Tasks/managingsuppressionlist.htm "As you begin to send email, Email Delivery automatically adds email addresses with bounce codes showing permanent failures, or user complaints to the suppression list to protect the sender reputation. Email Delivery doesn't send any messages to these recipients in the future.")

#### FAQs

[Click here for frequently asked questions](https://www.oracle.com/cloud/networking/email-delivery/faq/)

[List-Unsubscribe](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Email/Reference/list-unsubscribe.htm "List-Unsubscribe is an industry-standard email feature that provides recipients with a simplified way to opt-out of receiving further emails from a sender, using their email client. It uses special email headers to provide participating email clients with a method to send unsubscribe requests. It is a best practice for senders to include these headers in the email to decrease spam complaint rates, and increase overall engagement.")

#### Set Up Email Delivery

[Creating a Group and User](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Email/Reference/gettingstarted_topic-create-group.htm "Learn how to create a group and user, and add a user to a group.")

[Creating User Permissions](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Email/Reference/gettingstarted_topic-create-policy.htm "Learn how to create a policy to allow a group to manage email-family resources.")

[Creating SMTP Credentials](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Email/Reference/gettingstarted_topic-create-smtp-credentials.htm "Learn how to create SMTP credentials.")

[Creating an Email Domain](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Email/Reference/gettingstarted_topic-create-email-domain.htm "Learn how to create a DNS email domain to send bulk emails from approved senders.")

[Creating an Approved Sender](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Email/Reference/gettingstarted_topic-Create_an_approved_sender.htm "Learn how to create an approved sender who can send emails using the OCI Email Delivery service.")

[Setting up Email Submissions](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Email/Reference/gettingstarted_topic-set-up-email-submissions.htm "Use OCI Email Delivery for a fast and reliable managed solution to submit secure, high-volume marketing, and transactional emails using the Hypertext Transfer Protocol Secure (HTTPS) interface or the Simple Mail Transfer Protocol (SMTP) interface.")

[Sending an Email](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Email/Reference/gettingstarted_topic-Begin_sending_email.htm "Learn to use the Email Delivery service by configuring Oracle Cloud and third-party applications to send an email.")

[Validating Email Delivery](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Email/Reference/gettingstarted_topic-Validate_email.htm "Learn how to use the Oracle Cloud Infrastructure Logging service to validate whether an email is delivered.")

#### Developer Tools

[API for Email Delivery](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/api/#/en/emaildelivery/latest/)

[SDKs and the CLI](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/API/Concepts/sdks.htm)

[Cloud Shell](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/API/Concepts/devcloudshellintro.htm)

[Quick start in Git hub](https://github.com/oracle-quickstart/oci-ed-setup)

#### Community

[Email-related blog posts](https://blogs.oracle.com/cloud-infrastructure/authors/Blog-Author/CORE94AB2886640E403B93A7D9469F1C5AD9/josh-nason)

[Oracle Cloud Infrastructure blog](https://blogs.oracle.com/cloud-infrastructure/)

#### Troubleshooting

[Troubleshoot Email Delivery](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Email/Concepts/troubleshooting.htm "Use troubleshooting information to identify and address common issues that can occur while working Email Delivery.")

[Troubleshoot Email Delivery with service logs](https://blogs.oracle.com/cloud-infrastructure/post/announcing-availability-of-oracle-cloud-infrastructure-oci-email-delivery-service-logs)

#### Integrations

[Oracle Application Express](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Email/Reference/apex.htm "Use Oracle APEX to send emails through the Email Delivery service.")

[Postfix](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Email/Reference/postfix.htm "Use Postfix to send emails through the Email Delivery service.")

[JavaMail](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Email/Reference/javamail.htm "Use JavaMail to send emails through the Email Delivery service.")

[PeopleSoft](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Email/Reference/peoplesoft.htm "Use PeopleSoft to send emails through the Email Delivery service.")

#### Support

[Get help and contact support](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/GSG/Tasks/contactingsupport.htm)

[Create a service request](https://support.oracle.com/)

Events
======

The Events service helps you create automation based on the state changes of resources throughout your tenancy.

[What's new](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/releasenotes/services/events/)

#### Get Started

[Learn about Events](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Events/Concepts/eventsoverview.htm "Create automation based on the state changes of resources throughout your tenancy.")

[Tutorial: Create a rule (CLI)](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Events/Concepts/eventsgetstarted.htm#CLI)

#### Match Events to Filters

Resource Manager
================

Automate deployment and operations for Oracle Cloud Infrastructure resources using Resource Manager. With supported Infrastructure as Code (IaC) tools, DevOps engineers can develop and deploy their infrastructure anywhere.

[What's new](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/releasenotes/services/resource-manager/)

[Product page](https://www.oracle.com/devops/resource-manager/)

[View the state of a stack](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/ResourceManager/Tasks/get-stack-tf-state.htm "Download the Terraform state file used by a stack in Resource Manager. The Terraform state file for a stack is the one associated with the most recent successful job.")
