# Oracle AI Database@Google Cloud

### [Oracle Cloud Infrastructure Documentation](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/home.htm)

* * *

All Pages

[Skip to main content](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/database-at-gcp/home.htm#dcoc-content-body)

Oracle AI Database@Google Cloud
===============================

Learn about using Oracle AI Database@Google Cloud to run Oracle databases in your existing Google Cloud environment.

Oracle AI Database@Google Cloud is a database service that lets you to access OCI managed Oracle Exadata infrastructure inside Google Cloud data centers.

The service lets you to migrate on-premises Oracle workloads to Google Cloud, giving your business-critical applications low-latency connectivity to your Oracle databases. With Oracle AI Database@Google Cloud, you also have access to Oracle Real Application Clusters (RAC) and Oracle Exadata performance for mission-critical applications in Google Cloud. The service integrates with other Google Cloud services, and you get a single invoice through Google Cloud Marketplace that includes Oracle AI Database@Google Cloud billing. Oracle AI Database@Google Cloud usage counts toward Google Cloud Commitment.

The following high-level architecture diagram shows how an OCI region (parent site) is connected with an Google Cloud region's Oracle Zone (child site) through an OCI managed network as part of Oracle AI Database@Google Cloud. An application server deployed on Google Cloud VM connects to the Oracle databases deployed in an Oracle Database (ODB) network. The control plane, which is highly available and fault-tolerant, is deployed in the OCI Region and offers a seamless database management and administration experience.

[![High-level architecture.](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/database-at-gcp/images/gcp-arch-high-level.png "Click to expand")](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/database-at-gcp/images/gcp-arch-high-level.png)

Benefits of Using Oracle AI Database@Google Cloud
-------------------------------------------------

Oracle AI Database@Google Cloud offers a powerful and flexible solution for running Oracle workloads directly within Google Cloud. Key benefits include the following features:

### Simplified Migration

Oracle AI Database@Google Cloud lets you seamlessly migrate your Oracle workloads to either Oracle Exadata Database Service on Dedicated Infrastructure or Oracle Autonomous AI Database, deployed within Google Cloud. These migrations require minimal database changes, retain full Oracle feature sets, maintain architectural compatibility, and deliver performance equal to on-premises environments. Supported migration tools include RMAN, Oracle Data Guard, transportable tablespaces, Oracle Data Pump, Oracle GoldenGate, and Oracle Zero Downtime Migration (ZDM).

### Low-Latency Connectivity

Hosting Oracle AI Database close to your Google Cloud applications reduces network latency, which ensures faster response times and improved application performance. This proximity minimizes data transfer delays and optimizes system efficiency.

### Unified Management and Operations

Oracle AI Database@Google Cloud offers a cohesive operational experience with integrated purchasing, support, and lifecycle management across Oracle and Google Cloud. It supports your existing Google Cloud commitments and Oracle license entitlements. You can manage Oracle resources using Google Cloud API, CLI, and SDK, which internally invoke the necessary OCI APIs for provisioning and management.

### Seamless Integration

Oracle AI Database@Google Cloud seamlessly integrates with core Google Cloud services such as Google Compute Engine, Virtual Private Network (VPC), and IAM, enabling cohesive security and infrastructure management. It also supports Google Cloud-native tools such as Google Cloud Observability for monitoring.

Available Database Services
---------------------------

Oracle AI Database@Google Cloud offers the following Oracle database services:

| Service Name | Available Versions | Private Offer Eligible? | Public Offer Eligible? |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| [Oracle Exadata Database Service on Dedicated Infrastructure](https://docs.oracle.com/en/engineered-systems/exadata-cloud-service/ecscm/exadata-cloud-infrastructure-overview.html) | 19c, 26ai | Yes | No  |
| [Oracle Exadata Database Service on Exascale Infrastructure](https://docs.oracle.com/en/engineered-systems/exadata-database-exascale/exdxs/overview-exadb-xs-service.html) | 19c, 26ai | Yes | Yes |
| [Oracle Base Database Service](https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/paas/base-database/about/) | 19c, 26ai | Yes | Yes |
| [Oracle Autonomous AI Database](https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/paas/autonomous-database/serverless/adbsb/autonomous-intro-adb.html) | 19c, 26ai | Yes | Yes |
| [Oracle GoldenGate](https://docs.oracle.com/en/database/goldengate/index.html) | 21c, 26ai | Yes | Yes |
| [Oracle Database Autonomous Recovery Service](https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/paas/recovery-service/dbrsu/overview-recovery-service.html) | NA  | Yes | No  |

Oracle Exadata Database Service on Dedicated Infrastructure, Oracle Exadata Database Service on Exascale Infrastructure, Oracle Base Database Service, and Oracle Autonomous AI Database use the same underlying high-performance hardware optimized for database workloads, but they each offer distinct advantages to meet different customer needs.

| **Characteristic** | **Oracle Exadata Database Service on Dedicated Infrastructure** | **Oracle Exadata Database Service on Exascale Infrastructure** | **Oracle Base Database Service** | **Oracle Autonomous AI Database** |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Usage | Fully managed dedicated infrastructure for running Oracle databases. Suitable for mission-critical, latency-sensitive workloads requiring predictable performance where operational monitoring of the database belongs to the customer. | Fully managed shared infrastructure for running Oracle databases. It delivers Exadata intelligence with all cloud benefits including pay-per-use economics and a low entry cost.<br><br>Suitable for mission-critical, latency-sensitive workloads requiring predictable performance where operational monitoring of the database belongs to the customer. | Fully managed shared infrastructure for running single instance Oracle databases using low-cost general-purpose infrastructure to provide VM compute and network attached storage. Suitable for running your choice of Database editions. | Fully managed, elastic, and scalable database service. Designed for variable workloads, cloud native applications, and flexible usage, automatically scaling resources up and down as needed. Minimal admin effort needed. |
| Performance | Offers consistent and predictable high performance with low latency because of optimized hardware and software integration. | Offers consistent and predictable high performance with low latency because of optimized hardware and software integration. | Offers consistent and predictable performance for general purpose, business critical enterprise level workloads. | Provides high performance with automatic scaling. Delivers low latency and high throughput, leveraging the elasticity of the cloud. |
| Management | Requires manual management and administration, including database configuration, tuning, and maintenance. | Requires manual management and administration of the database including configuration, tuning, and maintenance. | Requires manual management and administration, including database configuration, tuning, and maintenance. | Self-driving and self-managing, automating tasks such as provisioning, scaling, tuning, patching, and backups. |
| Operations | Cloud automation provides the creation, maintenance updates, and basic monitoring for databases. Database administrators need to tune the database, apply maintenance updates, and configure additional database monitoring. Strong Oracle support problem resolution model. | Cloud automation provides the creation, maintenance updates, and basic monitoring for databases. Database administrators need to tune the database, apply maintenance updates, and configure additional database monitoring. Strong Oracle support problem resolution model. | Cloud automation provides the creation, maintenance updates, and basic monitoring for databases. Database administrators need to tune the database, apply maintenance updates, and configure additional database monitoring. Strong Oracle support problem resolution model. | Reduces operational overhead, as Oracle manages most administrative tasks. Customers only need to focus on application development and data management. |
| Cost | Upfront subscription cost for dedicated infrastructure and pay-per-use for CPUs allocated to VM Cluster resources. Provides predictable pricing. | Upfront subscription cost for shared infrastructure and pay-per-use for ECPUs allocated to VM Cluster resources. Provides predictable pricing at a lower entry point. | Upfront subscription cost for shared infrastructure and pay-per-use for ECPUs allocated to VM Cluster resources. Choice of database edition enables lower costs. | Uses a pay-as-you-go model, charging based on actual resource consumption. Costs scale up and down with usage, providing flexibility and potential cost savings. |
| Scaling | Provides elastic scaling for infrastructure so you can vertically and horizontally scale when you need more resources for your workloads. | Provides elastic scaling of VM Clusters and corresponding storage so you scale when you need more resources for your workloads. | Provide elastic independent scaling of ECPUs and storage so you scale when you need more resources for your workloads. | Offers automatic and elastic scaling. Can scale up and down based on workload demands without manual intervention, providing true serverless scalability. |
| Security | Robust security features including encryption, security patches, and operator access controls for infrastructure. Customers are responsible for security configuration and management. | Robust security features including encryption, security patches, and operator access controls for infrastructure. Customers are responsible for security configuration and management. | Robust security features including encryption, security patches, and operator access controls for infrastructure. Customers are responsible for security configuration and management. | Built-in security with automatic data encryption, user authentication, and threat detection. Oracle manages security updates and patches. |
| Configuration | Requires initial configuration and setup, including hardware provisioning and software installation. Full control over configuration but default values suggested in provisioning. | No infrastructure provisioning required. Full control over VM and database configuration but default values suggested in provisioning. | No infrastructure provisioning required. Full control over VM and database configuration but default values suggested in provisioning. | Pre-configured and instantly provisioned, requiring minimal setup and configuration. |
| Patching | Infrastructure security and maintenance updates applied automatically on configured schedule. You receive Database, Grid Infrastructure, and Guest OS patches, which you apply based on your schedule. | Infrastructure security and maintenance updates applied automatically on configured schedule. You receive Database, Grid Infrastructure, and Guest OS patches, which you apply based on your schedule. | You receive Database, Grid Infrastructure, and Guest OS patches, which you apply based on your schedule. | Automatic patching with no downtime, managed by Oracle. |
| Isolation | Complete isolation as customers has their own dedicated infrastructure. | Virtual machine isolation as well as the corresponding storage for database files. | Virtual machine isolation as well as the corresponding storage for database files. | Data isolation through dedicated database instances, ensuring customer data separation. |
| Flexibility | Offers flexibility in customization within the VM for existing app scripts and dependent libraries, so easiest lift and shift to cloud. | Start with a single node VM and scale up to a 10 node VM Cluster and 200 ECPU per VM. Offers flexibility in customization within the VM for existing app scripts and dependent libraries, so easiest lift and shift to cloud. | Start with 4 ECPUs and 300 GB of storage and scale up to 200 ECPUs and 40 TB of storage. Offers flexibility in customization within the VM for existing app scripts and dependent libraries, so easiest lift and shift to cloud. | Fully managed and monitored by Oracle. Provides flexibility in usage and scaling, adapting to dynamic workload demands. |
| Third-party integrations | Integrates with third-party tools and applications through Oracle's extensive partner ecosystem and APIs. | Integrates with third-party tools and applications through Oracle's extensive partner ecosystem and APIs. | Integrates with third-party tools and applications through Oracle's extensive partner ecosystem and APIs. | Integrates with third-party tools and applications through Oracle's extensive partner ecosystem and APIs. |

Use Cases
---------

Oracle Exadata Database Service on Dedicated Infrastructure, Oracle Exadata Database Service on Exascale Infrastructure, Oracle Base Database Service and Oracle Autonomous AI Database offer high performance and run on the Oracle optimized hardware, the choice depends on the specific needs of the enterprise. Each service has it's advantages:

*   Exadata Database Service on Dedicated Infrastructure provides dedicated infrastructure, predictable performance, and customization, making it a good choice for mission-critical, latency-sensitive applications, especially in regulated industries.
*   Exadata Database Service on Exascale Infrastructure provides shared infrastructure, predictable performance, and customization, making it a good choice for mission-critical, latency-sensitive applications.
*   Base Database Service provides fully managed shared infrastructure for running single instance Oracle databases using low-cost general-purpose infrastructure suitable for running your choice of Database editions.
*   Autonomous AI Database offers flexibility, automation, and dynamic scaling, making it ideal for variable workloads and cloud native applications.

### Oracle Exadata Database Service on Dedicated Infrastructure

Oracle Exadata Database Service on Dedicated Infrastructure offers a predictable performance, scalable, and reliable database solution for enterprises with demanding workloads and specific compliance requirements.

#### Small Enterprises

While Exadata Database Service is typically chosen by larger enterprises, small businesses with specific compliance or performance requirements can also benefit. For example, a small financial services firm dealing with sensitive data might require the dedicated infrastructure and extreme performance that Exadata offers.

Example: A boutique investment firm handling sensitive financial data uses Exadata Database Service because it provides them with a dedicated, highly secure, and high performance database environment, and lets them meet strict regulatory compliance standards.

#### Mid-Sized Enterprises

Mid-sized enterprises with high-volume, mission-critical workloads can benefit from the power and scalability of Exadata Database Service. It provides them with the performance, reliability, and availability needed to support their critical applications.

Example: A mid-sized online gaming company with millions of concurrent users uses Exadata Database Service because the platform's extreme performance and scalability can handle the high-volume transactional workload, ensuring a seamless and responsive user experience.

#### Large Enterprises

Large enterprises with demanding enterprise applications, data warehouses, or consolidation projects can use Exadata Database Service to handle massive data volumes, high concurrency, and complex queries with ease.

Example: A large telecommunications company with terabytes of data and complex analytical queries can use Exadata Database Service because the platform's parallel processing and in-memory capabilities speed up query performance, enabling faster decision-making and improved operational efficiency.

### Oracle Exadata Database Service on Exascale Infrastructure

Oracle Exadata Database Service on Exascale Infrastructure offers a predictable performance, scalable, and reliable database solution for enterprises with smaller workloads with Exadata benefits.

#### Small Enterprises

Exadata Database Service on Exascale Infrastructure is ideally suited for small businesses with Exadata performance requirements. For example, a small financial services firm dealing with sensitive data might require extreme performance that Exadata offers.

Example: A small hospital using Medical Data of Clinical & Patient Record Management Systems supprting research analytics and clinical applications. Delivered high performance for critical workloads, including OLTP and genomic data analysis, with streamlined database management and optimized IT operations.

#### Mid-Sized Enterprises

Mid-sized enterprises with high-volume, mission-critical workloads can benefit from the power and scalability of Exadata Database Service on Exascale Infrastructure. It provides them with the performance, reliability, and availability needed to support their critical applications.

Example: ISVs that require local database presence in many regions with variable market sizes in each region that require Exadata performance and availability for their mission critical applications. Exadata Database Service on Exascale Infrastructure provides low entry point to each market and easily scales as that market grows.

#### Large Enterprises

Large enterprises with demanding enterprise applications, data warehouses, or consolidation projects can use Exadata Database Service on Exascale Infrastructure to handle massive data volumes, high concurrency, and complex queries with ease.

Example: A large multinational company that operates departments or regions separately, each with their own budgets. Exadata Database Service on Exascale Infrastructure provides the ability to right-size the Exadata infrastructure according to each department size or budget.

### Oracle Base Database Service

Oracle Base Database Service is an excellent choice to run single instance Oracle databases using low-cost general-purpose infrastructure to provide VM compute and network attached storage and running your choice of Database editions.

#### Small Enterprises

Small businesses with limited Oracle footprint and using Oracle applications such as E-Business Suite, JD Edwards, PeopleSoft.

Example: A small university wanted to avoid any downtime to core administrative systems, because it would be extremely disruptive to operations. After migrating its JD Edwards financial management and procurement applications to Oracle Cloud Infrastructure (OCI) with Oracle AI Database, the university saw improved productivity and availability.

#### Mid-Sized Enterprises

Mid-sized enterprises often have growing data needs that require a cost-effective and scalable solution. Base Database Service allows customers to select database edition and scale resources to meet their workloads.

Example: A mid-size insurance, a provider of life, health, motor, and corporate insurance offers seamless digital services to its customers. Managing its core customer policy lifecycle applications on an aging on-premises infrastructure was costly and complex, prompting the need for a more efficient solution. After migration to Oracle Base Database Service, the company recognized a noticeable operational cost reduction while improving system performance.

#### Large Enterprises

Large enterprises often have diverse database requirements, including handling critical and unpredictable workloads. Basse Database Service allows customers to scale up to 200 ECPUs and 40 TB of storage to run critical application while maintaining flexibility and operational control.

Example: A large luggage manufacturing and retailer used Oracle APEX on Base Database Service to deploy a luggage-as-a-service application that supports customers globally with reduced downtime and costs. The app is designed to save time and money by handling passenger claims and repairs for damaged luggage.

### Oracle Autonomous AI Database

Oracle Autonomous AI Database is an excellent choice for enterprises of all sizes, offering high performance, flexibility and economical database solutions.

#### Small Enterprises

Small businesses often have dynamic and unpredictable workloads. Autonomous AI Database is ideal for this scenario. It lets small enterprises focus on their core business without the overhead of managing database infrastructure.

_Example:_ A small e-commerce startup uses Autonomous AI Database as the foundation of its online store because the database can scale automatically based on demand, ensuring the best performance during peak sales periods and lowering costs when demand is low.

#### Mid-Sized Enterprises

Mid-sized enterprises often have growing data needs that require a cost-effective and scalable solution. Autonomous AI Database lets them to pay for only what they use, making it a flexible and economical choice.

_Example:_ A mid-sized SaaS company offering customer relationship management (CRM) solutions uses Autonomous AI Database for their service. As their customer base grows, the database can scale seamlessly, ensuring they only pay for the resources consumed during that period. This helps them manage costs effectively while delivering a reliable service to their clients.

#### Large Enterprises

Large enterprises often have diverse database requirements, including handling critical and unpredictable workloads. Autonomous AI Database provides the flexibility to accommodate such needs without the complexity of managing the infrastructure.

_Example:_ A large retail chain use Autonomous AI Database for its inventory management system. During seasonal sales or promotional events, the database can automatically scale to handle increased transaction volumes, ensuring smooth operations. The autonomous capabilities also reduce the burden on the IT team, letting them to focus on strategic initiatives.

*   [Oracle AI Database@Google Cloud](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/database-at-gcp/home.htm#top)
    
*   [Benefits of Using Oracle AI Database@Google Cloud](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/database-at-gcp/home.htm#benefits)

# Archive Storage

### [Oracle Cloud Infrastructure Documentation](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/home.htm)

* * *

All Pages

[Skip to main content](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Archive/home.htm#dcoc-content-body)

Archive Storage
===============

Use Archive Storage to store data that is accessed infrequently and requires a longer retention period.

[What's new](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/releasenotes/services/objectstorage/)

#### Get Started

# Block Volume

### [Oracle Cloud Infrastructure Documentation](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/home.htm)

* * *

All Pages

[Skip to main content](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Block/home.htm#dcoc-content-body)

Block Volume
============

Block Volume provides high-performance network storage capacity that supports a broad range of I/O intensive workloads.

[What's new](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/releasenotes/services/blockvolume/)

#### Get Started

[Learn about Block Volume](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Block/Concepts/overview.htm "The Oracle Cloud Infrastructure Block Volume service lets you dynamically provision and manage block storage volumes. You can create, attach, connect, and move volumes, as well as change volume performance, as needed, to meet your storage, performance, and application requirements. After you attach and connect a volume to an instance, you can use the volume like a regular hard drive. You can also disconnect a volume and attach it to another instance without the loss of data.")

[Video: Overview](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=pmyxqM2eQwI)

[Product page](https://www.oracle.com/cloud/storage/block-volumes)

#### Volume Groups

[Learn about volume groups](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Block/Concepts/volumegroups.htm "The Oracle Cloud Infrastructure Block Volume service provides you with the capability to group together many volumes in a volume group. A volume group can include both types of volumes, boot volumes, which are the system disks for your compute instances, and block volumes for your data storage. You can use volume groups to create volume group backups and clones that are point-in-time and crash-consistent.")

[Volume group backups](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Block/Concepts/blockvolumebackups.htm "The Oracle Cloud Infrastructure Block Volume service offers a secure, cost-effective, policy-based, fully-managed backup solution.")

[Creating a volume group](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Block/Tasks/create-bv-volume-group-backup.htm "Learn how to create an automatic, point-in-time-consistent backups of all the volumes in a volume group.")

[Cloning a volume group](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Block/Concepts/clone-volume-group.htm "Clone a volume group in the Block Volume service.")

#### Developer Tools

[Overview of SDKs and the CLI](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/API/Concepts/sdks.htm)

[CLI for Block Volume](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/tools/oci-cli/latest/oci_cli_docs/cmdref/bv.html)

[API for Block Volume](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/api/#/en/iaas/latest/Volume/)

[Cloud Shell](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/API/Concepts/devcloudshellintro.htm)

#### Common Tasks with Volumes

[Create a volume](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Block/Tasks/creatingavolume.htm "Create a block volume in the Block Volume service.")

[Attach a volume](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Block/Tasks/attach-compute-volume-attachment.htm "Learn how to attach a block volume to a compute instance to expand the available storage on the instance.")

[Connect to a volume](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Block/Tasks/connectingtoavolume.htm "Connect to a block volume that's attached to a compute instance.")

[Replicate a volume](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Block/Concepts/volumereplication.htm "The Block Volume service provides you with the capability to perform ongoing automatic asynchronous replication of block volumes, boot volumes, and volume groups to other regions and availability domains.")

[Resize a volume](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Block/Tasks/resizingavolume.htm "The Oracle Cloud Infrastructure Block Volume service lets you expand the size of block volumes and boot volumes. You have several options to increase the size of your volumes.")

#### Volume Backups

[Learn about volume backups](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Block/Concepts/blockvolumebackups.htm "The Oracle Cloud Infrastructure Block Volume service offers a secure, cost-effective, policy-based, fully-managed backup solution.")

[Schedule a volume backup](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Block/Tasks/schedulingvolumebackups.htm "Use custom and predefined backup policies to schedule backups for volumes and volume groups in Block Volume for adherence to data compliance and regulatory requirements. Predefined (Oracle-defined) backup policies have a set backup frequency and retention period that can't be changed. Custom (user-defined) backup policies include configurable frequency, retention, optional cross-region copy, and optional schedules. You can apply both Oracle-defined and user-defined backup policies to volumes and volume groups.")

[Copy a volume backup to a new region](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Block/Tasks/copy-bv-backup.htm "Learn how to create a volume backup copy in a specific region.")

#### Community

[Oracle Cloud Infrastructure blog](https://blogs.oracle.com/cloud-infrastructure/)

[Cloud infrastructure community forum](https://forums.oracle.com/ords/apexds/domain/dev-community/category/15686-platform?tags=cloud-applications,cloud-infrastructure)

#### Block Volume Performance

[Learn about Block Volume performance](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Block/Concepts/blockvolumeperformance.htm)

[Change the performance of a volume](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Block/Tasks/changingvolumeperformance.htm "The Block Volume service enables you to dynamically configure the performance level for block volumes and boot volumes.")

[Enable performance autotune for a volume](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Block/Tasks/create-autotunepolicies-bv-volume.htm "Block Volume provides dynamic performance scaling with autotuning. This feature enables you to configure your volumes so that the service adjusts the performance level automatically to optimize performance.")

[Learn about Block Volume metrics and performance testing](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Block/Concepts/blockvolumeperformance_topic-Metrics_and_Performance_Testing.htm "Observe performance for the Balanced elastic performance configuration option and learn about the host maximum.")

#### Boot Volumes

[Learn about boot volumes](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Block/Concepts/bootvolumes.htm "A boot volume is created when you create an instance.")

[Attach a boot volume to an instance](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Block/Tasks/attach-compute-boot-volume-attachment.htm "Learn how to reattach a volume after it's been detached from an associated instance.")

[Create a boot volume backup](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Block/Tasks/create-bv-boot-volume-backup.htm "Learn how create a backup for a boot volume.")

[Detach a boot volume](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Block/Tasks/detach-compute-boot-volume-attachment.htm "If a boot volume issue is causing a compute instance issues, you can stop the instance and detach the boot volume, then attach it to another instance as a data volume to troubleshoot it.")

#### Support

[Learn about support requests](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/GSG/Tasks/contactingsupport.htm)

[Create a service request](https://support.oracle.com/)

# Cloud Guard

### [Oracle Cloud Infrastructure Documentation](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/home.htm)

* * *

All Pages

[Skip to main content](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/cloud-guard/home.htm#dcoc-content-body)

Cloud Guard
===========

Cloud Guard is a cloud native service that helps customers monitor, identify, achieve, and maintain a strong security posture on Oracle Cloud. Use the service to examine your Oracle Cloud Infrastructure resources for security weakness related to configuration, and your Oracle Cloud Infrastructure operators and users for risky activities. Upon detection, Cloud Guard can suggest, help, or take corrective actions, based on your configuration.

[What's New](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/releasenotes/services/cloud-guard/)

#### Get Started

[Video: Overview](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=WrEBDKJxSjo)

[Concepts](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/cloud-guard/using/cg-concepts.htm "Understand Cloud Guard components and terminology.")

[Prerequisites](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/cloud-guard/using/prerequisites.htm "Perform these tasks before you enable Oracle Cloud Guard.")

[Planning](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/cloud-guard/using/part-start.htm#cg-start-planning "Spending some time planning how Cloud Guard functionality is mapped onto your environment, before you enable and configure Cloud Guard, might save you some time later.")

[Enablement strategies](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/cloud-guard/using/part-start.htm#cg-access-enable-strats)

[Instance security](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/cloud-guard/using/cgis.htm "Instance Security is a new Oracle Cloud Guard detector recipe that monitors compute hosts for suspicious activity.")

[Product page](https://www.oracle.com/security/cloud-security/cloud-guard/)

#### About Recipes

[Oracle- and user-managed](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/cloud-guard/using/common.htm#cg-concepts-recipe-mgrs "Understand differences between these two categories of recipes.")

[How user-managed recipes work](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/cloud-guard/using/common.htm#cg-concepts-recipe-clones "Understand how a user-managed recipe retains ties to the Oracle-managed recipe from which it was cloned.")

[Configure recipes](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/cloud-guard/using/common.htm#common-recipes "Understand how Oracle Cloud Guard separates recipe settings into two levels, and how to update different settings for Oracle-managed or user-managed (cloned), detector and responder recipes at those two levels.")

#### Responders

[Required policies](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/cloud-guard/using/respond-recipes-about.htm#prereq-policies-responders "Add policy statements that are required for particular responders.")

[Responder rules](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/cloud-guard/using/respond-recipes-modify-rules.htm "You can modify different sets of rule settings in Oracle-managed and user-managed recipes in Cloud Guard")

[Automatic execution](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/cloud-guard/using/targets-modify-rules-respond.htm "Make tactical changes in responder rules from the Targets page in Cloud Guard.")

[Monitor responders](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/cloud-guard/using/respond-dash.htm "View the status of recent responders that have been triggered, and specify further actions to be taken on responders that have not completed processing.")

#### Support

[Support Requests](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/GSG/Tasks/contactingsupport.htm)

[Create a service request](https://support.oracle.com/)

#### Overview Console

[Summary information](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/cloud-guard/using/overview.htm "Use the Overview page to quickly get a sense of the overall state of security in your environment, and quickly focus on the highest risk problems Cloud Guard has detected.")

[Security score](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/cloud-guard/using/overview-security-score.htm "The security score in Cloud Guard provides a rough estimate of how secure your system is.")

[Risk score](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/cloud-guard/using/overview-risk-score.htm "The risk score in Cloud Guard provides a rough estimate of the risk level to your environment that's posed by the problems that Cloud Guard detects.")

[Trendline charts](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/cloud-guard/using/overview-trendlines.htm "The trendline charts in Cloud Guard show the change over time in the Security score number, the Problems snapshot total number, and total number reflected in Security recommendations.")

[Integration](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/cloud-guard/using/export-notifs-config.htm "Use the Events and Notifications services to send notifications, whenever Cloud Guard detects a problem for which you want to be notified.")

#### Detectors

[Activity detectors](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/cloud-guard/using/detect-recipes.htm#detect-recipes-ref-activity "Reference material for the Oracle-managed activity detector recipe that Cloud Guard provides is grouped below by resource type. Expand a Rule Display Name to view the details.")

[Configuration detectors](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/cloud-guard/using/detect-recipes.htm#detect-recipes-ref-config "Reference material for the Oracle-managed configuration detector recipes that Cloud Guard provides is grouped below by resource type. Expand a Rule Display Name to view the details.")

[Container Security Config detectors](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/cloud-guard/using/detect-recipes.htm#detect-recipes-ref-wlp "Reference material for the Oracle-managed Container Security Config detector recipes that Cloud Guard provides is grouped below by categories.")

[Instance Security detectors](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/cloud-guard/using/detect-recipes.htm#detect-recipes-ref-wlp-1 "Reference material for Oracle managed Instance Security detector recipes.")

[Threat detectors](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/cloud-guard/using/detect-recipes.htm#detect-recipes-ref-threat "Reference material for the Oracle-managed threat detector recipe that Cloud Guard provides.")

#### Developer Tools

[API for Cloud Guard](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/api/#/en/cloud-guard/latest/)

[OCI functions for notifications](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/cloud-guard/using/export-notifs-config.htm#export-notifs-functions "Use the Events and Notifications services to send notifications through Oracle Cloud Infrastructure Functions, wheneverCloud Guard detects a problem for which you want to be notified.")

[SDKs and the CLI](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/API/Concepts/sdks.htm)

[Cloud Shell](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/API/Concepts/devcloudshellintro.htm)

#### Troubleshooting

[Troubleshoot Cloud Guard](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/cloud-guard/using/trouble.htm "Use troubleshooting information to identify and address common issues that can occur while working with Oracle Cloud Guard service.")

#### Problem Handling

[Overview](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/cloud-guard/using/part-problems.htm "Learn about the different tools that Cloud Guard provides to process the problems that it reports.")

[Problem lifecycle](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/cloud-guard/using/problems-page-about.htm#problems-page-about__sect_prob_lifecycle)

[Problems page](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/cloud-guard/using/problems-page.htm "View, sort, and filter the list of problems detected. View details for individual problems, and take actions on problems individually or in groups.")

[Take actions](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/cloud-guard/using/problems-page-about.htm#problems-page-about__sect_take_actions)

[Resolve problems](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/cloud-guard/using/problems-page-resolve.htm "After you decide how to handle a particular problem detected by Cloud Guard, you can implement a resolution, mark the problem as resolved, and dismiss it.")

[Recommendations](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/cloud-guard/using/recommends-page.htm "Locate and resolve the highest priority problems that Cloud Guard has detected.")

#### Advanced Detection

[Queries](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/cloud-guard/using/queries.htm "Use Cloud Guard queries to get OS data that's relevant to your security needs.")

[Logging](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/cloud-guard/using/logging.htm "Understand the logs produced by Cloud Guard.")

[Threat Monitoring](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/cloud-guard/using/threats.htm "Cloud Guard can detect patterns of activity that indicate possible malicious attempts to gain access to resources in your environment and use them for corrupt purposes.")

#### Community

[Oracle Cloud Infrastructure blog](https://blogs.oracle.com/cloud-infrastructure/)

[Cloud infrastructure community forum](https://community.oracle.com/tech/apps-infra/categories/18430-cloud-infrastructure)

[Cloud Guard learning videos](https://www.youtube.com/playlist?list=PLKCk3OyNwIzuzfWoqvCg4ucrkSK0J-vBD)

# Vault

### [Oracle Cloud Infrastructure Documentation](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/home.htm)

* * *

All Pages

[Skip to main content](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/KeyManagement/home.htm#dcoc-content-body)

Vault
=====

OCI Vault lets you store and manage encryption keys to securely access resources.

[What's new](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/releasenotes/services/vault/)

#### Get Started

[Learn about key management](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/KeyManagement/Concepts/keyoverview.htm "The Key Management service stores and manages keys within vaults for secure access to resources.")

[Video: Overview](https://apexapps.oracle.com/pls/apex/f?p=44785:265:0:::265:P265_CONTENT_ID:31645)

[Video: Block Volume Encryption](https://apexapps.oracle.com/pls/apex/f?p=44785:265:0:::265:P265_CONTENT_ID:32506)

[Create your first vault](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/KeyManagement/Tasks/managingvaults_topic-To_create_a_new_vault.htm "Learn how to create a vault in OCI.")

[Product page](https://www.oracle.com/security/cloud-security/key-management/)

#### External Key Management

[Onboarding](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/KeyManagement/Tasks/ekms_onboarding.htm "Steps required to onboard a third-party key management system for integration with OCI External Key Management Service (EKMS).")

[Managing Key References](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/KeyManagement/Tasks/ekms_managing_key_references.htm "Learn how to manage key references in OCI External Key Management to external encryption keys created in a third-party key management system.")

[Using Key References](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/KeyManagement/Tasks/ekms_managing_cryptographic_operations.htm "Use key references for cryptographic operations with Thales.")

#### Support

[Support Requests](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/GSG/Tasks/contactingsupport.htm)

[Create a service request](https://support.oracle.com/)

#### Manage Vaults

[Create a vault](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/KeyManagement/Tasks/managingvaults_topic-To_create_a_new_vault.htm "Learn how to create a vault in OCI.")

[Back up a vault](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/KeyManagement/Tasks/backingupvaultsandkeys.htm "Learn about backing up and restoring virtual private vaults and master encryption keys in a Hardware Security Module (HSM).")

[Replicate a vault](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/KeyManagement/Tasks/replicatingvaults.htm "Replicate vaults and keys for disaster recovery scenarios. Replication helps in reading keys in a vault from a different region within the same realm.")

#### Developer Tools

[API and CLI for Vault](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/KeyManagement/Reference/developing-with-vault.htm "To perform tasks programmatically in Vault, use CLI references, API operation, and other developer resources.")

[SDKs and the CLI](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/API/Concepts/sdks.htm)

[Cloud Shell](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/API/Concepts/devcloudshellintro.htm)

#### Troubleshooting

[Troubleshoot Vault](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/KeyManagement/Concepts/troubleshootingvault.htm "Use the troubleshooting information to identify and address common issues that can occur while working with the Vault service.")

#### Manage Keys

[Create a key](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/KeyManagement/Tasks/managingkeys_topic-To_create_a_new_key.htm "Learn how to create a master encryption key in OCI's Key Management service.")

[Assign a key](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/KeyManagement/Tasks/assigningkeys.htm "Assign master encryption keys to supported resources and remove them when they are not needed anymore.")

[Use a key](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/KeyManagement/Tasks/usingkeys.htm "Use the vault master encryption keys for cryptographic operations.")

[Import a key or key version](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/KeyManagement/Tasks/importingkeys.htm "When you use imported key material, you remain responsible for the key material while allowing the Vault service to use a copy of it.")

[Export a key or key version](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/KeyManagement/Tasks/exportingkeys.htm "Learn how to export a software-protected master encryption key or key version for performing cryptographic operations.")

[Oracle Cloud Infrastructure blog](https://blogs.oracle.com/cloud-infrastructure/)

[Cloud Infrastructure community forum](https://forums.oracle.com/ords/apexds/domain/dev-community/category/15686-platform?tags=cloud-applications,cloud-infrastructure)

# Oracle WebLogic Server for Oracle Cloud Infrastructure - Get Started

[Go to main content](https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/paas/weblogic-cloud/index.html#)

 Search

 

*   [Help Center Home](https://docs.oracle.com/)
    

*   [Get Started](https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/paas/weblogic-cloud/index.html)
    
*   [What's New](https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/paas/weblogic-cloud/new/index.html)

*   [Videos](https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/paas/weblogic-cloud/videos.html)

![](https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/paas/weblogic-cloud/sp_common/shared-images/Java-cloud-270.png)

1.  [Home](https://docs.oracle.com/index.html)
    
2.  [Cloud](https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/index.html)
    
3.  [Cloud Platform](https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/paas/index.html)
    
4.  [WebLogic Server for Oracle Cloud Infrastructure](https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/paas/weblogic-cloud/index.html)
    

Oracle WebLogic Server for Oracle Cloud Infrastructure
======================================================

The Oracle WebLogic Server complete implementation of the Java Platform, Enterprise Edition (Java EE) specification provides a standard set of APIs for creating distributed Java applications that can access a wide variety of services, such as databases, messaging services, and connections to external enterprise systems.

Oracle WebLogic Server for OCI Stack Oracle WebLogic Server for OCI Image

Oracle WebLogic Server for Stack
--------------------------------

Quickly provision an Oracle WebLogic Server domain in Oracle Cloud Infrastructure (OCI) for developing and deploying Java Enterprise Edition (Java EE) applications.

### Start Here

*   [Learn about Oracle WebLogic Server for OCI](https://www.oracle.com/java/weblogic/weblogic-for-oracle-cloud-infrastructure/index.html)
    
*   [Learn about the Product Components](https://www.oracle.com/pls/topic/lookup?ctx=en/cloud/paas/weblogic-cloud&id=WLCUG-GUID-D5F1469F-C9C5-4A7C-99B6-8DA7F1C16F79)
    
*   [Create your first Domain](https://www.oracle.com/pls/topic/lookup?ctx=en/cloud/paas/weblogic-cloud&id=WLCGS)
    
    Related
    
    *   [Video](https://apexapps.oracle.com/pls/apex/f?p=44785:265:0::::P265_CONTENT_ID:28465)
        
    
*   [Oracle Cloud Cost Estimator](https://www.oracle.com/cloud/cost-estimator.html)
    

### Create

*   [Perform Prerequisite Tasks](https://www.oracle.com/pls/topic/lookup?ctx=en/cloud/paas/weblogic-cloud&id=WLCUG-GUID-C34C8258-5382-4386-8A38-4B5941CD3EB5)

### Manage

*   [Access the WebLogic Console](https://www.oracle.com/pls/topic/lookup?ctx=en/cloud/paas/weblogic-cloud&id=WLCUG-GUID-59CBD7D3-061F-4F9D-89EE-E8591D8D3F4F)
    
*   [Start and Stop a Domain](https://www.oracle.com/pls/topic/lookup?ctx=en/cloud/paas/weblogic-cloud&id=WLCUG-GUID-5DD381DA-CB02-48C0-A3C0-579673991FBD)
    
*   [Add IDCS after Creating a Domain](https://www.oracle.com/pls/topic/lookup?ctx=en/cloud/paas/weblogic-cloud&id=WLCUG-GUID-951A377C-BAE8-4746-A104-7F7C8B8E9E9B)
    
*   [Configure Session Persistence](https://www.oracle.com/pls/topic/lookup?ctx=en/cloud/paas/weblogic-cloud&id=WLCUG-GUID-DF90FD42-DC53-4867-8FE5-D6C54CAE90F4)

Oracle WebLogic Server for Image
--------------------------------

Create Oracle Cloud Infrastructure Compute instances with the entitlement to create Oracle WebLogic Server domains. Quickly install Oracle WebLogic Server using the WebLogic Server and Java Development Toolkit binaries provided on disk.

# OS Management Hub

### [Oracle Cloud Infrastructure Documentation](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/home.htm)

* * *

All Pages

[Skip to main content](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/osmh/doc/home.htm#dcoc-content-body)

OS Management Hub
=================

Oracle OS Management Hub (formerly OS Management) is the next-generation management solution for your operating system environments. Use a centralized management console to manage updates and patches for instances in OCI, private data centers, or supported third-party cloud environments.

[What's new](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/releasenotes/services/os-management-hub/)

#### Get Started

[Overview](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/osmh/doc/overview.htm#top "Oracle OS Management Hub is the next generation management solution for operating system environments. It provides a centralized management console to monitor and manage updates across your entire environment.")

[Get started](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/osmh/doc/getstarted.htm#top "Get started with OS Management Hub by ensuring service prerequisites are met before enabling the service and registering instances.")

[Supported environments](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/osmh/doc/getstarted.htm#supported-environments)

[Required IAM policies](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/osmh/doc/policies.htm#policies "IAM policies specify which users and services can access certain OCI resources. For OS Management Hub, you must identify the resources that the service can manage and which users can manage those resources.")

[Product page](https://www.oracle.com/linux/os-management/)

#### Register Instances

[Create a profile](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/osmh/doc/create-profile.htm#top "Create a profile in OS Management Hub to register an instance with the service.")

[Register an OCI instance](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/osmh/doc/register-oci-instance.htm#top "Register an OCI instance with OS Management Hub by enabling the Oracle Cloud Agent service plugin and selecting a profile.")

[Register on-premise or third-party instance](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/osmh/doc/register-non-oci-instance.htm#top "Register an on-premises or supported third-party cloud instance by installing necessary packages, adding a registration profile, and installing the Management Agent.")

[Register a station](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/osmh/doc/register-management-station.htm#top "Register a management station with OS Management Hub by using the profile for the management station and installing the Management Agent.")

#### Events

[List events](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/osmh/doc/service-events.htm#top "View a list of events detected on OS Management Hub instances within a particular compartment.")

[View event details](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/osmh/doc/service-events.htm#unique_1797443969 "View detailed information about an event in OS Management Hub.")

#### Training

[Overview video \[2 min\]](https://youtu.be/zBDX5VmurZM)

[Training Track](https://www.oracle.com/goto/osmhlearning)

#### Support

[Get help and contact Support](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/GSG/Tasks/contactingsupport.htm)

[Create a service request](https://support.oracle.com/epmos/faces/MosIndex.jspx)

#### Software Sources

[About software sources](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/osmh/doc/understand-software-sources.htm#understand-sources "OS Management Hub provides OS content to Oracle Linux instances using software sources (repositories).")

[Add vendor sources](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/osmh/doc/add-vendor-software-sources.htm#top "Select Oracle Linux software sources in OS Management Hub to make them available to instances in the region.")

[Add third-party or private sources](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/osmh/doc/add-third-party-source.htm#top "Add a yum repository from an independent software vendor (ISV), independent hardware vendor (IHV), or your private yum repository as a software source in OS Management Hub.")

[Create a custom source](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/osmh/doc/create-custom-software-source.htm#top "Select specific content from vendor software sources to create a custom software source in OS Management Hub.")

#### Groups

[About groups](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/osmh/doc/understand-groups.htm#understand-groups "Groups provide a way to manage and monitor a collection of instances in OS Management Hub. You can use groups to apply errata, update software, and schedule jobs for common content management tasks.")

[Create a group](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/osmh/doc/create-group.htm#top "Use groups to standardize and unify content across a set of instances in OS Management Hub.")

[Create a group profile](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/osmh/doc/create-profile-group.htm#top "Create a group profile to associate an instance with a group when registering with OS Management Hub.")

#### Dynamic Sets

[About dynamic sets](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/osmh/doc/understand-dynamic-sets.htm "Use dynamic sets to organize instances based on specific attributes within OS Management Hub. Dynamic sets let you manage specific segments of the environment, run update jobs, and run reports.")

[Create a dynamic set](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/osmh/doc/create-dynamic-set.htm "Define the matching rule conditions that identify which instances to include in the dynamic set for OS Management Hub.")

[Update a set's instances](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/osmh/doc/update-dynamic-set.htm#top "Create a job in OS Management Hub to update all the instances in the dynamic set. You can schedule the job to run immediately or at a future date and time. You can also schedule the update job to run as a recurring event.")

#### Lifecycle Environments

[About lifecycles](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/osmh/doc/understand-lifecycle.htm#understand-lifecycles "A lifecycle environment is a user-defined pipeline to deliver select, versioned content in an ordered manner.")

[Create a lifecycle](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/osmh/doc/create-lifecycle-environment.htm#top "Create a lifecycle environment to define the stages of a software's lifecycle in OS Management Hub. For example, you might have stages for Development, Test, Acceptance, and Production.")

[Create a versioned source](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/osmh/doc/create-custom-software-source.htm#top "Select specific content from vendor software sources to create a custom software source in OS Management Hub.")

[Promote content to a stage](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/osmh/doc/promote-content-lifecycle-stage.htm#top "Promote the content in a versioned custom software source in OS Management Hub to provide that content to all instances assigned to a lifecycle stage.")

#### Developer Tools

[API for OS Management Hub](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/api/#/en/osmh/latest/)

[CLI for OS Management Hub](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/tools/oci-cli/latest/oci_cli_docs/cmdref/os-management-hub.html)

[SDKs and the CLI](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/API/Concepts/sdks.htm)

[Cloud Shell](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/API/Concepts/devcloudshellintro.htm)

[Terraform](https://registry.terraform.io/providers/oracle/oci/latest/docs/resources/os_management_hub_event)

#### Reports

[Compartment-level report](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/osmh/doc/view-reports.htm#top "View a compartment-level summary of instances that are up-to-date on security vulnerability patches and bug fixes and their check-in status with OS Management Hub.")

[Security report](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/osmh/doc/view-security-report.htm#top "View the security updates report in OS Management Hub. This report indicates whether the instances are up-to-date on security patches and provides details about associated security advisories.")

[Vulnerability report](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/osmh/doc/view-vulnerability-report.htm#top "Use the vulnerability report in OS Management Hub to identify security vulnerabilities on an instance. The report lists security advisories, the corresponding CVEs, and the affected packages.")

#### Schedule Updates

[About update management](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/osmh/doc/updates.htm#updates "OS Management Hub lets you create one-time or recurring update jobs to update instances.")

[Update an instance](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/osmh/doc/create-scheduled-job-instance.htm#top "Create an update job to schedule updates for an instance in OS Management Hub. You can schedule the job to run immediately or at a future date and time. You can also schedule the update job to run as a recurring event.")

[Update a group](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/osmh/doc/create-scheduled-job-group.htm#top "Create a job in OS Management Hub to update all the instances in a group. You can schedule the job to run immediately or at a future date and time. You can also schedule the update job to run as a recurring event.")

[Update a compartment](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/osmh/doc/create-update-job-compartment.htm#top "Schedule a job to update all instances in a compartment in OS Management Hub. The instances can be a mix of OS versions, architectures, and locations.")

#### Troubleshooting

[Registration failures](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/osmh/doc/troubleshoot-instance-station-registration-failure.htm#troubleshoot-instance-station-registration-failure "An instance or management station can fail to register with OS Management Hub for several reasons.")

[Troubleshooting](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/osmh/doc/troubleshooting.htm#troubleshooting "Identify and address common issues that can occur while working with OS Management Hub.")

[Known Issues](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/osmh/doc/known-issues.htm#known-issues "These known issues have been identified in OS Management Hub.")

#### Community

[Cloud Customer Connect](https://community.oracle.com/customerconnect/)

[Oracle Cloud Infrastructure blog](https://blogs.oracle.com/cloud-infrastructure/)

[Cloud infrastructure community forum](https://community.oracle.com/tech/apps-infra/categories/18430-cloud-infrastructure)

[Linux cloud community](https://community.oracle.com/customerconnect/categories/oci-linux)

# Media Services

### [Oracle Cloud Infrastructure Documentation](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/home.htm)

* * *

All Pages

[Skip to main content](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/media-services/home.htm#dcoc-content-body)

Media Services
==============

Media Services is a fully managed service for processing media (video) source content. It provides scalable distribution and origination for just-in-time packaged adaptive bitrate (ABR) video content. Media Services includes two components, Media Flow and Media Streams, which can be used independently or together and operate on the content stored in Object Storage.

[What's new](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/releasenotes/services/media-services/)

#### Get Started

[Video: Media Flow overview](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=bJJL3SgQVow)

[Video: Media Streams overview](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=eHHoMta9nyY)

[Learn about Media Services](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/media-services/overview.htm "The Oracle Cloud Infrastructure (OCI) Media Services is a fully managed service for processing media (video) source content. It provides scalable distribution and origination for just-in-time packaged Adaptive Bitrate (ABR) video content. The OCI Media Services includes OCI Media Flow and OCI Media Streams. The services can be used independently or together and operate on the content stored in the OCI Object Store.")

[Get started with Media Services](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/media-services/get-started.htm "Get started with the Media Services.")

[IAM policies](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/media-services/iam-policies-media-services.htm "Create IAM policies to control who has access to Media Services resources, and to control the type of access for each group of users.")

#### Developer Tools

[API for Media Services](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/api/#/en/dms/latest/)

[CLI for Media Services](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/tools/oci-cli/latest/oci_cli_docs/cmdref/media-services.html)

[SDKs and the CLI](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/API/Concepts/sdks.htm)

[Cloud Shell](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/API/Concepts/devcloudshellintro.htm)

#### Manage Media Workflows

[Create a workflow](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/media-services/mediaflow/create-media-workflow.htm "Create a media workflow and configure the required tasks for the workflow in Media Flow.")

[Update a workflow](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/media-services/mediaflow/media-flow-renaming-editing-mf.htm "Rename a media workflow and update the tasks in Media Flow.")

[Delete a workflow](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/media-services/mediaflow/media-flow-deleting-mf.htm "Delete a media workflow using the Console, CLI, or API in Media Flow.")

#### Manage Media Flow Jobs

[Run a job](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/media-services/mediaflow/run-media-job.htm "Run an asynchronous Media Flow job using the Console, CLI, or API.")

[Monitor a job](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/media-services/mediaflow/media-flow-monitoring-a-job.htm "Check the lifecycle state on the job details page in Media Flow.")

[Cancel a job](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/media-services/mediaflow/media-flow-cancelling-jobs.htm "Cancel a Media Flow job that's not complete or terminated.")

#### Community

[Oracle Cloud Infrastructure blog](https://blogs.oracle.com/cloud-infrastructure/)

[Cloud infrastructure community forum](https://forums.oracle.com/ords/apexds/domain/dev-community/category/15686-platform?tags=cloud-applications,cloud-infrastructure)

#### Manage Distribution Channels

[Create a distribution channel](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/media-services/mediastreams/ms-create-dist-channel.htm "Create a distribution channel to distribute streamed content in Media Streams.")

[Configure the CDN](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/media-services/mediastreams/configure-cdn.htm "Configure the CDN for an existing distribution channel in Media Streams.")

[Create a packaging configuration](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/media-services/mediastreams/create-packaging-config.htm "Create a packaging configuration for HTTP Live Streaming (HLS) packaging of video content in Media Streams.")

[Ingest an asset](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/media-services/mediastreams/asset-ingestion.htm "Add the content that you want to distribute in the playlists in Media Streams.")

[Create a preview URL](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/media-services/mediastreams/create-preview-url.htm "Create a preview URL in Media Streams and view your content asset in a browser.")

[Play an asset](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/media-services/mediastreams/media-streams-med-serv-asset-playback.htm "View the generated preview URL for a content asset in Media Streams.")

#### Troubleshooting

[Troubleshoot Media Services](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/media-services/troubleshooting.htm "Use troubleshooting information to identify and address common issues that can occur while working with Media Services.")

#### Support

[Support Requests](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/GSG/Tasks/contactingsupport.htm)

[Create a service request](https://support.oracle.com/)

# Cluster Placement Groups

### [Oracle Cloud Infrastructure Documentation](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/home.htm)

* * *

All Pages

[Skip to main content](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/cluster-placement-groups/home.htm#dcoc-content-body)

Cluster Placement Groups
========================

The Cluster Placement Groups service lets you deploy resources into the same logical grouping, known as a cluster placement group, to ensure that they're placed physically near one another to support low-latency networking use cases.

[What's new](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/releasenotes/services/cluster-placement-groups/)

#### Get Started

[Learn about Cluster Placement Groups](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/cluster-placement-groups/overview.htm "Oracle Cloud Infrastructure Cluster Placement Groups lets you create resources in close proximity to one another to support low-latency networking use cases.")

[Secure Cluster Placement Groups](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/Security/Reference/clusterplacementgroups_security.htm)

#### Troubleshooting

[Troubleshoot problems](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/cluster-placement-groups/troubleshooting.htm "Use troubleshooting information to identify and address common issues that can occur while working with the Cluster Placement Groups service.")

#### Manage Cluster Placement Groups

[Create a cluster placement group](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/cluster-placement-groups/create-cluster-placement-group.htm "Create a cluster placement group in which you can colocate resources to support low-latency networking use cases.")

[Activate a cluster placement group](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/cluster-placement-groups/activate-cluster-placement-group.htm "Activate a deactivated cluster placement group to resume its use.")

[Update a cluster placement group](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/cluster-placement-groups/update-cluster-placement-group.htm "You can update the description of a cluster placement group.")

[Delete a cluster placement group](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/cluster-placement-groups/delete-cluster-placement-group.htm "Delete a cluster placement group if you no longer need it.")

#### Support

[Get help and contact support](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/GSG/Tasks/contactingsupport.htm)

[Create a service request](https://support.oracle.com/)

#### Developer Tools

[Cluster Placement Groups API](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/api/#/en/clusterplacementgroups/latest/)

[CLI for Cluster Placement Groups](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/tools/oci-cli/latest/oci_cli_docs/cmdref/cpg.html)

#### Community

[Oracle Cloud Infrastructure blog](https://blogs.oracle.com/cloud-infrastructure/)

[Cloud infrastructure community forum](https://forums.oracle.com/ords/apexds/domain/dev-community/category/15686-platform?tags=cloud-applications,cloud-infrastructure)

# Process Automation

### [Oracle Cloud Infrastructure Documentation](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/home.htm)

* * *

All Pages

[Skip to main content](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/process-automation/index.html#dcoc-content-body)

Process Automation
==================

Process Automation helps you to rapidly design, automate, and manage business processes in the cloud. With the Process Automation design-time (Designer) and the runtime (Workspace) environments, you can easily create, develop, manage, test, and monitor process applications and their components.

**Note:**

This page is for _administrators_ and contains information about _provisioning and administering an instance_. For all other documentation, see [Oracle Cloud Infrastructure Process Automation.](https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/paas/process-automation/index.html)

Get Started
-----------

*   [What's New](https://www.oracle.com/pls/topic/lookup?ctx=en/cloud/paas/process-automation&id=PRMWN-GUID-865813F9-297C-424E-A6AE-DC5FB75368F7)
    
*   [Get Started with Process Automation](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/process-automation/oci-process-automation/get-started.html)
    
*   [Before You Begin](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/process-automation/oci-process-automation/you-begin.html)
    
*   [Set Up Users, Groups, and Policies](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/process-automation/oci-process-automation/set-users-groups-and-policies.html)
    

Related Content
---------------

*   [Provision and Manage Instances](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/process-automation/oci-process-automation/provision-and-manage-oracle-cloud-infrastructure-process-automation-instances.html)
    
*   [Monitor Instances](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/process-automation/oci-process-automation/monitor-oracle-cloud-infrastructure-process-automation.html)
    
*   [REST API](https://www.oracle.com/pls/topic/lookup?ctx=process-automation&id=PROCA)
    
*   [Service Instance REST API Reference](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/api/#/en/opa/20210621/)
    

More Resources
--------------

# Queue

### [Oracle Cloud Infrastructure Documentation](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/home.htm)

* * *

All Pages

[Skip to main content](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/queue/home.htm#dcoc-content-body)

Queue
=====

Oracle Cloud Infrastructure (OCI) Queue is a fully managed serverless service that helps decouple systems and enable asynchronous operations. Queue handles high-volume transactional data that requires independently processed messages without loss or duplication.

[What's new](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/releasenotes/)

#### Get Started

[Overview of Queue](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/queue/overview.htm#overview "Oracle Cloud Infrastructure (OCI) Queue is a fully managed serverless service that helps decouple systems and enable asynchronous operations. Queue handles high-volume transactional data that requires independently processed messages without loss or duplication. Queue supports transparent, automatic scaling based on throughput for producers and consumers. Queue uses open standards to support communication with any client or producer with minimal effort.")

[Queue policies](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/queue/policy-reference.htm#policy-reference "Use the Oracle Cloud Infrastructure Identity and Access Management (IAM) service to create policies for your queues.")

[Queue product page](http://oracle.com/cloud/cloud-native/queue/)

#### Support

[Support Requests](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/GSG/Tasks/contactingsupport.htm)

[Create a service request](https://support.oracle.com/)

#### Queue Management

[Create a queue](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/queue/queue-create.htm#top "Create a queue.")

[Get queue details](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/queue/queue-details.htm#top "Retrieve queue details.")

[Edit a queue](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/queue/queue-update.htm#top "Update a queue.")

[Delete a queue](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/queue/queue-delete.htm#top "Delete a queue.")

Web Application Firewall
========================

Use Web Application Firewall (WAF) to protect applications from malicious and unwanted internet traffic. WAF can protect any internet facing endpoint, providing consistent rule enforcement across a customer's application.

[What's new](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/releasenotes/services/web-application-firewall/)

#### Get Started

Network Load Balancer
=====================

Use Network Load Balancer to provide automated traffic distribution from one entry point to multiple servers in a backend set. Network load balancers ensure that your services remain available by directing traffic only to healthy servers based on Layer 3/Layer 4 (IP protocol) data.

[What's new](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/releasenotes/services/network-load-balancer/)

#### Get Started

[Introducing the Network Load Balancer service](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/NetworkLoadBalancer/introduction.htm "Learn about how network load balancers can provide automated traffic distribution from one entry point to multiple servers in a backend set.")

[Learn network load balancer types](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/NetworkLoadBalancer/introduction.htm#NetworkLoadBalancerTypes)

[Understand network load balancer policies](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/NetworkLoadBalancer/introduction.htm#Policies)

[Product page](https://www.oracle.com/cloud/networking/load-balancing/)

#### Health Check Policies

[Review health status indicators](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/NetworkLoadBalancer/HealthCheckPolicies/health-check-policy-management.htm#HeathStatusIndicators)

# Vulnerability Scanning

### [Oracle Cloud Infrastructure Documentation](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/home.htm)

Vulnerability Scanning
======================

Oracle Cloud Infrastructure Vulnerability Scanning Service helps improve your security posture by routinely checking hosts and container images for potential vulnerabilities. The service gives developers, operations, and security administrators comprehensive visibility into misconfigured or vulnerable resources, and generates reports with metrics and details about these vulnerabilities including remediation information.

[What's new](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/releasenotes/services/scanning/)

#### Get Started

[Learn about Scanning](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/scanning/using/overview.htm "Oracle Cloud Infrastructure Vulnerability Scanning Service helps improve your security posture by routinely checking hosts and container images for potential vulnerabilities. The service gives developers, operations, and security administrators comprehensive visibility into misconfigured or vulnerable resources, and generates reports with metrics and details about these vulnerabilities including remediation information.")

[Video: Overview](https://apexapps.oracle.com/pls/apex/f?p=44785:265:0:::265:P265_CONTENT_ID:32231)

[Get started with Scanning](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/scanning/using/overview.htm#get_started "Use the Vulnerability Scanning service to check for security vulnerabilities in the Compute instances for a single compartment.")

[Product page](https://www.oracle.com/security/cloud-security/vulnerability-scanning-service/)

#### Reports

[View host scans](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/scanning/using/view-host-scan-report.htm "List host (agent scans, port scans, and CIS benchmark scans) scans.")

[View port scans](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/scanning/using/port-scan.htm "Oracle Cloud Infrastructure Vulnerability Scanning Service helps identify ports in your Compute instances that are unintentionally left open. Open ports might be a potential attack vector to your cloud resources or enable hackers to exploit other vulnerabilities.")

[View container image scans](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/scanning/using/image-scan.htm "View container image scans in Oracle Cloud Infrastructure Vulnerability Scanning Service to identify security vulnerabilities in your Container Registry images, like critical OS patches.")

[View benchmarks](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/scanning/using/view-host-scan-report-benchmarks.htm "View the results of CIS benchmark testing on a specific Compute instance.")

[View vulnerabilities](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/scanning/using/host-vulnerabilities-reports.htm "Oracle Cloud Infrastructure Vulnerability Scanning Service scans your targets based on the schedule and scanning properties in the recipe assigned to each target. Use vulnerabilities reports to identify security issues in your targets like critical OS patches.")
