# Overview of Compliance Documents

### [Oracle Cloud Infrastructure Documentation](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/home.htm)

* * *

All Pages

[Skip to main content](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/ComplianceDocuments/Concepts/compliancedocsoverview.htm#dcoc-content-body)

Overview of Compliance Documents
================================

The Compliance Documents service lets you view and download compliance documents.

**Note**  
  
Compliance Documents is available in Oracle US Government Cloud, Oracle US Defense Cloud, and Oracle UK Sovereign Cloud.

Types of Compliance Documents
-----------------------------

When viewing compliance documents, you can filter on the following types:

*   **Attestation.** A Payment Card Industry (PCI) Data Security Standard (DSS) Attestation of Compliance document.
*   **Audit.** A general audit report.
*   **Bridge Letter.** A bridge letter. Bridge letters provide compliance information for the period of time between the end date of an SOC report and the date of the release of a new SOC report.
*   **Certificate.** A document indicating certification by a particular authority, with regard to certification requirements and examination results conforming to said requirements.
*   **SOC3.** A Service Organization Controls 3 audit report that provides information relating to a service organization's internal controls for security, availability, confidentiality, and privacy.
*   **Other.** A compliance document that doesn't fit into any of the preceding, more specific categories.

If you need to further narrow down what documents are displayed, you can combine the type filter with the service filter.

Types of Services
-----------------

The services, environments, or business pillars or platforms, to which the documents belong include:

*   **OCI.** Oracle Cloud Infrastructure is a set of complementary cloud infrastructure services that let you build and run applications and services in a highly available hosted environment.
*   **PAAS.** Oracle Platform as a Service (PaaS) provides various platforms to build and deploy applications within the public, private, or hybrid cloud.
*   **SAAS Services.** Oracle Software as a service (SaaS) is a cloud-based software delivery model in which Oracle develops and maintains cloud application software. To view and download documents, users must be subscribed to the supported SaaS services in their tenancy. See the service documentation for information on whether the service is supported.
    

Regions and Availability Domains
--------------------------------

You can use the Compliance Documents service in all regions. For a list of supported regions, see [Regions and Availability Domains](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/General/Concepts/regions.htm)
.

Ways to Access Oracle Cloud Infrastructure
------------------------------------------

You can access Oracle Cloud Infrastructure (OCI) by using the [Console](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/GSG/Tasks/signingin_topic-Signing_In_for_the_First_Time.htm)
 (a browser-based interface), [REST API](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/API/Concepts/usingapi.htm)
, or [OCI CLI](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/API/Concepts/cliconcepts.htm)
. Instructions for using the Console, API, and CLI are included in topics throughout this documentation. For a list of available SDKs, see [Software Development Kits and Command Line Interface](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/API/Concepts/sdks.htm)
.

You cannot specifically access Compliance Documents by using the API or Command Line Interface (CLI). Compliance Documents does not have public API, SDK, or CLI support at this time. To go to the **Console** sign-in page, open the navigation menu at the top of this page and click **Infrastructure Console**. You are prompted to enter your cloud tenant, your user name, and your password. Open the **navigation menu** , select **Identity & Security**, and then select **Compliance**.

*   [Overview of Compliance Documents](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/ComplianceDocuments/Concepts/compliancedocsoverview.htm#Overview_of_Compliance_Documents)
    
*   [Types of Compliance Documents](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/ComplianceDocuments/Concepts/compliancedocsoverview.htm#doctypes)
    
*   [Types of Services](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/ComplianceDocuments/Concepts/compliancedocsoverview.htm#envtypes)
    
*   [Regions and Availability Domains](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/ComplianceDocuments/Concepts/compliancedocsoverview.htm#regions)
    
*   [Ways to Access Oracle Cloud Infrastructure](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/ComplianceDocuments/Concepts/compliancedocsoverview.htm#Ways_to_Access_Oracle_Cloud_Infrastructure)

---

# Interconnect for AWS (Limited Availability)

### [Oracle Cloud Infrastructure Documentation](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/home.htm)

* * *

All Pages

[Skip to main content](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/multicloud/interconnect-aws.htm#dcoc-content-body)

Interconnect for AWS (Limited Availability)
===========================================

**Important**  
  

**Pre-General Availability: 2026-05-15**

This documentation is in pre-General Availability status and is intended for demonstration and preliminary use only. It may not be specific to the hardware on which you are using the software. Oracle Corporation and its affiliates are not responsible for and expressly disclaim all warranties of any kind with respect to this documentation and will not be responsible for any loss, costs, or damages incurred due to the use of this documentation.

This documentation is not a commitment by Oracle to deliver any material, code, functionality or services. This documentation, and Oracle Pre-GA programs and services are subject to change at any time without notice and, accordingly, should not be relied upon in making purchasing decisions. The development, release, and timing of any features or functionality for Oracle’s Pre-GA programs and services remains at the sole discretion of Oracle. All release dates or other predictions of future events are subject to change. The future availability of any future Oracle program or service should not be relied on in entering into any license or service agreement with Oracle.

See [Oracle Legal Notices](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/legalnotices.htm)
.

**Note**  
  

Oracle Interconnect for AWS is currently in limited availability.

Limited availability is intended for testing only. We don't recommend production workloads during limited availability.

All connections will be deleted at the end of the limited availability period.

Oracle Interconnect for AWS is a managed dedicated connectivity service that allows you to provision secure, private cross-cloud connections directly between Oracle Cloud Infrastructure (OCI) and AWS in specific regions listed under the Availability section. This connection lets you set up cloud-to-cloud workloads without the traffic between the clouds going over the internet or through third-party providers. This topic describes how to set up virtual networking infrastructure resources to enable this deployment.

Highlights
----------

*   Fully managed, private interconnect solution powered by FastConnect. OCI and AWS configures and manages all BGP routing exchange, encryption, redundancy, and network isolation.
*   Fast, on-demand provisioning. Provision an interconnect virtual circuit within minutes over preconfigured cross-connects.
*   Built-in redundancy. Each interconnect virtual circuit maps across multiple redundant FastConnect devices and two separate FastConnect locations.
*   All traffic between OCI FastConnect devices and AWS network devices is encrypted using industry-standard MACsec (IEEE standard 802.1AE).
*   You can connect an Oracle Cloud Infrastructure (OCI) [virtual cloud network (VCN)](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/Network/Tasks/VCNs.htm)
     with an [Amazon virtual private cloud (VPC)](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/vpc/latest/userguide/what-is-amazon-vpc.html)
     and run a cloud-to-cloud workload. A sample use case could be that you have AI training data and applications in AWS which would like to take advantage of the GPU infrastructure and capacity in OCI.

Availability
------------

Oracle Interconnect for AWS is only available in the following paired regions.

### North America (NA)

| OCI region | AWS region |
| --- | --- |
| US East (Ashburn) (`us-ashburn-1`) - `IAD` | US East (N. Virginia) (`us-east-1`) |

Oracle Interconnect for AWS Architecture
----------------------------------------

Oracle Interconnect for AWS is automatically configured to provide device and location redundancy. All provisioned Oracle Interconnect for AWS virtual circuits map to redundant FastConnect devices across two separate FastConnect locations for an OCI region. The following diagram illustrates the high-level physical connectivity between OCI and AWS.

[![High-level physical connectivity between OCI and AWS.](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/multicloud/images/aws-interconnect-architecture.png "Click to expand")](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/multicloud/images/aws-interconnect-architecture.png)

Interconnect virtual circuit traffic is encrypted using industry-standard MACsec (IEEE standard 802.1AE) when routed between the OCI FastConnect devices and AWS network devices.

All redundancy, routing, and encryption for your interconnect is represented by a single virtual circuit in your OCI tenancy. The interconnect virtual circuit is attached to your [Dynamic Routing Gateway (DRG)](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/Network/Tasks/managingDRGs.htm)
 using a standard virtual circuit DRG attachment. Similar to other DRG attachments, use DRG route tables and import route distributions to influence the subnet CIDRs advertised to AWS and control which attachments have access to your interconnect virtual circuit. To learn more, see [Dynamic Routing Gateways](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/Network/Tasks/managingDRGs.htm)
.

Overview of Supported Traffic
-----------------------------

Here are more details about the supported types of traffic.

### VCN-to-VPC Connection: Extension from One Cloud to Another

You can connect a VCN and VPC so that traffic that uses private IP addresses goes over a cloud-to-cloud connection.

For example, the following diagram shows a VCN connected to a VPC. The traffic between the OCI VCNs and AWS VPCs uses a logical circuit that runs on the cloud-to-cloud connection between AWS and OCI.

[![Connection between AWS VPCs and OCI VCN.](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/multicloud/images/aws-vcn-vpc.png "Click to expand")](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/multicloud/images/aws-vcn-vpc.png)

To enable the connection between the VCN and VPC, you setup an OCI FastConnect interconnect virtual circuit and an AWS interconnect. The resulting connection automatically has built-in location redundancy, with no requirement to set up additional connections to enable a highly available, resilient network.

### Peered VCNs

The connection traffic can flow from one or more VPCs to one or more peered VCNs in the same OCI region or in other OCI regions through the OCI global backbone.

### Types of Traffic Not Supported by the Connection

This connection doesn't enable traffic between an on-premises network through OCI to the VPC, or from an on-premises network through AWS to OCI.

### Controlling Traffic Flow Over the Connection

Even if a connection has been established between VCN and VPC, you can control the packet flow over the connection with a combination of DRG route tables and VCN route tables. For example, you can restrict traffic to only specific subnets in the VPC.

Without terminating the connection, you can stop traffic flow to the VPC by removing route rules that direct traffic from the VCN to the VPC. You can also effectively stop the traffic by removing any security rules that enable VPC ingress or egress traffic. This action doesn't stop traffic flowing over the connection, but stops it at the VNIC level.

### Using Default Security List Rules with the VCN

If the VCN's subnets use the [default security list](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/Network/Concepts/securitylists.htm#Default)
 with the default rules, two rules in that list allow ingress traffic from anywhere (`0.0.0.0/0`, and thus the VPC):

*   Stateful ingress rule that allows traffic on TCP port 22 (SSH) from `0.0.0.0/0` and any source port
*   Stateful ingress rule that allows traffic on ICMP type 3, code 4 from `0.0.0.0/0` and any source port

Evaluate these rules and whether additional rules are required to enable traffic flow between VCN and VPC. Ensure that all allowed inbound or outbund traffic is intended or expected and defined.

Setting Up Oracle Interconnect for AWS
--------------------------------------

This section describes how to set up Oracle Interconnect for AWS.

The Amazon Web Services side of this connection uses what AWS refers to as "AWS Interconnect." The OCI side uses the FastConnect interconnect virtual circuit.

### Prerequisites: Resources You Need

You must have:

*   An AWS VPC with subnets, a Direct Connect gateway.
*   An OCI [VCN with subnets](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/Network/Tasks/VCNs.htm)
     and an attached [dynamic routing gateway (DRG)](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/Network/Tasks/managingDRGs.htm)
    . Remember to [attach the DRG](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/Network/Tasks/attach-vcn-drg.htm)
     to the VCN after you create it. If you already have Site-to-Site VPN or FastConnect between an on-premises network and VCN, then the VCN already has an attached DRG. You can use that same DRG here when setting up Oracle Interconnect for AWS.
*   IAM permissions to configure the resources needed for the required OCI components.
*   A valid subscription in both OCI and AWS for the regions you want to connect.
*   Note your AWS account ID or your [OCI tenancy OCID](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/GSG/Tasks/contactingsupport_topic-Locating_Oracle_Cloud_Infrastructure_IDs.htm)
    .

### Prerequisites: Required IAM Policy

You must already have the necessary AWS access and Oracle Cloud Infrastructure IAM access to create and work with the relevant AWS and Oracle networking resources. If your user account is in the [Administrators group](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/Identity/Concepts/overview.htm#The)
, you already have the required authority. Otherwise, this policy covers all the Networking resources:

    Allow group NetworkAdmins to manage virtual-network-family in tenancy

To only create and manage a virtual circuit, you must have a policy such as this:

    Allow group VirtualCircuitAdmins to manage drgs in tenancy
    Allow group VirtualCircuitAdmins to manage virtual-circuits in tenancy

For more information, see [IAM Policies for Networking](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/Network/Concepts/overview.htm#Policies)
.

### Provisioning Overview

The overall provisioning process requires configuration steps in both OCI and AWS consoles. Creation of a new Oracle Interconnect for AWS virtual circuit can be started from the OCI console and completed in the AWS console. Alternatively, begin in the AWS console, then complete the configuration in OCI.

Depending on your starting console, you're asked for either your AWS account ID or your OCI tenancy OCID for the remote cloud provider. After you initially create a new interconnect virtual circuit, a service key is generated. Use the provided service key to approve the request when you complete the configuration in the remaining console.

After you complete the configuration, OCI and AWS proceeds with the full provisioning process. The Oracle Interconnect for AWS virtual circuit attaches to your OCI dynamic routing gateway and AWS Direct Connect gateway. BGP sessions automatically establish and the contents of the DRG route table associated with your interconnect virtual circuit are advertised to AWS.

### Option 1: Start from OCI Console

Create an interconnect virtual circuit in the OCI console for a connection to AWS.

This section details the configuration process if you start the configuration for OCI Interconnect for AWS in the OCI console and complete the configuration in the AWS console.

#### Task 1: Setup OCI Interconnect for AWS virtual circuit

1.  On the **FastConnect connections** list page, select **Create FastConnect**. If you need help finding the list page, see [To list virtual circuits](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/multicloud/interconnect-aws.htm#vc-list)
    .
2.  Select **FastConnect interconnect** to configure the OCI Interconnect for AWS.
3.  Select **Next**.
4.  Enter the following for the virtual circuit:
    *   **Name**: A descriptive name for the virtual circuit. The value doesn't need to be unique across virtual circuits, and you can change it later. Avoid entering confidential information.
    *   **Compartment**: Leave as is (the compartment you're working in) or select a different compartment for this resource.
    *   **Select the cloud provider to initiate your interconnect configuration**: Select **Configure in OCI first**.
    *   **AWS account ID**: Input your AWS account ID for the AWS end of the OCI Interconnect for AWS to attach to. See, [Viewing your AWS account ID](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/IAM/latest/UserGuide/console-account-id.html)
        .
    *   **Dynamic Routing Gateway**: Select the DRG to route the virtual circuit traffic to. This DRG could be attached to several VCNs or to other DRGs with attached VCNs.
    *   **Provisioned Bandwidth**: Select a value. If the bandwidth needs to be modified later, you can update the virtual circuit to use a different value (see [To edit a virtual circuit](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/multicloud/interconnect-aws.htm#vc-edit)
        ).
5.  After inputting all the required values in Step 4, select **Next**.
6.  On the following **Review and create** page, verify all configuration, then select **Create**.
    
    The virtual circuit is created and displays a status page. Select **Close** to return to the list of virtual circuits.
    

Until you complete the next task in the AWS console, the virtual circuit's Lifecycle State is PENDING PARTNER and the BGP state is DOWN.

#### Task 2: Complete the AWS end of the virtual circuit

1.  Browse to the details page for the interconnect virtual circuit. If you need help finding the list page, see [To list virtual circuits](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/multicloud/interconnect-aws.htm#vc-list)
    .
2.  In the banner at the top of the virtual circuit details page, select **Copy activation key**.
    
    The key is copied to your clipboard.
    
3.  Select **AWS portal** to open a link to the AWS console.
4.  Complete the OCI Interconnect for AWS configuration in the AWS console.
    
    See [Accepting a new multicloud Interconnect created from another CSP using the AWS Console](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/interconnect/latest/userguide/getting-started-multicloud.html#accept-interconnect)
    .
    

After the AWS side configuration is completed, the Lifecycle State switches to PROVISIONING, then PROVISIONED when completed. When the BGP session is established and working, the BGP state changes to UP.

### Option 2: Start from AWS Console

This section details the configuration process if you start the configuration for OCI Interconnect for AWS in the AWS console and complete the configuration in the OCI console.

#### Task 1: Begin configuration in the AWS console

If you haven't already, complete your configuration in the AWS console by following the instructions for [Create your first multicloud Interconnect starting from the AWS Console](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/interconnect/latest/userguide/getting-started-multicloud.html#create-first-interconnect)
.

Copy the activation key provided to you by AWS. This key is required for completing Task 2 in this section.

#### Task 2: Complete the OCI Interconnect for AWS configuration in the OCI console

1.  On the **FastConnect connections** list page, select **Create FastConnect**. If you need help finding the list page, see [To list virtual circuits](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/multicloud/interconnect-aws.htm#vc-list)
    .
2.  Select **FastConnect interconnect** to configure the OCI Interconnect for AWS.
3.  Select **Next**.
4.  Enter the following for the virtual circuit:
    *   **Name**: A descriptive name for the virtual circuit. The value doesn't need to be unique across virtual circuits, and you can change it later. Avoid entering confidential information.
    *   **Compartment**: Leave as is (the compartment you're working in) or select a different compartment for this resource.
    *   **Select the cloud provider to initiate your interconnect configuration**: Select **Configured in AWS first**.
    *   **Service key**: Input the key provided by AWS in Task 1. AWS calls this the activation key.
    *   **Dynamic Routing Gateway**: Select the DRG to route the virtual circuit traffic to. This DRG could be attached to several VCNs or to other DRGs with attached VCNs.
5.  After inputting all the required values in Step 4, select **Next**.
6.  On the following **Review and create** page, verify all configuration, then select **Create**.
    
    The virtual circuit is created and displays a status page. Select **Close** to return to the list of virtual circuits.
    

The configured bandwidth of your interconnect virtual circuit is automatically inherited from the AWS side configuration.

After the OCI side of the configuration is completed, the Lifecycle State switches to PROVISIONING. After several minutes, the Lifecycle State changes to PROVISIONED when completed. When the BGP session is established and working, the BGP state changes to UP.

Managing a Virtual Circuit
--------------------------

You can perform the following tasks with virtual circuits.

*   [To list virtual circuits](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/multicloud/interconnect-aws.htm#vc-list)
    
*   [To edit a virtual circuit](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/multicloud/interconnect-aws.htm#vc-edit)
    
*   [To deactivate and activate a virtual circuit](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/multicloud/interconnect-aws.htm#vc-deactivate)
    
*   [To terminate a virtual circuit](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/multicloud/interconnect-aws.htm#vc-terminate)
    

### To list virtual circuits

1.  Open the **navigation menu**  and select **Networking**. Under **Customer connectivity**, select **FastConnect**.
    
    The **FastConnect connections** list page opens. All virtual circuits in the selected compartment are displayed in a table.
    
2.  To view the connections in a different compartment, use the **Compartment** filter to switch compartments.
    
    You must have permission to work in a compartment to see the resources in it. If you're not sure which compartment to use, contact an administrator. For more information, see [Understanding Compartments](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/GSG/Concepts/settinguptenancy.htm#Understa)
    .
    

### To edit a virtual circuit

You can change these items for a virtual circuit:

*   The name
*   The bandwidth.
    
    **Note**  
      
    Only 1G virtual circuits are available for limited availability. Changing the bandwidth during limited availability isn't supported.
    
*   Which DRG it uses
*   [Tags](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/Tagging/home.htm)
    

**Note**  
  
Changing the DRG for a virtual circuit while it's already in the PROVISIONED state causes an outage.

1.  On the **FastConnect connections** list page, select the virtual circuit that you want to work with. If you need help finding the list page, see [To list virtual circuits](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/multicloud/interconnect-aws.htm#vc-list)
    .
2.  Select **Edit** and make changes. Avoid entering confidential information.
3.  Select **Update**.

### To deactivate and activate a virtual circuit

**Note**  
  
This action is provided so you can test failover of redundant virtual circuits. The BGP session between on-premises and OCI networks is temporarily shut down (this isn't a "graceful shutdown") and resumes quickly after failover is validated.

1.  On the **FastConnect connections** list page, select the virtual circuit that you want to work with. If you need help finding the list page, see [To list virtual circuits](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/multicloud/interconnect-aws.htm#vc-list)
    .
2.  Select **Actions** and then select **Deactivate**.
3.  To enable BGP again, select the **Actions** and then select **Activate**.

### To terminate a virtual circuit

**Note**  
  
Terminating your OCI Interconnect for AWS virtual circuit can be initiated from either the OCI or AWS console. The resource is deleted in the remote cloud automatically.

1.  On the **FastConnect connections** list page, select the virtual circuit that you want to work with. If you need help finding the list page, see [To list virtual circuits](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/multicloud/interconnect-aws.htm#vc-list)
    .
2.  From the **Actions** menu (three dots) for the virtual circuit, select **Delete**.
3.  When prompted, confirm the deletion.

The virtual circuit's Lifecycle State changes to TERMINATING and then to TERMINATED.

*   [Interconnect for AWS (Limited Availability)](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/multicloud/interconnect-aws.htm#top)
    
*   [Highlights](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/multicloud/interconnect-aws.htm#highlights)
    
*   [Availability](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/multicloud/interconnect-aws.htm#availability)
    
*   [Oracle Interconnect for AWS Architecture](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/multicloud/interconnect-aws.htm#architecture)
    
*   [Overview of Supported Traffic](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/multicloud/interconnect-aws.htm#supported-traffic)
    
*   [Setting Up Oracle Interconnect for AWS](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/multicloud/interconnect-aws.htm#setup)
    
*   [Managing a Virtual Circuit](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/multicloud/interconnect-aws.htm#manage)

# Security Zones

### [Oracle Cloud Infrastructure Documentation](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/home.htm)

* * *

All Pages

[Skip to main content](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/security-zone/home.htm#dcoc-content-body)

Security Zones
==============

Security Zones let you be confident that your resources in Oracle Cloud Infrastructure, including Compute, Networking, Object Storage, and Database resources, comply with your security policies.

[What's new](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/releasenotes/services/security-zone/)

#### Get Started

[Learn about zones](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/security-zone/using/security-zones.htm "Security Zones let you be confident that your resources in Oracle Cloud Infrastructure, including Compute, Networking, Object Storage, Block Volume and Database resources, comply with your security policies.")

[Video: Overview](https://youtu.be/vtQPi-Ko-xQ?si=kUt4-vbLYaUYmCoZ)

[Create your first zone](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/security-zone/using/get-started.htm "After creating IAM policies and enabling Cloud Guard, create a security zone for a compartment and check for any security zone policy violations.")

[Create IAM policies](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/security-zone/using/managing-security-zones.htm#required-iam-policy "To use Oracle Cloud Infrastructure, you must be granted the required type of access in an IAM policy written by an administrator, whether you're using the Console or the REST API with an SDK, CLI, or other tool.")

[Product page](https://www.oracle.com/security/cloud-security/security-zones/)

#### Manage Zones

[Create a zone](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/security-zone/using/create-security-zone.htm "Create a security zone in a compartment to ensure that the resources in a compartment comply with security policies.")

[Edit a zone](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/security-zone/using/edit-security-zone.htm "Edit the description or select a different recipe for a security zone. You can't change the name of a security zone.")

[View policy violations](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/security-zone/using/view-policy-violations.htm "If the compartment for the security zone has any existing resources, you can use the Console to identify the resources that violate the security zone's policies, and take corrective actions.")

[Remove a child compartment](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/security-zone/using/remove-compartment-from-zone.htm "When you remove a subcompartment from a security zone, Oracle Cloud Infrastructure no longer enforces security zone policies on the resources in the subcompartment.")

#### Support

[Support Requests](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/GSG/Tasks/contactingsupport.htm)

[Create a service request](https://support.oracle.com/)

#### Recipes and Policies

[Learn about zone policies](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/security-zone/using/security-zone-policies.htm "When you create and update resources in a security zone, Oracle Cloud Infrastructure validates these operations against the policies in the security zone. If any policy is violated, then the operation is denied.")

[View recipes and policies](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/security-zone/using/get-recipe.htm "View the details for a security zone recipe, including what policies are in the recipe.")

[Create a recipe](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/security-zone/using/create-recipe.htm "Create a recipe for a security zone.")

[Clone a recipe](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/security-zone/using/clone-recipe.htm "Create a security zone recipe by cloning an existing one.")

#### Developer Tools

[API for Security Zones](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/api/#/en/cloud-guard/)

[CLI for Security Zones](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/tools/oci-cli/latest/oci_cli_docs/cmdref/cloud-guard.html)

[SDKs and the CLI](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/API/Concepts/sdks.htm)

[Cloud Shell](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/API/Concepts/devcloudshellintro.htm)

#### Use Cases

[Reference architecture](https://docs.oracle.com/en/solutions/oci-security-zones/index.html)

#### Troubleshooting

[Troubleshoot Security Zones](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/security-zone/using/troubleshooting.htm "Use troubleshooting information to identify and address common issues that can occur while working with Security Zones.")

#### Community

[Cloud Security blog](https://blogs.oracle.com/cloudsecurity/)

[Cloud infrastructure community forum](https://forums.oracle.com/ords/apexds/domain/dev-community/category/15686-platform?tags=cloud-applications,cloud-infrastructure)

[Security Zones learning videos](https://www.youtube.com/playlist?list=PLKCk3OyNwIzs4-WaQgkgEo9lrnrHbZxmU)

# Notifications

### [Oracle Cloud Infrastructure Documentation](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/home.htm)

* * *

All Pages

[Skip to main content](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Notification/home.htm#dcoc-content-body)

Notifications
=============

The Notifications service lets you know when something happens with your resources in Oracle Cloud Infrastructure. Using alarms, event rules, and connectors, you can get human-readable messages through supported endpoints, including email and text messages (SMS). You can also automate tasks through custom HTTPS endpoints and Oracle Cloud Infrastructure Functions.

[What's new](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/releasenotes/services/notifications/)

#### Get Started

[Video: Overview](https://apexapps.oracle.com/pls/apex/f?p=44785:265:0:::265:P265_CONTENT_ID:31657)

[Learn about subscriptions and topics](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Notification/Concepts/notificationoverview.htm#hownw)

[Best practices for subscriptions and topics](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Notification/Concepts/notificationbestpractices.htm "Review best practices for subscriptions and topics used with Oracle Cloud Infrastructure Notifications.")

[Secure Notifications](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/Security/Reference/notifications_security.htm)

[Product page](https://www.oracle.com/devops/notifications/)

#### Developer Tools

[API for Notifications](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/api/#/en/notification/latest/)

[Command Line Reference for Notifications](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/tools/oci-cli/latest/oci_cli_docs/cmdref/ons.html)

[SDKs and the CLI](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/API/Concepts/sdks.htm)

[Cloud Shell](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/API/Concepts/devcloudshellintro.htm)

#### Manage Subscriptions and Topics

[Create a topic](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Notification/Tasks/create-topic.htm "Create a topic in Notifications.")

[Create a subscription](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Notification/Tasks/create-subscription.htm "Create a subscription in Notifications to receive notifications.")

[Create an email subscription](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Notification/Tasks/create-subscription-email.htm "Create an email subscription in Notifications to receive notifications by email. An email subscription can reference any email address, including an alias for a distribution list.")

[Confirm a new subscription](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Notification/Tasks/confirm-subscription.htm "Confirm a subscription in Notifications. Confirmation isn't required for function subscriptions.")

[Integrate notifications with PagerDuty](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Notification/Tasks/pagerduty.htm "Learn how to integrate Oracle Cloud Infrastructure Notifications with PagerDuty so you can trigger PagerDuty incidents.")

#### Troubleshooting

[HTTPS (Custom URL) subscription confirmations](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Notification/Tasks/troubleshootingnotifications.htm#conf-https "Troubleshoot a missing confirmation message for a new HTTPS (Custom URL) subscription.")

[Unreceived messages](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Notification/Tasks/troubleshootingnotifications.htm#msgno "Troubleshoot a missing message for a subscription.")

[Function not invoked or run](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Notification/Tasks/troubleshootingnotifications.htm#fxno "Troubleshoot a function that didn't get invoked or didn't run as expected through a subscription.")

[Email subscription disappeared](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Notification/Tasks/troubleshootingnotifications.htm#email-disappear "Identify events that might have caused a subscription to disappear.")

#### Community

[Oracle Cloud Infrastructure blog](https://blogs.oracle.com/cloud-infrastructure/)

[Cloud infrastructure community forum](https://forums.oracle.com/ords/apexds/domain/dev-community/category/15686-platform?tags=cloud-applications,cloud-infrastructure)

[Notifications learning videos](https://www.youtube.com/playlist?list=PLKCk3OyNwIztB7-JWD5xc-6_MjDmqgOBf)

#### Set Up Notifications

[Automatically resize VMs using Functions](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Notification/Tasks/scenarioa.htm "Set up automatic resizing for virtual machines (VMs) that exceed memory by using Notifications, Functions, and Monitoring services.")

[Send alarm messages to Slack and SMS](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Notification/Tasks/scenariob.htm "Set up automatic notifications to a Slack channel and an SMS phone number when alarms are triggered.")

[File Jira tickets](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Notification/Tasks/scenarioc.htm "Automatically file a Jira ticket whenever a maintenance reminder event occurs. In this scenario, whenever a reminder for upcoming database maintenance comes from Oracle Cloud Infrastructure, a Jira ticket is created for the on-call engineer.")

[Set up contextual notifications for an instance](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Notification/Tasks/contextual-notifications-compute.htm "You can get messages when something happens with a compute instance. Use contextual notifications in the Console to create event rules and alarms for an instance. Quick start templates are available.")

# IAM with Identity Domains

### [Oracle Cloud Infrastructure Documentation](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/home.htm)

* * *

All Pages

[Skip to main content](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Identity/home.htm#dcoc-content-body)

IAM with Identity Domains
=========================

Identity and Access Management (IAM) uses identity domains to provide identity and access management features such as authentication, single sign-on (SSO), and identity lifecycle management for Oracle Cloud as well as for Oracle and non-Oracle applications, whether SaaS, cloud hosted, or on premises.

[What's new](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/releasenotes/services/identity/)

#### Get Started

[Video: Overview](https://apexapps.oracle.com/pls/apex/f?p=44785:265:0:::265:P265_CONTENT_ID:31681)

[Learn about IAM](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Identity/getstarted/identity-domains.htm "Oracle Cloud Infrastructure Identity and Access Management (IAM) provides identity and access management features such as authentication, single sign-on (SSO), and identity lifecycle management for Oracle Cloud as well as Oracle and non Oracle applications, whether SaaS, cloud-hosted, or on-premises. Employees, business partners, and customers can access applications at any time, from anywhere, and on any device in a secure manner.")

[Review key concepts](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Identity/usingidentitydomains/oci-iam-concepts.htm "Get introduced to some concepts you'll come across when you work with IAM with identity domains.")

[IAM Policies Overview](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Identity/policieshow/Policy_Basics.htm "IAM policies govern control of resources in Oracle Cloud Infrastructure (OCI) tenancies.")

[Tutorials](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Identity/tutorials/iam-tutorials.htm "These tutorials show you how to complete identity-related tasks with step-by-step instructions.")

[Product page](https://www.oracle.com/security/cloud-security/identity-cloud/)

#### Users and Groups

[Manage users](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Identity/users/about-managing-users.htm "Create and manage user accounts, including updating and deleting them.")

[Manage groups](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Identity/groups/managinggroups.htm "Use groups with identity domains in IAM to manage user access to applications and resources.")

[Import users](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Identity/datatransfer/import-users.htm "Import users using a comma-separated values (CSV) file.")

[Import groups](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Identity/datatransfer/import-groups.htm "Import groups using a comma-separated values (CSV) file.")

#### Developer Tools

[API for identity domains](https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/paas/iam-domains-rest-api/index.html)

[API for data plane](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/api/#/en/identity-dp/latest/)

[API for control plane](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/api/#/en/identity/)

[CLI for identity domains](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/tools/oci-cli/latest/oci_cli_docs/cmdref/identity-domains.html)

[Get started with REST API](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Identity/api-getstarted/api-get-started.htm "The identity domains REST API securely manage resources, including identities and configuration data. Support for OpenID Connect allows integration with compliant applications and identity domains. The OAuth2 service provides an API infrastructure for authorization that supports a range of token grant types that enable you to securely connect clients to services.")

[SDKs and the CLI](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/API/Concepts/sdks.htm)

#### Support

[Link to a Support account](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Identity/users/link-to-mos.htm "Link a user in an OCI IAM identity domain with a My Oracle Cloud Support Account.")

[Support Requests](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/GSG/Tasks/contactingsupport.htm)

[Create a service request](https://support.oracle.com/)

#### Prerequisites

[Learn about identity domain types](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Identity/sku/overview.htm "Learn about identity domain types and the features and limits associated with each.")

[Understand administrator roles](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Identity/roles/understand-administrator-roles.htm "Learn about administrator roles and the privileges associated with each role so that you can delegate administrative tasks to other users, as needed.")

#### Work with Policies

[IAM Policies Overview](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Identity/policieshow/Policy_Basics.htm "IAM policies govern control of resources in Oracle Cloud Infrastructure (OCI) tenancies.")

[Manage policies](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Identity/Tasks/managingpolicies.htm#Managing_Policies)

[Service Policy Reference](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Identity/policyreference/policyreference.htm "Get an overview of IAM policy reference topics, including verbs, resources types, and general variables.")

#### Identity Providers

[Adding a SAML identity provider](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Identity/identityproviders/add-saml-identity-provider.htm "Use the Console to add a SAML 2.0 identity provider (IdP) to an identity domain so authenticated users from the IdP can access Oracle Cloud Infrastructure can access resources and cloud applications.")

[Exporting SAML IdP metadata](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Identity/identityproviders/add-saml-identity-provider.htm#export-saml-metadata "Exporting the SAML metadata for an identity domain in IAM.")

#### Applications

[Learn about types of applications](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Identity/applications/overview.htm "Identity domains provide a secure and centralized cloud service to manage your cloud, Oracle, custom, and enterprise applications.")

[Use case: add custom applications](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Identity/applications/use-case-adding-applications.htm "To understand how to add custom applications in an identity domain, read this use case.")

[How to add applications](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Identity/applications/add-applications.htm "You can add Custom Applications, if you're assigned to either the identity domain administrator role or the application administrator role.")

#### Community

[Oracle Cloud Infrastructure blog](https://blogs.oracle.com/cloud-infrastructure/)

[Cloud infrastructure community forum](https://forums.oracle.com/ords/apexds/domain/dev-community/category/15686-platform?tags=cloud-applications,cloud-infrastructure)

#### Identity Domains

[Learn about identity domains](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Identity/domains/overview.htm "An identity domain is a container for managing users and roles, federating and provisioning of users, secure application integration through Oracle Single Sign-On (SSO) configuration, and SAML/OAuth based Identity Provider administration. It represents a user population in Oracle Cloud Infrastructure and its associated configurations and security settings (such as MFA).")

[Learn about the default identity domain](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Identity/domains/the_default_domain.htm "Each tenancy includes a Default identity domain in the root compartment.")

[Create an identity domain](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Identity/domains/to-create-new-identity-domain.htm "To create an identity domain in IAM, administrators need to know which identity domain type they want to create, in which compartment to create it, and the new identity domain administrator's sign-in credentials, if needed. The domain types that you're allowed to create are based on your subscription.")

[Change an identity domain](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Identity/domains/to-change-identity-domain-type.htm "Change an identity domain's type in IAM.")

#### Administration

[Managing Identity Assurance](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Identity/securing/configure_idv_providers_and_identity_assurance.htm "Use Identity Verification and Facial Biometrics to ensure that a user is who they claim to be before accessing enterprise resources.")

[Manage default settings](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Identity/defaultsettings/manage-default-settings.htm "Domain settings are applied to this identity domain in the Cloud. You can specify settings such as the time zone, password recovery email, and language.")

[Manage session settings](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Identity/sessionsettings/overview.htm "Define session expiration, the sign-in, sign-out, and error URLs, and configure Allow Cross-Origin Resource Sharing (CORS) to allow client applications that run on one domain to obtain data from another domain.")

[Manage user credentials](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Identity/access/managing-user-credentials.htm "Learn how users can manage their own credentials and what administrators can do with various types of user credentials for other users.")

[Delegate administration to users and groups](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Identity/users/assign-users-roles.htm "Assign users in an OCI IAM identity domain to a role.")

[Run reports](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Identity/reports/understand-types-reports.htm "Find out about the different reports available for an OCI IAM identity domain.")

#### Troubleshooting

[Known issues](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Identity/known-issues/known-issues_root.htm "Known issues have been identified in IAM with Identity Domains.")

[Troubleshooting](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Identity/troubleshooting/troubleshooting_root.htm "Use troubleshooting information to identify and address common issues that can occur while working with IAM with Identity Domains.")

#### What's New

[Release Notes](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/releasenotes/services/identity/)

[Service Change Announcements](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/servicechanges.htm)

# Autonomous Linux

### [Oracle Cloud Infrastructure Documentation](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/home.htm)

* * *

All Pages

[Skip to main content](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/autonomous-linux/doc/home.htm#dcoc-content-body)

Autonomous Linux
================

Oracle Autonomous Linux reduces the complexity and overhead of common operating system management tasks, including automatic daily updates and monitoring of critical events.

[What's new](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/releasenotes/services/autonomous-linux/)

#### Get Started

[Overview](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/autonomous-linux/doc/overview.htm#top "Oracle Autonomous Linux reduces the complexity and overhead of common operating system management tasks, including automatic daily updates and monitoring of critical events.")

[Get started](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/autonomous-linux/doc/getstarted.htm#top "Get started with Autonomous Linux by ensuring service prerequisites are met before enabling the service and registering instances.")

[Supported environments](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/autonomous-linux/doc/getstarted.htm#supported-environments)

[Required IAM policies](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/autonomous-linux/doc/policies.htm#policies "Use policies to control access to Autonomous Linux.")

[Register an instance](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/autonomous-linux/doc/register-instance.htm#top "A newly created Autonomous Linux instance automatically registers with the Autonomous Linux service using a service-provided registration profile. You can also choose your own profile when creating the instance.")

#### Events

[List events](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/autonomous-linux/doc/events-list.htm "View a list of events detected on Autonomous Linux instances within a particular compartment.")

[View event details](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/autonomous-linux/doc/events-details.htm "View detailed information about an event in Autonomous Linux.")

[Enable event collection](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/autonomous-linux/doc/event-collection.htm#top "Enable or disable critical event collection on an Autonomous Linux instance or group. When enabled the instance collects critical event data and sends it to the service.")

#### Support

[Get help and contact Support](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/GSG/Tasks/contactingsupport.htm)

[Create a service request](https://support.oracle.com/epmos/faces/MosIndex.jspx)

#### Groups

[About groups](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/autonomous-linux/doc/understand-groups.htm#understand-groups "Groups provide a way to manage and monitor a collection of instances in Autonomous Linux. You can use groups to apply errata, update software, and schedule jobs for common content management tasks.")

[Create a group](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/autonomous-linux/doc/create-group.htm#top "Use groups to standardize and unify content across a set of instances in Autonomous Linux.")

[Create a group profile](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/autonomous-linux/doc/create-profile-group.htm#top "Create a group profile to associate an instance with a group when registering with Autonomous Linux.")

#### Profiles

[About profiles](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/autonomous-linux/doc/understand-profiles.htm#understand-profiles "A profile is required to register an instance with Autonomous Linux. Profiles provide a way to consistently define how an instance registers with the service.")

[Create a profile](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/autonomous-linux/doc/create-profile.htm#top "Create a profile in Autonomous Linux to register an instance with the service.")

[Select default profile](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/autonomous-linux/doc/set-default-profile.htm#top "Define the profile to use as a default when registering a particular instance type (location, OS version, architecture) with Autonomous Linux. Default profiles must reside in the root compartment.")

#### Developer Tools

[API for Autonomous Linux](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/api/#/en/osmh/latest/)

[CLI for Autonomous Linux](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/tools/oci-cli/latest/oci_cli_docs/cmdref/os-management-hub.html)

[SDKs and the CLI](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/API/Concepts/sdks.htm)

[Cloud Shell](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/API/Concepts/devcloudshellintro.htm)

[Terraform](https://registry.terraform.io/providers/oracle/oci/latest/docs/resources/os_management_hub_event)

#### Reports

[Compartment-level report](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/autonomous-linux/doc/view-reports.htm#top "View a compartment-level summary of instances that are up-to-date on security vulnerability patches and bug fixes and their check-in status with Autonomous Linux.")

[Security report](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/autonomous-linux/doc/view-security-report.htm#top "View the security updates report in Autonomous Linux. This report indicates whether the instances are up-to-date on security patches and provides details about associated security advisories.")

[Vulnerability report](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/autonomous-linux/doc/view-vulnerability-report.htm#top "Use the vulnerability report in Autonomous Linux to identify security vulnerabilities on an instance. The report lists security advisories, the corresponding CVEs, and the affected packages.")

#### Schedule Updates

[About update management](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/autonomous-linux/doc/updates.htm#updates "Autonomous Linux lets you create one-time or recurring update jobs to update instances.")

[Update an instance](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/autonomous-linux/doc/create-scheduled-job-instance.htm#top "Create an update job to schedule updates for an instance in Autonomous Linux. You can schedule the job to run immediately or at a future date and time. You can also schedule the update job to run as a recurring event.")

[Update a group](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/autonomous-linux/doc/create-scheduled-job-group.htm#top "Create a job in Autonomous Linux to update all the instances in a group. You can schedule the job to run immediately or at a future date and time. You can also schedule the update job to run as a recurring event.")

[Update a compartment](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/autonomous-linux/doc/create-update-job-compartment.htm "Schedule a job to update all instances in a compartment in Autonomous Linux. The instances can be a mix of OS versions, architectures, and locations.")

#### Troubleshooting

[Registration failures](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/autonomous-linux/doc/troubleshoot-instance-station-registration-failure.htm#troubleshoot-instance-station-registration-failure "An instance can fail to register with Autonomous Linux for several reasons.")

[Troubleshooting](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/autonomous-linux/doc/troubleshooting.htm#troubleshooting "Identify and address common issues that can occur while working with Autonomous Linux.")

[Known Issues](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/autonomous-linux/doc/alx-known-issues.htm#known-issues "These known issues have been identified in Autonomous Linux.")

#### Community

[Cloud Customer Connect](https://community.oracle.com/customerconnect/)

[Oracle Cloud Infrastructure blog](https://blogs.oracle.com/cloud-infrastructure/)

[Cloud infrastructure community forum](https://community.oracle.com/tech/apps-infra/categories/18430-cloud-infrastructure)

[Linux cloud community](https://community.oracle.com/customerconnect/categories/oci-linux)

# Java Management

### [Oracle Cloud Infrastructure Documentation](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/home.htm)

* * *

All Pages

[Skip to main content](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/jms/index.html#dcoc-content-body)

Java Management
===============

Java Management Service is a reporting and management service within Oracle Cloud Infrastructure Platform. Fleet Management provides insights into the Java usage in your environment while Java Download enables your Java workloads to always use the most up-to-date and secure Java releases.

Get Started
-----------

*   [About Java Management Service](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/jms/doc/java-management-service.html#AJSUG-GUID-3FC9F2D3-1680-4B32-8509-7F922335C068)
    
*   [Key Concepts and Terminology](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/jms/doc/key-concepts-and-terminology.html#AJSUG-GUID-E6F53040-22AB-4446-A037-844D4AC1879B)
    
*   [Fleet Management](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/jms/doc/fleet-management.html)
    
*   [Setting Up Oracle Cloud Infrastructure for Java Management Service](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/jms/doc/setting-oracle-cloud-infrastructure-java-management-service.html#AJSUG-GUID-A2B4E402-108C-4510-A430-4F1B4CD90167)
    
*   [Troubleshooting Fleet Management Issues](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/jms/doc/troubleshooting.html)
    
*   [Java Download](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/jms/doc/java-download.html)
    

Related Content
---------------

*   [Java Management Service Fleets API](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/api/#/en/jms/20210610/)
    
*   [Java Management Service Java Download API](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/api/#/en/jms-java-download/20230601/)
    
*   [Java Management Service Utilities API](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/api/#/en/jms-utils/20250521/)
    
*   [Securing Java Management](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/Security/Reference/java_security.htm#securing-java-management)
    
*   [JMS Reference Architecture](https://docs.oracle.com/en/solutions/manage-java-env/index.html)
    
*   [Policy Reference Details for Java Management Service](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/Identity/policyreference/javamanagementreference.htm)
    

Livelabs
--------

*   [Manage Java Runtimes, Applications and Managed Instances Inventory with Java Management Service](https://livelabs.oracle.com/pls/apex/r/dbpm/livelabs/view-workshop?wid=912)
    
*   [Using Java Management Service Advanced Features](https://livelabs.oracle.com/pls/apex/r/dbpm/livelabs/view-workshop?wid=3202)
    
*   [Integrate OCI Services with Java Management Service](https://livelabs.oracle.com/pls/apex/r/dbpm/livelabs/view-workshop?wid=3203)
    
*   [Analyze Java Applications with Java Management Service](https://livelabs.oracle.com/pls/apex/r/dbpm/livelabs/view-workshop?wid=4271)
    

More Resources
--------------

# Managed Services for Mac

### [Oracle Cloud Infrastructure Documentation](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/home.htm)

* * *

All Pages

[Skip to main content](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/managed-mac/home.htm#dcoc-content-body)

Managed Services for Mac
========================

Managed Services for Mac lets you create and manage orders for dedicated, partially managed Mac servers hosted in an Oracle Cloud Infrastructure Data Center.

[What's new](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/releasenotes/)

#### Get Started

[Learn how Managed Services for Mac works](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/managed-mac/overview.htm "Learn about the basic operating concepts of Managed Services for Mac.")

[Limitations and conditions](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/managed-mac/overview.htm#limitations)

#### Community

[Oracle Cloud Infrastructure blog](https://blogs.oracle.com/cloud-infrastructure/)

[Cloud infrastructure community forum](https://forums.oracle.com/ords/apexds/domain/dev-community/category/15686-platform?tags=cloud-applications,cloud-infrastructure)

#### Manage Orders and Servers

[List your Mac server orders](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/managed-mac/list-order.htm "View the orders for Mac servers in a specific compartment in Managed Services for Mac.")

[Create a new Mac server order](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/managed-mac/create-order.htm "Create an order for Mac servers in Managed Services for Mac.")

[View a Mac server order's details](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/managed-mac/get-order.htm "View the details for a Mac server order in Managed Services for Mac.")

[View and manage Mac servers in an order](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/managed-mac/manage-mac-servers.htm "Manage your Mac servers' orders and functionality.")

#### Developer Tools

[SDKs and the CLI](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/API/Concepts/sdks.htm)

[Cloud Shell](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/API/Concepts/devcloudshellintro.htm)

#### Support

[Learn about support requests](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/GSG/Tasks/contactingsupport.htm)

[Create a service request](https://support.oracle.com/)

Oracle Cloud Infrastructure GoldenGate
======================================

Oracle's industry leading data replication tool is now an Oracle Cloud Infrastructure native service.

Get Started
-----------

### Get to know the service

*   [OCI GoldenGate Tutorials](https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/paas/goldengate-service/tutorials.html)
    
*   [About Oracle Cloud Infrastructure GoldenGate](https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/paas/goldengate-service/ocigg/about-oracle-cloud-infrastructure-goldengate.html)
    
*   [What's supported?](https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/paas/goldengate-service/ocigg/whats-supported.html)
    
*   **Create your first instance**
    *   [Create cloud resources](https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/paas/goldengate-service/ocigg/create-oracle-cloud-resources.html)
        
    *   [Create a deployment](https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/paas/goldengate-service/ocigg/create-data-replication-resources.html)
        
    *   [Create connections](https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/paas/goldengate-service/ocigg/explore-connections.html)
        
    *   [Assign a connection to a deployment](https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/paas/goldengate-service/ocigg/create-data-replication-resources.html#GUID-69F7C944-DD31-40FA-8589-4CB5113E74D0)
        

### Try it

New to OCI GoldenGate? Try one of the following tutorials. No environment set up needed!

*   [Replicate data using OCI GoldenGate](https://livelabs.oracle.com/ords/r/dbpm/livelabs/view-workshop?wid=797)
    
*   [Set up bidirectional replication in OCI GoldenGate](https://livelabs.oracle.com/ords/r/dbpm/livelabs/view-workshop?wid=3485)
    
*   [Send data from Oracle GoldenGate to OCI GoldenGate](https://livelabs.oracle.com/ords/r/dbpm/livelabs/view-workshop?wid=851)
    
*   [Send data from OCI GoldenGate to Oracle GoldenGate](https://livelabs.oracle.com/ords/r/dbpm/livelabs/view-workshop?wid=881)
    
*   [Replicate data from Autonomous Transaction Processing to OCI Object Storage](https://livelabs.oracle.com/ords/r/dbpm/livelabs/view-workshop?wid=3884)
    
*   [Replicate data from MySQL to Autonomous Database](https://livelabs.oracle.com/ords/r/dbpm/livelabs/view-workshop?wid=3503)
    
*   [Replicate data from Autonomous Transaction Processing to Apache Kafka](https://livelabs.oracle.com/ords/r/dbpm/livelabs/view-workshop?wid=3868)
    
*   [Replicate and transform data with OCI GoldenGate Data Transforms](https://livelabs.oracle.com/ords/r/dbpm/livelabs/view-workshop?wid=3916)

# Digital Assistant

### [Oracle Cloud Infrastructure Documentation](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/home.htm)

* * *

All Pages

[Skip to main content](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/digital-assistant/index.html#dcoc-content-body)

Digital Assistant
=================

Oracle Digital Assistant is a platform that allows you to create and deploy digital assistants, which are AI-driven interfaces that help users accomplish a variety of tasks in natural language conversations.

Get Started
-----------

*   [Overview of Digital Assistants and Skills](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/digital-assistant/doc/overview-digital-assistants-and-skills.html#GUID-386AB33B-C131-4A0A-9138-6732AE841BD8)
    
*   [Order the Service and Provision an Instance](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/digital-assistant/doc/order-service-and-provision-instance.html#GUID-EB06833C-7B1C-46F6-B63C-1F23375CEB7E)
    
*   [Get Started with the Service](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/digital-assistant/doc/get-started-skills-and-digital-assistants.html#GUID-2DB01832-3BA3-4356-95DC-7ED760953DD6)
    
*   [Create, Configure, and Version Skills](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/digital-assistant/doc/create-configure-and-version-skills1.html#GUID-C8F01D74-4906-49D1-9DE7-6A0E07E0A835)
    

Related Content
---------------

*   [REST API Reference](https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/paas/digital-assistant/rest-api-oci/index.html)
    
*   [SDKs](https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/paas/digital-assistant/sdks.html)
    

More Resources
--------------

# Security Advisor

### [Oracle Cloud Infrastructure Documentation](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/home.htm)

* * *

All Pages

[Skip to main content](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/SecurityAdvisor/home.htm#dcoc-content-body)

Security Advisor
================

Security Advisor supports and reinforces Oracle security best practices, including the configuration requirements for resources in Security Zones.

[What's new](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/releasenotes/services/security-advisor/)

#### Get Started

[Learn about Security Advisor](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/SecurityAdvisor/Concepts/securityadvisoroverview.htm#securityadvisoroverview "Oracle Cloud Infrastructure Security Advisor supports and reinforces Oracle security best practices, including the configuration requirements for resources in Security Zones. It combines and streamlines existing workflows to efficiently create resources that meet baseline security requirements from the outset.")

[Check required IAM policies](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/SecurityAdvisor/Concepts/get-started.htm#get-started "Before creating secure resources with Oracle Cloud Infrastructure Security Advisor, complete these prerequisite tasks.")

#### Support

[Support Requests](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/GSG/Tasks/contactingsupport.htm)

[Create a service request](https://support.oracle.com/)

#### Secure Resources

[Create a secure bucket](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/SecurityAdvisor/Tasks/creatingsecurebucket.htm#creatingsecurebucket "Use Security Advisor to create a secure bucket in Object Storage. In this context, a secure bucket is one that's encrypted with a customer-managed key and therefore meets minimum security requirements established by security zones.")

[Create a secure compute instance](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/SecurityAdvisor/Tasks/creatingsecurevirtualmachine.htm#creatingsecurevirtualmachine "Use Security Advisor to create a secure virtual machine (VM) instance in Compute. In this context, a secure instance is one with a boot volume that's encrypted with a customer-managed key and therefore meets minimum security requirements established by security zones.")

[Create a secure file system](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/SecurityAdvisor/Tasks/creatingsecurefilesystem.htm#creatingsecurefilesystem "Use Security Advisor to create a secure file system in File Storage. In this context, a secure file system is one that's encrypted with a customer-managed key and therefore meets minimum security requirements established by security zones.")

[Create a secure volume](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/SecurityAdvisor/Tasks/creatingsecureblockvolume.htm#creatingsecureblockvolume "Use Security Advisor to create a secure block volume in Block Volume. In this context, a secure block volume is one that's encrypted with a customer-managed key and therefore meets minimum security requirements established by security zones.")

#### Developer Tools

[APIs](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/api/#/en/iaas/)

[SDKs and the CLI](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/API/Concepts/sdks.htm)

[Cloud Shell](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/API/Concepts/devcloudshellintro.htm)

#### Security Zones

[Learn about Security Zones](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/security-zone/using/security-zones.htm)

[Learn about zone policies](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/security-zone/using/security-zone-policies.htm)

#### Community

[Cloud Security blog](https://blogs.oracle.com/cloudsecurity/)

[Cloud infrastructure community forum](https://community.oracle.com/tech/apps-infra/categories/18430-cloud-infrastructure)

---

# Networking

### [Oracle Cloud Infrastructure Documentation](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/home.htm)

* * *

All Pages

[Skip to main content](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Network/Concepts/landing.htm#dcoc-content-body)

Networking
==========

Oracle Cloud Infrastructure Networking helps you set up virtual versions of traditional network components.

[What's new](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/releasenotes/services/network/)

#### Get Started

[Video: Overview](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=Laxxzga2bWw)

[Learn about virtual cloud networks (VCNs) and subnets](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Network/Tasks/VCNs.htm "A Virtual Cloud Network (VCN) is a customizable and private network set up in Oracle Cloud Infrastructure.")

[Learn about VNICs](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Network/Tasks/managingVNICs.htm)

[Learn about DNS in VCNs](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Network/Concepts/dns.htm)

[Product page](https://www.oracle.com/cloud/networking/)

#### Learn more

[Learn about DNS and Traffic Management](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/DNS/home.htm)

[Learn about enabling IPv6](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Network/Concepts/ipv6.htm "Learn about support for IPv6 addressing in your VCN.")

[Learn about load balancing](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/Balance/Concepts/balanceoverview.htm)

[Learn about access to the internet](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Network/Concepts/internetaccess.htm "See these topics for how to give a Virtual Cloud Network (VCN) access to the internet:")

[Learn about other networking components](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Network/Concepts/overview.htm#Componen)

#### Connect to OCI from your network

[Learn about dynamic routing gateways (DRGs)](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Network/Tasks/managingDRGs.htm)

[Set up Site-to-Site VPN](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Network/Tasks/managingIPsec.htm "Site-to-Site VPN provides an IPSec connection between an on-premises network and a Virtual Cloud Network (VCN).")

[Set up FastConnect](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Network/Concepts/fastconnectoverview.htm)

#### Developer Tools

[Core Services API](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/api/#/en/iaas/)

[SDKs and the CLI](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/API/Concepts/sdks.htm)

[Cloud Shell](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/API/Concepts/devcloudshellintro.htm)

#### Community

[Oracle Cloud Infrastructure blog](https://blogs.oracle.com/cloud-infrastructure/)

[The A-Team Chronicles](https://www.ateam-oracle.com/)

[Cloud Infrastructure community forum](https://forums.oracle.com/ords/apexds/domain/dev-community/category/15686-platform?tags=cloud-applications,cloud-infrastructure)

[Networking learning videos](https://www.youtube.com/playlist?list=PLKCk3OyNwIzumMX0ui-Qg2Kigk_UTxqdB)

#### Network Command Center

[Inter-Region Latency](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Network/Concepts/inter_region_latency.htm "View the Inter-Region Latency dashboard in the Console. The dashboard provides the average network round-trip latency (round-trip time or RTT) for all pairs of regions in an Oracle Cloud Infrastructure realm. In realms with only one region, the Inter-Region Latency dashboard isn't available.")

[Network Path Analyzer](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Network/Concepts/path_analyzer.htm "Learn about the Network Path Analyzer tool.")

Bastion
=======

Oracle Cloud Infrastructure Bastion provides restricted and time-limited access to target resources that don't have public endpoints.

# Object Storage

Object Storage
==============

Object Storage is a fully programmable, scalable, and durable cloud storage service. Use this service to store and easily access an unlimited amount of data at low cost.

[What's new](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/releasenotes/services/objectstorage/)

#### Get Started

[Overview of Object Storage](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Object/Concepts/objectstorageoverview.htm "Learn how to use Object Storage to store and easily access an unlimited amount of data at low cost.")

[Ways to access Object Storage](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Object/Concepts/objectstorageoverview.htm#accessways)

[Use Object Storage](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Object/Concepts/objectstorageoverview.htm#using)

[Product page](https://www.oracle.com/cloud/storage/object-storage/)

#### Storage Tiers

[Manage object lifecycles](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Object/Tasks/usinglifecyclepolicies.htm "Learn how to use Object Lifecycle Management to automatically manage the archiving and deletion of objects.")

# Welcome to Oracle Cloud Infrastructure

[Skip to main content](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/GSG/Concepts/baremetalintro.htm#dcoc-content-body)

Welcome to Oracle Cloud Infrastructure
======================================

Oracle Cloud Infrastructure (OCI) is a set of complementary cloud services that enable you to build and run a range of applications and services in a highly available hosted environment. OCI provides high-performance compute capabilities (as physical hardware instances) and storage capacity in a flexible overlay virtual network that is securely accessible from your on-premises network.

[What's new](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/releasenotes/)

Management Dashboards
=====================

Dashboards are data visualization tools that gather real-time data from the various tiers of applications or other monitored resources, and display it in widgets. They provide insight into the health and performance of your resources and help identify potential problems.

# Data Safe

### [Oracle Cloud Infrastructure Documentation](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/home.htm)

* * *

All Pages

[Skip to main content](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/data-safe/index.html#dcoc-content-body)

Data Safe
=========

Oracle Data Safe is a fully-integrated Cloud service focused on the security of your data. It provides a complete and integrated set of features for protecting sensitive and regulated data in Oracle Cloud databases. Features include Security Assessment, User Assessment, Data Discovery, Data Masking, Activity Auditing, Security Policies, SQL Firewall, and Alerts.

Get Started
-----------

*   [Access Oracle Data Safe](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/data-safe/doc/access-set-up-oracle-data-safe.html)
    
*   [Register a Target Database](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/data-safe/doc/target-database-registration.html)
    
*   [Assess Database Security](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/data-safe/doc/security-assessment.html)
    
*   [Assess User Security](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/data-safe/doc/user-assessment.html)
    
*   [Discover Sensitive Data](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/data-safe/doc/data-discovery.html)
    
*   [Mask Sensitive Data](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/data-safe/doc/data-masking.html)
    
*   [Audit Database Activity](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/data-safe/doc/activity-auditing.html)
    
*   [Create Security Policies](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/data-safe/doc/security-policies.html)
    
*   [Manage SQL Firewall](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/data-safe/doc/sql-firewall.html)
    
*   [Generate Alerts](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/data-safe/doc/alerts.html)
