Developer Tools
---------------

[OCI Java SDK Version 2 Is Deprecated](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/servicechanges.htm#)

**Service Change:** OCI Java SDK version 2 is being deprecated.

**Date Announced:** April 6, 2023

**Date in Effect:** June 30, 2023

**Details:** Oracle has recently released Oracle Cloud Infrastructure (OCI) Java SDK version 3. OCI Java SDK version 3 is a major SDK release, and we recommend that you upgrade to this latest version. We will continue to fully support OCI Java SDK version 2 until the end of June 2023. During this time, both OCI Java SDK versions 2 and 3 will receive regular updates to add support for new service APIs, critical bug fixes and security patches, and documentation modifications.

**Does this impact me?** After June 30, 2023, OCI Java SDK version 2 will no longer receive updates to add support for new regions, new services, or features in existing services, unless otherwise specified. For older versions of the OCI Java SDK version 2 that are less than 12 months old, upon [request](https://support.oracle.com/portal/)
, OCI will backport critical bug fixes and security issues only.

**What do I need to do?** Upgrade to OCI Java SDK version 3.

DevOps
------

[Deprecated DevOps APIs](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/servicechanges.htm#)

**Service Change:** Oracle Cloud Infrastructure's DevOps APIs (two APIs) of version `20210630` were deprecated on March 29, 2022.

**Date Announced:** March 29, 2022

**Date in Effect:** March 29, 2023

**Details:** Starting March 29, 2022, two DevOps APIs of version `20210630` were deprecated. From March 29, 2023, onwards the deprecated APIs will be unavailable.

| Deprecated API | Replacement API |
| --- | --- |
| [GetRepositoryFileLines](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/api/#%23/en/devops/latest/Repository/GetRepositoryFileLines) | [GetRepoFileLines](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/api/#%23/en/devops/20210630/Repository/GetRepoFileLines) |
| [GetFileDiff](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/api/#%23/en/devops/20210630/Repository/GetFileDiff) | [GetRepoFileDiff](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/api/#%23/en/devops/20210630/Repository/GetRepoFileDiff) |

[DevOps Ends Support for Oracle Linux 7](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/servicechanges.htm#)

**Service Change:** OCI DevOps ends support for Oracle Linux 7.

**Date Announced:** August 27, 2025

**Date in Effect:** August 28, 2026

**Details:** Effective August 28, 2026, the support for Oracle Linux 7 comes to an end, after which users can't create or run builds on OL7-based build runners. To ensure a smooth transition, we advise migrating to Oracle Linux 8 before October 12, 2025 to avoid latency in Oracle Linux 7-based build runner launches. To ensure a seamless transition and best build performance, update the pipeline configurations to use Oracle Linux 8.

Event Hub
---------

[Event Hub service deprecated](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/servicechanges.htm#)

**Service Change:** The Event Hub service is deprecated.

**Date Announced:** April 29, 2022

**Date in Effect:** May 31, 2023

**Details:** Effective May 31, 2023, the Oracle Event Hub service will reach end of life (EOL). Prior to the EOL date, we recommend that you migrate your data streams from Event Hub to [Oracle Cloud Infrastructure Streaming](https://www.oracle.com/cloud/cloud-native/streaming/?elqat=2&elqaid=96348&elqTrackId=516d7366256f4363b1733960f1cdf191)
 .

**Does this impact me?** If you use Event Hub service to create Kafka Clusters and/or Event Hub topics, you will no longer be able to after May 31, 2023. Previously created clusters will continue to operate in your tenancy without changes.

**What do I need to do?** All Event Hub customers can now use [Streaming](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/Streaming/home.htm)
 to move data using its tight integrations with Oracle Cloud Infrastructure (OCI), Database, GoldenGate, and Integration Cloud. The service uses a [Kafka Connect harness](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/Streaming/Tasks/managingkafkaconnectharnesses.htm)
 to provide out-of-the-box integrations for hundreds of third-party products across categories such as DevOps, databases, big data, and SaaS applications.

File Storage
------------

[New service limit introduced for number of file systems attached to a snapshot policy](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/servicechanges.htm#)

**Service Change:** A maximum of 100 file systems may be attached to any particular snapshot policy.

**Date Announced:** August 5, 2023

**Date in Effect:** August 7, 2023

**Details:** Starting August 7, 2023, a new service limit will be introduced to limit the total number of file systems attached to a snapshot policy. This change will allow up to 100 file systems to be attached per snapshot policy per tenant per availability domain.

**Does this impact me?** If you're planning to attach more than 100 file systems to any particular snapshot policy, then you won't be able to do so after August 7, 2023. Any existing tenants who have more than 100 file systems attached per snapshot policy before August 7, 2023 will be given an exception. No exceptions will be given after August 7, 2023.

**What do I need to do?** If you need to attach more than 100 file systems to a snapshot policy, then create a second snapshot policy or use any other existing snapshot policy. You can create 100 snapshot policy per tenant per availability domain. You can still generate policy-based snapshots for file systems, but you might have to use more than one snapshot policy.

[New IAM policy required to grant file systems permissions to use custom encryption keys](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/servicechanges.htm#)

**Service Change:** File Storage file systems using customer-managed encryption keys need new IAM policies.

**Date Announced:** May 29, 2024

**Date in Effect:** May 29, 2024

**Details:** On May 29, 2024, File Storage introduces a new method for granting file systems permission to use custom encryption keys. Before this change, policies to grant file systems permissions to use custom encryption keys used the File Storage service user. For example:

    Allow service <file_storage_service_user> to use keys

After this change, File Storage resources are granted permission to use custom keys. For more information, see [Encrypting a File System](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/File/Tasks/encrypt-file-system.htm)
.

**Does this impact me?** Before May 29, 2024, File Storage file systems using customer-managed Vault encryption keys instead of Oracle-managed keys used the File Storage service user in required IAM policies.

The service user won't have access to customer-managed keys in the future.

**What do I need to do?** Create new IAM policies that lets File Storage encrypt and decrypt file systems using resource principals. Ensure file systems use resource principal policies instead of service principal policies.

For more information, see [Encrypting a File System](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/File/Tasks/encrypt-file-system.htm)
 and [Verifying Resource Principal Access to Encryption Keys](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/File/Tasks/verify-encryption-key-principal.htm)
.

Fleet Application Management
----------------------------

[Fleet Application Management End of Life](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/servicechanges.htm#)

**Service Change:** OCI Fleet Application Management End of Life

**Date Announced:** May 6, 2026

**Date in Effect:** May 1, 2027

**Details:** OCI Fleet Application Management is deprecated as of May 6, 2026. If you are an existing OCI Fleet Application Management customer, you can continue to use the service in your existing tenancies and regions until the last date of service, May 1, 2027. For alternative cloud-based monitoring solutions, we recommend the following options:

*   Use Java Management Service (JMS) for Java workloads.
*   Use Database Management service to monitor all Oracle database and Exadata resources.
*   To monitor other resource types, select the appropriate OCI Observability & Management services.

**Does this impact me?** Yes, if you currently use OCI Fleet Application Management.

**What do I need to do**:

*   Plan and implement a transition to an alternative lifecycle management and resource management solution before the end-of-service date to prevent disruption.
*   Evaluate and select a replacement that meets your business and technical requirements. For cloud-based monitoring solutions, select Java Management Service (JMS) for Java workloads or OCI Database Management for monitoring Oracle database and Exadata resources. To monitor other resource types, use the applicable OCI Observability and Management services.
*   For assistance or additional information, submit a Service Request through My Oracle Support.

[Deprecated Fleet Application Management APIs](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/servicechanges.htm#)

**Service Change:** Oracle Cloud Infrastructure's Fleet Application Management APIs of version `20230831` were deprecated on May 14, 2025.

**Date Announced:** May 14, 2025

**Date in Effect:** May 14, 2025

**Details:** Starting May 14, 2025, Fleet Application Management APIs of version 20230831 was deprecated. From May 14, 2025, onwards the deprecated APIs will be unavailable. The Update/Delete/Get/List Operations on any resources created by deprecated APIs will be supported by the new APIs.

Full Stack Disaster Recovery
----------------------------

[Deprecated Member Type](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/servicechanges.htm#)

**Service Change:** The `COMPUTE_INSTANCE` member type in `DrProtectionGroupMemberType` is getting deprecated and will no longer be supported.

**Date Announced:** October 31, 2023

**Date in Effect:** October 31, 2024.

**Details:** The member type `COMPUTE_INSTANCE` is getting deprecated and will be replaced by the following alternate member types:

*   `COMPUTE_INSTANCE_MOVABLE`: Used for compute instances that move during DR operations.
*   `COMPUTE_INSTANCE_NON_MOVABLE`: Used for compute instances that do not move during DR operations.

Migrate to one of the new instance types before the effective deprecation date.

**Does this impact me?** If you use the `COMPUTE_INSTANCE` member type in your DR configuration, this change affects you. Make sure that you migrate to one of the new instance types before the effective deprecation date.

**What do I need to do?** To migrate from an existing `COMPUTE_INSTANCE` to one of the new instance types, follow [these instructions](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/disaster-recovery/doc/migrating-to-new-compute-instance.html)
.

[Deprecated blockVolumeOperations Property](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/servicechanges.htm#)

**Service Change:** The blockVolumeOperations property for a Non Movable Compute Instance is being deprecated and will no longer be supported.

**Date Announced:** May 20, 2025

**Date in Effect:** May 20, 2026

**Details:** The `blockVolumeOperations` property in `DrProtectionGroupMemberComputeInstanceNonMovable`, `CreateDrProtectionGroupMemberComputeInstanceNonMovableDetails`, and `UpdateDrProtectionGroupMemberComputeInstanceNonMovableDetails` data types are deprecated. From April 30, 2026, onwards, the deprecated property will be unavailable.

| Deprecated data types | Replacement data types |
| --- | --- |
| The **blockVolumeOperations** property for a Non Movable Compute Instance is deprecated in the following data types:<br><br>*   [`DrProtectionGroupMemberComputeInstanceNonMovable`](https://docs.public.content.oci.oraclecloud.com/en-us/iaas/api/#/en/disaster-recovery/20220125/datatypes/DrProtectionGroupMemberComputeInstanceNonMovable)<br>     data type<br>*   [`CreateDrProtectionGroupMemberComputeInstanceNonMovableDetails`](https://docs.public.content.oci.oraclecloud.com/en-us/iaas/api/#/en/disaster-recovery/20220125/datatypes/CreateDrProtectionGroupMemberComputeInstanceNonMovableDetails)<br>     data type<br>*   [`UpdateDrProtectionGroupMemberComputeInstanceNonMovableDetails`](https://docs.public.content.oci.oraclecloud.com/en-us/iaas/api/#/en/disaster-recovery/20220125/datatypes/UpdateDrProtectionGroupMemberComputeInstanceNonMovableDetails)<br>     data type | Use the **blockVolumeAttachAndMountOperations** property instead of the `blockVolumeOperations` property in the following data types:<br><br>*   [`DrProtectionGroupMemberComputeInstanceNonMovable`](https://docs.public.content.oci.oraclecloud.com/en-us/iaas/api/#/en/disaster-recovery/20220125/datatypes/DrProtectionGroupMemberComputeInstanceNonMovable)<br>     data type<br>*   [`CreateDrProtectionGroupMemberComputeInstanceNonMovableDetails`](https://docs.public.content.oci.oraclecloud.com/en-us/iaas/api/#/en/disaster-recovery/20220125/datatypes/CreateDrProtectionGroupMemberComputeInstanceNonMovableDetails)<br>     data type<br>*   [`UpdateDrProtectionGroupMemberComputeInstanceNonMovableDetails`](https://docs.public.content.oci.oraclecloud.com/en-us/iaas/api/#/en/disaster-recovery/20220125/datatypes/UpdateDrProtectionGroupMemberComputeInstanceNonMovableDetails)<br>     data type |

**Does this impact me?**

No. However, you can not specify multiple mount points for a single block volume mounted by Full Stack DR using the deprecated **`blockVolumeOperations`** property. You can now specify multiple mount points for a single block volume mounted by Full Stack DR using the new **`blockVolumeAttachAndMountOperations`** property. You can also specify multiple mount points for a logical volume (LV) which can consist of multiple block volumes. Attachments and mounts are now completely independent of each other.

**Note**  
  
You cannot use the deprecated **`blockVolumeOperations`** property and the **`blockVolumeAttachAndMountOperations`** property together.

**What do I need to do?**

Use the `**blockVolumeAttachAndMountOperations**` property instead of the `**blockVolumeOperations**` property.

Functions
---------

[Fn Project CLI version 0.5.x (and earlier) no longer supported](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/servicechanges.htm#)

**Service Change:** Fn Project CLI version 0.5.x (and earlier) will no longer be supported.

**Date Announced:** June 29, 2021

**Date in Effect:** August 1, 2021

**Details:** Starting August 1, 2021, Fn Project CLI version 0.5.x (and earlier) will no longer work with OCI Functions.

**Does this impact me?** If you are currently using Fn Project CLI version 0.5.x (or earlier), you will need to upgrade to Fn Project CLI version 0.6.x (or later).

**What do I need to do?** Upgrade to Fn Project CLI version 0.6.x (or later), by following the instructions in [Upgrading the Fn Project CLI](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/Functions/Tasks/functionsupgradingfncli.htm)
.

[Fn Project FDK build-time and runtime base images built on Oracle Linux 8 (Alpine/Debian FDK base images deprecated)](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/servicechanges.htm#)

**Service Change:** From December 15, 2021, the Fn Project [Function Development Kits (FDKs)](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/Functions/Tasks/functionssupportedlanguageversions.htm)
 build-time and runtime base images, with the exception of the FDK for Python 3.7, are built on top of the Oracle Linux 8 slim distribution. The Alpine/Debian FDK base images are deprecated.

**Date Announced:** November 15, 2021

**Date in Effect:** December 15, 2021

**Details:** Starting December 15, 2021, most of the Fn Project [Function Development Kits (FDKs)](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/Functions/Tasks/functionssupportedlanguageversions.htm)
 build-time and runtime base images for the different supported languages are built on top of the Oracle Linux 8 slim distribution (instead of the Alpine and Debian Linux distributions). New functions you deploy will use these Oracle Linux 8 FDK base images. The only exceptions are the FDK build-time and runtime base images for Python 3.7, which continue to be built on top of the Debian Linux distribution.

The Alpine/Debian Linux distributions and the Oracle Linux 8 slim distribution have different package managers. After the transition to the Oracle Linux 8 FDK base images, the temporary Dockerfile created by OCI Functions when deploying new functions contains Oracle Linux 8 package manager commands.

**Does this impact me?**

For existing functions already deployed to OCI Functions:

*   If OCI Functions uses the settings in a function's func.yaml file to create a temporary Dockerfile containing the instructions from which to build the function's Docker image, the function will build and deploy without errors. The temporary Dockerfile includes the correct Oracle Linux 8 package manager commands.
*   If you have created a custom Dockerfile for a function (for example, by modifying the Dockerfile that OCI Functions creates, and setting `runtime: docker` in the function's func.yaml file), the function might now build and deploy with errors such as `missing apt-get ...`. The errors occur if the custom Dockerfile includes Alpine/Debian package manager commands.

**What do I need to do?** If you have created custom Dockerfiles that contain Alpine/Debian package manager commands, replace those commands with Oracle Linux 8 package manager commands.

If you cannot immediately start using the Oracle Linux 8 FDK base images because you have functions that still require the Alpine or Debian Linux distributions, there is a temporary workaround. Until December 15, 2022, the Alpine/Debian FDK base images remain available but with modified image tags. You can update custom Dockerfiles to use the deprecated Alpine/Debian FDK base images instead of the Oracle Linux 8 base images by explicitly specifying the modified image tags. See [My functions still require the Alpine and Debian Linux distributions. Is there a temporary workaround?](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/Functions/Tasks/functionsfdksandlinuxdistros_faqs.htm#functionsfdksandlinuxdistros_daqs__section_wq4_blf_4rb)
.

Fusion Applications Environment Management
------------------------------------------

[UpdateFusionEnvironment API kmsKeyId attribute is deprecated](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/servicechanges.htm#)

**Service Change:** The `kmsKeyId` attribute is deprecated for the UpdateFusionEnvironment API.

**Date Announced:** September 19, 2025.

**Date in Effect:** September 19, 2026.

**Details:** Before this service change, the `kmsKeyId` attribute is optional when you update a Fusion Applications environment via API. After this service change is implemented, the `kmsKeyId` attribute will be removed from the UpdateFusionEnvironment API.

**Does this impact me?** This service change impacts you if you're currently updating Fusion Applications environments via API using the `kmsKeyId` attribute.

**What do I need to do?** If you're calling the UpdateFusionEnvironment API, don't pass the `kmsKeyId` attribute. Instead, use the Oracle Cloud Console to add customer-managed keys to existing environments. For more information, see [Adding Customer-Managed Keys to New and Existing Environments](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/fusion-applications/manage-security-key-management.htm#fa-cm-tasks-fa-admin)
.

[Fusion Applications Administrator Password Field Removed from Fusion Applications Environment Management APIs](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/servicechanges.htm#)

**Service Change:** When you create a Fusion Applications Environment or a Fusion Applications Environment administrator, the administrator password will not be required. Also, the Reset Fusion Environment Administrator Password API will be deprecated; to reset the password via API, you will need to use the appropriate Fusion Applications API instead.

**Date Announced:** August 27, 2024

**Date in Effect:** August 27, 2025

**Details:**Starting August 27, 2025, You will no longer be able to create or reset the Fusion Applications Administrator password using Fusion Applications Environment Management APIs. Impacted APIs are:

| API | Change |
| --- | --- |
| ResetFusionEnvironmentPassword | This API is deprecated. In one year it will be removed. Do not use this API to reset the Fusion Applications Administrator password. |
| CreateFusionEnvironment<br><br>CreateFusionEnvironmentAdminUser | In object CreateFusionEnvironmentAdminUserDetails, the password parameter is currently optional. In one year, the parameter will be removed from these APIs. |

**Does this impact me?** If you are using the Fusion Applications Environment Management API to reset the Fusion Applications Administrator password, you will need to use the appropriate Fusion Applications API instead.

If you are using the API to create the Fusion Applications Administrator, the password will no longer be accepted. Instead, the Administrator user will enter their own password after receiving a welcome email.

The Console workflow is not impacted.

**What do I need to do?**

If you are calling the ResetFusionEnvironmentPassword, you must update your scripts to use the Fusion Applications API [Update a User](https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/applications-common/22d/farca/op-hcmrestapi-scim-users-id-patch.html)
.

If you are calling CreateFusionEnvironment or CreateFusionEnvironmentAdminUser, do not pass the password parameter. The user will be prompted to add their password via the welcome email.

Generative AI
-------------

[Deprecated APIs in Generative AI Inference API](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/servicechanges.htm#)

**Service Change:** The `GenerateText` and `SummarizeText` APIs are deprecated.

**Date Announced:**June 26, 2025

**Date in Effect:** June 26, 2026

**Details:** Starting 26th June 2025, the `GenerateText` API and `SummarizeText` API in the Generative AI Inference API are deprecated. From 26th June 2026 onwards the deprecated APIs will be unavailable.

| Deprecated | Replacement |
| --- | --- |
| The following APIs in the Generative AI Inference API are deprecated:<br><br>*   [GenerateText](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/api/#%23/en/generative-ai-inference/latest/GenerateTextResult/GenerateText)<br>    <br>*   [SummarizeText](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/api/#%23/en/generative-ai-inference/latest/SummarizeTextResult/SummarizeText) | Use the [Chat](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/api/#%23/en/generative-ai-inference/latest/ChatResult/Chat)<br> API in the Generative AI Inference API. |

Generative AI Agents
--------------------

[Deprecated knowledgeBaseIds Property](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/servicechanges.htm#)

**Service Change:** The `knowledgeBaseIds` property for a RAG agent is deprecated.

**Date Announced:** March 26, 2025

**Date in Effect:** March 26, 2026

**Details:** Starting March 26, 2025, the `knowledgeBaseIds` property in `Agent`, `AgentSummary`, `CreateAgentDetails`, and `UpdateAgentDetails` is deprecated. From March 26, 2026, onwards the deprecated property will be unavailable.

| Deprecated | Replacement |
| --- | --- |
| The `knowledgeBaseIds` property for a RAG agent is deprecated in the following Management API and data types:<br><br>*   [Agent](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/api/#%23/en/generative-ai-agents/latest/Agent/)<br>     API<br>*   [AgentSummary](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/api/#%23/en/generative-ai-agents/latest/datatypes/AgentSummary)<br>     data type<br>*   [CreateAgentDetails](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/api/#%23/en/generative-ai-agents/latest/datatypes/CreateAgentDetails)<br>     data type<br>*   [UpdateAgentDetails](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/api/#%23/en/generative-ai-agents/latest/datatypes/UpdateAgentDetails)<br>     data type | Use the required `knowledgeBaseConfigs` property of the new data type `RagToolConfig`.<br><br>`RagToolConfig` is one of the tool types of the data type `ToolConfig` in the new Management API `Tool`.<br><br>See the following:<br><br>[RagToolConfig](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/api/#%23/en/generative-ai-agents/latest/datatypes/RagToolConfig)<br> data type<br><br>[ToolConfig](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/api/#%23/en/generative-ai-agents/latest/datatypes/ToolConfig)<br> data type<br><br>[Tool](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/api/#%23/en/generative-ai-agents/latest/Tool/)<br> API |

Globally Distributed AI Database
--------------------------------

[Deprecated Certificate Management APIs for Globally Distributed Autonomous AI Database](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/servicechanges.htm#)

**Service Change:** The following GSM certificate management APIs will be deprecated when the simplified TLS workflow goes GA in Globally Distributed Autonomous AI Database.

*   GenerateDistributedAutonomousDatabaseGsmCertificateSigningRequest
*   DownloadDistributedAutonomousDatabaseGsmCertificateSigningRequest
*   UploadDistributedAutonomousDatabaseSignedCertificateAndGenerateWallet

**Date Announced:** March 11, 2026

**Date in Effect:** March 11, 2027

**Details:** Due to changes in Autonomous AI Database security requirements, the TLS workflow was adjusted in the Globally Distributed Autonomous AI Database TLS workflow. The APIs GenerateDistributedAutonomousDatabaseGsmCertificateSigningRequest, DownloadDistributedAutonomousDatabaseGsmCertificateSigningRequest, and are no longer required as part of this workflow, and are deprecated.

**Does this impact me?** After provisioning Globally Distributed Autonomous AI Database using CreateDistributedAutonomousDatabase, the next step is to call API ConfigureDistributedAutonomousDatabaseSharding with two new parameters, certificateId and caBundleId, which specify the OCIDs of the certificate and CA bundle which are deployed on Autonomous AI Database clusters.

**What do I need to do?** When creating a new distributed database, discontinue using the deprecated APIs, and instead pass the appropriate OCIDs with certificateId and caBundleId parameters in ConfigureDistributedAutonomousDatabaseSharding.

When adding shards to the existing distributed database, after PatchDistributedAutonomousDatabase operation, simply call ConfigureDistributedAutonomousDatabaseSharding the same way you do now. There is no need to supply certificateId and caBundleId parameters after a distributed database is initially created with these parameters.

[Desupported Globally Distributed AI Database APIs](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/servicechanges.htm#)

OCI's Globally Distributed AI Database API version 20230301 reaches end-of-life.

**Date Announced:** May 7, 2025

**Date in Effect:** May 8, 2025

**Details:** Starting from May 8, 2025, the Globally Distributed AI Database API version 20230301 reaches end-of-life. On May 8, 2025 the 20230301 API is replaced with API version 20250101, which provides enhanced features and support for current and future releases.

**Does this impact me?**

*   If you're a new customer and have never used our service before, this change doesn't impact you.
*   If you're an existing customer using the 20230301 APIs, you must update your scripts to use the 20250101 APIs.

[Deprecated peerCloudAutonomousVmClusterIds and peerVmClusterIds attributes](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/servicechanges.htm#)

The peerCloudAutonomousVmClusterIds attribute is deprecated in CreateDistributedAutonomousDatabaseCatalogWithDedicatedInfraDetails and CreateDistributedAutonomousDatabaseShardWithDedicatedInfraDetails, and the peerVmClusterIds attribute is deprecated in CreateDistributedDatabaseCatalogWithExadbXsDetails and CreateDistributedDatabaseShardWithExadbXsDetails.

**Date Announced:** November 5, 2025

**Date in Effect:** November 5, 2025

**Details:**

Starting from November 5, 2025, the Globally Distributed AI Database API peerCloudAutonomousVmClusterIds attribute for data types CreateDistributedAutonomousDatabaseCatalogWithDedicatedInfraDetails and CreateDistributedAutonomousDatabaseShardWithDedicatedInfraDetails is deprecated.

The Data Guard standby addition/removal for Globally Distributed AI Database and Globally Distributed Autonomous AI Database shards and catalog was done using attributes peerCloudAutonomousVmClusterIds and peerVmClusterIds. Any operation using these attributes should be updated with the replacement attribute, peerDetails.

**Does this impact me?** Yes, if you have code that references the attribute, peerCloudAutonomousVmClusterIds or peerVmClusterIds.

**What do I need to do?** Update your code to reference the new property, peerDetails.

GoldenGate
----------

[Deprecation of Trail Files and Trail Sequences Listing APIs in GoldenGate Service](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/servicechanges.htm#)

**Service change**: The /trailFiles (ListTrailFiles) and /trailSequences (ListTrailSequences) APIs will be retired.

**Date announced**: April 30, 2026

**Date in effect**: April 30, 2027

**Details**: Starting April 30, 2027, the /trailFiles (ListTrailFiles) and /trailSequences (ListTrailSequences) APIs will be retired. These APIs currently allow customers to retrieve trail file and trail sequence metadata for a given GoldenGate deployment.

As part of the evolution of Oracle GoldenGate (starting from version 23ai), trail file management capabilities were moved to the OCI GoldenGate deployment console. Customers must now use the deployment console to access and manage trail file and sequence information instead of relying on these REST endpoints.

After the retirement date, these APIs will no longer be available and any requests made to them will fail.

**Does this impact me?** Yes

**What do I need to do?** Customers should update their workflows and integrations to stop using the deprecated /trailFiles and /trailSequences APIs. Instead, they must use the Oracle GoldenGate 23ai (or later) deployment console to perform trail file and trail sequence management operations.

[Deprecation of automatic defaulting to REDIRECT session mode for OracleConnection](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/servicechanges.htm#)

**Service change**: OracleConnection will stop defaulting to REDIRECT session mode automatically.

**Date announced**: April 21, 2026

**Date in effect**: April 21, 2027

**Details**: When creating Oracle Database connections, the `sessionMode` property was defaulted to `REDIRECT` for RAC databases when `databaseId` was provided. Going forward, this automatic defaulting will be removed.

**Does this impact me?** Yes

**What do I need to do?** Customers should explicitly set the `sessionMode` value in their configurations instead of relying on defaults when `databaseId` is provided for new connections. No changes are required for the configuration of existing connections.

[Amazon Kinesis connections' property to become mandatory field](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/servicechanges.htm#)

**Service change**: The Region property of Amazon Kinesis connections will become mandatory.

**Date announced**: July 2025

**Date in effect**: July 2026

**Details**: Starting July 2026, the Region property of Amazon Kinesis connections will be a mandatory field.

**Does this impact me?** Yes, if you have Amazon Kinesis connections that don't currently have a value assigned for Region.

**What do I need to do?** When you create new Amazon Kinesis connections, ensure that you provide a value for Region. For existing Amazon Kinesis connections, ensure that you edit the connection properties and provide a value for Region.

[Amazon S3 connections' property to become mandatory field](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/servicechanges.htm#)

**Service change**: The Region property of Amazon S3 connections will become mandatory as of June 10, 2026.

**Date announced**: June 10, 2025

**Date in effect**: June 10, 2026

**Details**: Starting June 10, 2026, the Region property of Amazon S3 connections will be a mandatory field.

**Does this impact me?** Yes, if you have Amazon S3 connections that don't currently have a value assigned for Region.

**What do I need to do?** When you create new Amazon S3 connections, ensure that you provide a value for Region. For existing Amazon S3 connections, ensure that you edit the connection properties and provide a value for Region.

For more information about Amazon S3 connections with GoldenGate, see [Connect to Amazon S3](https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/paas/goldengate-service/gasfd/index.html)
.

[Deprecated GoldenGate Database Registration APIs](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/servicechanges.htm#)

**Service change**: Oracle Cloud Infrastructure's GoldenGate APIs for `DatabaseRegistrations` are deprecated as of November 01, 2022.

**Date announced**: November 01, 2022

**Date in effect**: November 01, 2023

**Details**: Starting November 01, 2022, `DatabaseRegistrations` APIs are deprecated and replaced by `Connections` APIs. From November 01, 2023, the deprecated APIs will be unavailable.

**Does this impact me?** Yes. `DatabaseRegistrations` APIs worked for Oracle Database connections, whereas the new, extensible `Connections` APIs let you connect to many other types of data technologies.

**What do I need to do?** Use the `Connections` APIs instead of the `DatabaseRegistrations` APIs to connect to the source and target technologies.

[Deprecated timeUpgradeRequired Property](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/servicechanges.htm#)

**Service change**: The `timeUpgradeRequired` property of the `Deployment` and `DeploymentSummary` APIs are deprecated as of March 14, 2023.

**Date announced**: March 14, 2023

**Date in effect**: March 14, 2024

**Details**: With the new Maintenance features rolled out on March 14 2023, the `timeUpgradeRequired` property of the `Deployment` and `DeploymentSummary` APIs are deprecated.

**Does this impact me?** The read-only `timeUpgradeRequired` property was used to help you determine how long you had to manually upgrade to a new deployment version, but the service didn't automatically upgrade your deployment when the deadline passed. The new Maintenance feature schedules one or more upgrades and automatically upgrades your deployment on the given date. You can find these dates on your deployment details page.

**What do I need to do?** You can adjust the scheduled upgrades as needed when you create the deployment, or from the deployment details page.

[Deprecated adminPassword Property](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/servicechanges.htm#)

**Service change:** The `adminPassword` property used in `CreateOggDeploymentDetails` and `UpdateOggDeploymentDetails` model objects of the `CreateDeploymentDetails` and `UpdateDeploymentDetails` APIs were deprecated as of August 15, 2023.

**Date announced:** August 15, 2023

**Date in effect:** August 15, 2024

**Details:** With the new single-sign on feature introduced on August 15, 2023, the `adminPassword` property used in `CreateOggDeploymentDetails` and `UpdateOggDeploymentDetails` model objects of the `CreateDeploymentDetails` and `UpdateDeploymentDetails` APIs are now deprecated.

**Does this impact me?** Yes.

**What do I need to do?** New deployments created from August 15, 2023, require you to select a Credential store (OCI Identity and Access Management (IAM) or GoldenGate) in tenancies where OCI IAM with Identity Domains is enabled. If you select OCI IAM, you can log in to the deployment console using your Oracle Cloud account, whereas GoldenGate requires you to create a Vault and add a secret in which to store your password, that you'll use to log in to the deployment console with.

[Deprecated privateIP Property](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/servicechanges.htm#)

**Service change:** The `privateIp` property of all `CreateConnectionDetails` model objects in the Connection APIs is deprecated as of December 5, 2023.

**Date announced:** December 5, 2023

**Date in effect:** December 5, 2024

**Details:** With the release of updated network options, the `privateIp` property of all `CreateConnectionDetails` model objects in the Connection APIs is deprecated as of December 5, 2023.

**Does this impact me?** You can continue to use your old connections, however, you must update them if you previously provided a Private IP value. Any new connections you create December 5, 2023 and onward will use new Network connectivity settings that you select

**What do I need to do?** Edit any existing connection where you provided a Private IP value, enter the private IP in the Host name or connection string field, and then save your changes. If you provide a host name, GoldenGate forwards the DNS resolution to your subnet. If you provide a `privateIp`, GoldenGate connects to it directly.

[Deprecated OGG property value](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/servicechanges.htm#)

**Service change:** The `CreateDeploymentDetails` payload `deploymentType` property value `OGG` is deprecated and replaced by `DATABASE_ORACLE`.

**Date announced:** June 5, 2024

**Date in effect:** June 5, 2025

**Details:** As OCI GoldenGate expands support for different types of database technologies and introduces new deployment types, it became necessary to rename the `deploymentType` property value from `OGG` to `DATABASE_ORACLE`.

**Does this impact me?** If you previously created a deployment using the property value `OGG`, you won't be affected, as the `OGG` value will be migrated to `DATABASE_ORACLE`. However, if you used the `OGG` value in code for some other purpose (like comparisions) you may be affected.

**What do I need to do?** Going forward, to create a new OCI GoldenGate deployment for Oracle Database, ensure that you use the `deploymentType` property value `DATABASE_ORACLE` instead of the deprecated value `OGG`.

[loadBalancerSubnetId property to become mandatory](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/servicechanges.htm#)

**Service change:** When the `isPublic` property of `CreateDeploymentDetails` or `UpdateDeploymentDetails` payloads is set to true, then the `loadBalancerSubnetId` property is mandatory.

**Date announced:** June 5, 2024

**Date in effect:** June 5, 2025

**Details:** When you create or update a public deployment using `CreateDeploymentDetails` or `UpdateDeploymentDetails` and set the `isPublic` to true, then the `loadBalancerSubnetId` property is mandatory, and you must provide a valid public subnet OCID.

**Does this impact me?** This impacts you if you set the `isPublic` property to true when creating a deployment using `CreateDeploymentDetails` or `UpdateDeploymentDetails` APIs, and if you have existing public deployments.

**What do I need to do?** When you set the `isPublic property` of the `CreateDeploymentDetails` or `UpdateDeploymentDetails` APIs to true, then the `loadBalancerSubnetId` property becomes mandatory, and you must provide a valid public subnet OCID. For existing public deployments, you must edit the deployment and select a `loadBalancerSubnetId`. You can edit the deployment using the Oracle Cloud console to make the selection or use the API to update existing public deployments to provide a valid subnet OCID for `loadBalancerSubnetId`.

[Connections using SHARED\_SERVICE\_ENDPOINT deprecated](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/servicechanges.htm#)

**Service change:** All existing connections that use `SHARED_SERVICE_ENDPOINT` in the API or 'Oracle Network' in the Oracle Cloud Console as the routing method must be updated.

**Date announced:** June 5, 2024

**Date in effect:** June 5, 2025

**Details:** Existing connections that use the `SHARED_SERVICE_ENDPOINT` in the API or 'Oracle Network' in the Oracle Cloud Console as the routing method must be updated

**Does this impact me?** Yes, if you are using connection with 'Oracle Network' (UI) or `SHARED_SERVICE_ENDPOINT` (API)

**What do I need to do?** Update all connections that use the `SHARED_SERVICE_ENDPOINT` in the API or 'Oracle Network' in the Oracle Cloud Console. In the Console, make a new selection for Traffic routing method when you edit using the Console, and then save your changes, You can also update the property with any client, SDK, Terraform, or CLI.

[Plain Text Fields in Connections](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/servicechanges.htm#)

**Service change:** Plain text fields in connections

**Date announced:** February 11, 2025

**Date in effect:** February 11, 2026

**Details:** Refrain from using plain-text fields in Connections for sensitive attributes, such as passwords, private keys, wallet contents, access keys, and other credentials. Sensitive attributes should be stored in OCI Vault Secrets and referenced from GoldenGate connections by using the corresponding Secret OCID fields, such as passwordSecretId, walletSecretId, privateKeyFileSecretId, privateKeyPassphraseSecretId, and other \*SecretId fields.

The legacy connection encryption flow that uses plain-text sensitive attributes together with vaultId and keyId is deprecated. The doesUseSecretIds field is also deprecated because Secret OCID fields are the expected mechanism for providing sensitive attributes. When creating or updating connections, provide the individual \*SecretId fields instead of plain-text sensitive fields, and do not rely on vaultId/keyId to encrypt newly supplied sensitive connection attributes.

**Does this impact me?** Yes

**What do I need to do?** Beginning February 11, 2025, use OCI Vault Secrets for sensitive attributes when creating new connections. Create the required secrets in OCI Vault, configure policies so GoldenGate and assigned deployments can access them, and provide the corresponding Secret OCIDs in the connection's \*SecretId fields. Update automation, SDK, CLI, Terraform, and API integrations to stop sending plain-text sensitive fields, vaultId/keyId-based encryption inputs, and doesUseSecretIds fornew connection definitions.

[GoldenGate Service Principal Deprecated](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/servicechanges.htm#)

**Service change**: The service principal 'goldengate' is deprecated and replaced by resource principal, 'dynamic-group goldengate-deployments'.

**Date announced**: February 24, 2025

**Date in effect**: June 2025

**Details**: Create a dynamic group, such as 'goldengate-deployments', and update all existing policies that use the service principal 'goldengate' with the dynamic group. When you add new policies, ensure that you use the same dynamic group name.

**Does this impact me?** Yes.

**What do I need to do?** Beginning February 24, 2025, you must create a dynamic group, such as 'goldengate-deployments', and use this resource principal in place of the service principal 'goldengate' for all new and existing policies. See [Minimum recommended policies](https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/paas/goldengate-service/rmrrr/#GUID-3E101995-8630-4D58-93DF-0AE94041B031)
 for details.

GoldenGate Cloud Service Classic
--------------------------------

[GoldenGate Cloud Service Classic End of Life](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/servicechanges.htm#)

**Service change**: End of Life for GoldenGate Cloud Service Classic effective April 11, 2024.

**Date announced**: March 22, 2024

**Date in effect**: April 11, 2024

**Details**: Effective April 11, 2024, Oracle GoldenGate Cloud Service Classic will reach End of Life (EOL). Once the service reaches EOL:

*   You cannot create new instances of Oracle GoldenGate Cloud Service Classic.
*   Oracle will no longer support GoldenGate Cloud Service Classic.

**Does this impact me?** Oracle GoldenGate Cloud Service Classic runs on Oracle Cloud Classic Gen 1, which has been deprecated in favor of Oracle Cloud Infrastructure (OCI) Gen 2 Cloud. If you are an Oracle GoldenGate Cloud Service Classic user, you can migrate your workloads off Oracle Cloud Classic Gen 1 to Oracle Cloud Infrastructure GoldenGate.

If you are currently an OCI GoldenGate user, this service change announcement doesn't apply to you.

**What do I need to do?** Migrate Oracle GoldenGate Cloud Service workloads to Oracle Cloud Infrastructure GoldenGate, which offers similar functionality. See [Migrating to Oracle Cloud Infrastructure GoldenGate](https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/paas/goldengate-service/migrating/index.html)
 for the detailed migration steps.

Internet of Things
------------------

[Deprecated APIs in Internet of Things API](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/servicechanges.htm#)

**Service Change:** Two values for the `type` attribute from the `IotDomainGroup` API are deprecated.

**Date Announced:** April 14, 2026

**Date in Effect:** April 14, 2026

**Details:** The `LIGHTWEIGHT` and `STANDARD` values for the type attribute in the `IotDomainGroup` API are getting depreciated and will be replaced with these new values:

*   `DEVELOPMENT`: Uses fewer resources and has a higher Recovery Time Objective (RTO), making it suitable for development and testing, the default domain group type.
*   `PRODUCTION`: Turn on in the console or define in the command when you [create an IoT domain group](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/internet-of-things/create-domain-group.htm)
    , recommended for production workloads.

**Does this impact me?** If you created an IoT domain group using `LIGHTWEIGHT` or `STANDARD` values then make sure you [create a new IoT domain group](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/internet-of-things/create-domain-group.htm)
 using one of the new values before the effective deprecation date. If you created an IoT domain group after April 14, 2026 it's a `DEVELOPMENT` or `PRODUCTION` IoT domain group. The default domain group type is `DEVELOPMENT`. To confirm the `type` [get an IoT domain group details](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/internet-of-things/get-domain-group-details.htm)
.

**What do I need to do?** [Create a new IoT domain group](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/internet-of-things/create-domain-group.htm)
 that uses one of the new `DEVELOPMENT` or `PRODUCTION` IoT domain group types. `LIGHTWEIGHT` and `STANDARD` domain groups will continue to work until April 14, 2027. On **April 14, 2027** the deprecated values for the `IotDomainGroup` API will be unavailable and domain groups using these attributes will no longer work.

| Deprecated | Replacement |
| --- | --- |
| The following values for the `type` attribute are deprecated aliases for IoT domain groups in the Internet of Things API:<br><br>*   `LIGHTWEIGHT`<br>*   `STANDARD` | Use these values for the `type` attribute of a domain group in the Internet of Things API:<br><br>*   `DEVELOPMENT`: Uses fewer resources and has a higher Recovery Time Objective (RTO), making it suitable for development and testing.<br>*   `PRODUCTION`: Recommended for production workloads. |

Kubernetes Engine
-----------------

[CreateCluster API endpointConfig.subnetId attribute is required, new Kubernetes clusters must be VCN-native](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/servicechanges.htm#)

**Service Change:** The previously optional `subnetId` attribute of the `endpointConfig` attribute from the Oracle Cloud Infrastructure's [CreateCluster](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/api/#/en/containerengine/latest/Cluster/CreateCluster)
 API is now required.

**Date Announced:** October 8, 2025

**Date in Effect:** October 28, 2026

**Details:** In earlier releases of Kubernetes Engine, clusters were provisioned with public Kubernetes API endpoints that were not integrated into your VCN. After March 2021, you could not use the Console to create such clusters. Instead, clusters you create using the Console are always completely integrated with your VCN (known as "VCN-native clusters"). In a VCN-native cluster, worker nodes, load balancers, and the Kubernetes API endpoint are part of your VCN, and you can configure them as public or private.

Prior to this Service Change, you could continue to use the CLI and the API to create clusters provisioned with public Kubernetes API endpoints and not integrated into your VCN. You could continue to use the CLI and the API to create such clusters because the `endpointConfig.subnetId` attribute was optional. Note that you could only create these clusters as basic clusters, not as enhanced clusters.

After this Service Change, the `endpointConfig.subnetId` attribute is required. As a result, you can only create Kubernetes clusters that are completely integrated with your VCN (known as "VCN-native clusters").

**Does this impact me?** If you have been using the [CreateCluster](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/api/#/en/containerengine/latest/Cluster/CreateCluster)
 API to create clusters provisioned with public Kubernetes API endpoints that are not integrated into your VCN, this impacts you. From September 30, 2026, any call to the [CreateCluster](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/api/#/en/containerengine/latest/Cluster/CreateCluster)
 API that does not set the `endpointConfig.subnetId` attribute will fail because it is missing the required attribute.

**What do I need to do?** If you have custom scripts or Terraform scripts that are using the [CreateCluster](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/api/#/en/containerengine/latest/Cluster/CreateCluster)
 API but not setting the `endpointConfig.subnetId` attribute, you must update such scripts to set the `endpointConfig.subnetId` attribute (and thus create "VCN-native clusters").

[Support for new Oracle Linux 7 (OL7) Arm (aarch64) Kubernetes worker nodes deactivated](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/servicechanges.htm#)

**Service Change:** Kubernetes Engine has deactivated the capability to create new Oracle Linux 7 (OL7) Arm (aarch64) worker nodes.

**Date Announced:** December 2, 2025

**Date in Effect:** January 16, 2026

**Details:** Oracle Linux 7 (OL7) support for Arm (aarch64) ended on January 1, 2025 and is not covered by OL7 Extended Support. To align with this upstream status, Kubernetes Engine has deactivated the capability to create new OL7 Arm worker nodes. Kubernetes Engine no longer builds or ships new OKE OL7 Arm images, and no longer supports OL7 Arm platform images for creating new worker nodes.

Existing clusters and nodes that already use OL7 Arm images will continue to run. However, those operating systems will not receive upstream security patches or bug fixes.

This change does not affect Kubernetes Engine control planes and applies only to worker node OS images on Arm. For more information, see [Oracle Linux 7 (ARM) support ended - actions for OKE customers](https://blogs.oracle.com/cloud-infrastructure/oracle-linux-7-arm-support-ended-actions-for-oke)
.

**Does this impact me?** This change affects customers running Kubernetes worker nodes on OL7 Arm (aarch64), whether managed through Kubernetes Engine node pools or self-managed nodes. It also impacts any plans to create new worker nodes from OL7 Arm images after the effective date. Customers using other architectures or already on OL8 Arm are not impacted by the deactivation of new OL7 Arm node creation.

**What do I need to do?** If you are running Kubernetes worker nodes on OL7 Arm (aarch64) or intend to create new worker nodes from OL7 Arm images, begin planning and executing your migration to OL8 Arm so that you maintain a supported and secure posture. For more information, see [Oracle Linux 7 (ARM) support ended - actions for OKE customers](https://blogs.oracle.com/cloud-infrastructure/oracle-linux-7-arm-support-ended-actions-for-oke)
.

[Type of new cluster defaults to ENHANCED\_CLUSTER instead of BASIC\_CLUSTER when using the CLI or API](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/servicechanges.htm#)

**Service Change:** When creating a new cluster using the CLI or API, the cluster type now defaults to `ENHANCED_CLUSTER` instead of `BASIC_CLUSTER`.

**Date Announced:** June 15, 2026

**Date in Effect:** June 15, 2027

**Details:** Prior to this Service Change, when using the CLI or the API to create a new cluster, if you did not specify the type of cluster to create, the cluster was created as a basic cluster.

After this Service Change, when using the CLI or the API to create a new cluster, if you do not specify the type of cluster to create, the cluster is created as an enhanced cluster.

Note the following:

*   This change only applies to the creation of new clusters using the CLI or API. There is no impact on the behavior of existing basic and enhanced clusters, and no impact on cluster update operations.
*   Basic clusters continue to be supported. You can continue to specify that you want to create a new basic cluster by explicitly setting the cluster's type as `BASIC_CLUSTER`.
*   Existing Console behavior is unaffected by this change. When using the Console to create a new cluster, the cluster is created as an enhanced cluster by default, unless you explicitly choose to create a basic cluster.

**Does this impact me?** This change only impacts you if you create basic clusters by:

*   Using the [CreateCluster](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/api/#/en/containerengine/latest/Cluster/CreateCluster)
     API and not explicitly setting the `CreateClusterDetails.type` attribute to `BASIC_CLUSTER`.
*   Using the [oci ce cluster create](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/tools/oci-cli/latest/oci_cli_docs/cmdref/ce/cluster/create.html)
     command and not explicitly setting the `--type` parameter to `BASIC_CLUSTER`.

From June 15, 2027, any use of the [CreateCluster](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/api/#/en/containerengine/latest/Cluster/CreateCluster)
 API or the [oci ce cluster create](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/tools/oci-cli/latest/oci_cli_docs/cmdref/ce/cluster/create.html)
 command that does not explicitly set the cluster's type as `BASIC_CLUSTER` will create an enhanced cluster.

This change does not impact you:

*   If you use the CLI, or the API to create clusters, and you explicitly specify the cluster's type as `BASIC_CLUSTER`.
*   If you use the CLI, or the API to create clusters, and you explicitly specify the cluster's type as `ENHANCED_CLUSTER`.
*   If you use the Console to create clusters. Enhanced clusters continue to be created by default, unless you explicitly choose to create a basic cluster.

**What do I need to do?**

*   If you have custom scripts or Terraform scripts that are using the [CreateCluster](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/api/#/en/containerengine/latest/Cluster/CreateCluster)
     API to create basic clusters but are not explicitly setting the `CreateClusterDetails.type` attribute to `BASIC_CLUSTER`, and you want to continue to create basic clusters rather than enhanced clusters, you must update such scripts to set the `CreateClusterDetails.type` attribute to `BASIC_CLUSTER`.
*   If you have custom scripts or Terraform scripts that are using the [oci ce cluster create](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/tools/oci-cli/latest/oci_cli_docs/cmdref/ce/cluster/create.html)
     command to create basic clusters but are not explicitly setting the `--type` parameter to `BASIC_CLUSTER`, and you want to continue to create basic clusters rather than enhanced clusters, you must update such scripts to set the `--type` parameter to `BASIC_CLUSTER`.

MySQL HeatWave
--------------

[DB System Backup Retention Period Reduction](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/servicechanges.htm#)

**Service Change:** The DB System backup retention period has been reduced from 10,000 to 365 days.

**Date Announced:** September 2020

**Date in Effect:** October 2020

**Details:** The DB System backup retention period has been reduced from 10,000 to 365 days.

**Does this impact me?** No.

**What do I need to do?** Nothing.

[DB System Deletion Policy](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/servicechanges.htm#)

**Service Change:** The default value of `AutomaticBackupRetention` is changed from DELETE to RETAIN.

**Date Announced:** January 2024

**Date in Effect:** January 2025

**Details:** Before this service change, the `AutomaticBackupRetention` attribute default value in DB System Deletion Policy was DELETE. With this change, the default value of `AutomaticBackupRetention` is changed to RETAIN. Note that this change doesn't impact the deletion policy of existing DB Systems. The change is solely applicable to DB Systems created after the date in effect.

**Does this impact me?** Yes, if you use defaults for deletion policy.

**What do I need to do?** If you prefer the default to be DELETE for `AutomaticBackupRetention` and are using SDK/CLI/Terraform without the value being set, you must set the preferred value explicitly.

[Deprecated OCPU Shapes](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/servicechanges.htm#)

**Service Change:** The OCPU shapes are deprecated from September 5, 2024 onwards.

**Date Announced:** September 5, 2024

**Date in Effect:** September 5, 2024

**Details:**

Starting from September 5, 2024, all HeatWave OCPU shapes for DB systems and HeatWave clusters are deprecated. However, customers can use ECPU shapes to deploy new DB systems and HeatWave clusters. The OCPU shapes will not be available to new customers while existing customers can continue to use OCPU shapes in new and existing DB systems and HeatWave clusters until September 5, 2025.

**Does this impact me?**

*   Yes, if you have existing DB systems that are deployed with OCPU shapes.

**What do I need to do?**

Use ECPU to deploy new DB systems and HeatWave clusters. For existing DB systems and HeatWave clusters running on OCPU shapes, you must plan an appropriate time to convert them to ECPU shapes before September 5, 2025.

IAM
---

[Oracle Public Cloud Service Apps OAuth Client Secrets Visibility Change](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/servicechanges.htm#)

To strengthen security, the OCI Console will no longer display OAuth client secrets for OPC Apps (Oracle Public Cloud Service Applications) in Identity Domains.

**Note**  
  

This change applies only to applications listed under Oracle Cloud Services.

Customer-created applications under Integrated Applications are not affected.

Customers who use OPC Apps OAuth client secrets in their custom integrations should assess the impact and update their applications accordingly. SaaS-PaaS Applications protected with Identity Domains will not be affected and can continue to access their applications without interruption.

Below are frequently asked questions for Identity and Service Integration customers.

#### 1\. What is changing on June 8, 2026?

After June 8, 2026, OAuth client secrets for OPC Apps listed under Oracle Cloud Services will no longer be visible in the OCI Console.

*   9 June – APAC Regions
*   11 June – EMEA Regions
*   16 June – South America Regions
*   18 and 23 June – North America Regions

#### 2\. Will previously retrieved secrets stop working on June 8?

No. For Oracle-provisioned applications under Oracle Cloud Services, previously retrieved secrets will be rotated gradually by the Oracle service team. Once a secret is rotated, the old secret will no longer work.

You should migrate your workload to custom OAuth client applications (Integrated Apps) as early as possible. When a secret is rotated and your workload continues to depend on Oracle-provisioned applications under Oracle Cloud Services, the workload will stop working immediately.

#### 3\. Will customers be notified again before secret rotation?

No. There will be no further communication regarding the execution of this OPC Apps OAuth client secrets rotation from the Identity Cloud Service team.

Secret rotation will occur gradually by Oracle service team. Customers should migrate their workloads to custom OAuth client applications as soon as possible. Once a secret is rotated, any workload that depends on OPC Apps OAuth client secrets provisioned under Oracle Cloud Services will stop working immediately.

#### 4\. Will my application break?

No impact is expected for Oracle-managed connected services.

However, if your custom application or workload uses a client secret obtained from an Oracle-provisioned Oracle Cloud Services application, the secret will be rotated and the existing secret will no longer work. To maintain connectivity, recreate the customer-owned integrated/confidential application and update your workload with the new Client ID and Client Secret.

#### 5\. Is Connectivity Agent impacted?

No. Connected applications are expected to continue functioning normally. Oracle service teams will continue to access and use the required credentials internally.

However, if a customer has copied and reused a client secret from an Oracle-provisioned application for a custom integration, that integration should be migrated to a customer-owned Integrated/Confidential application. This ensures continued operation after the Oracle-provisioned secret is rotated.

#### 6\. Are Integrated Applications affected?

No. Customer-created applications under Integrated Applications are not affected.

#### 7\. Are customer-created OAuth clients affected?

No. Customer-created OAuth clients or confidential applications are not affected by this update.

#### 8\. Does the June 8, 2026, date apply to all realms?

No. June 8, 2026, applies to OC1. Dates for other realms will be communicated separately.

#### 9\. How does a customer create a replacement application?

Customers should create a confidential application under Integrated Applications, configure the required OAuth settings, and record the client ID and client secret when the application is created. They should then activate the application.

Oracle's confidential application documentation includes the creation workflow. Secrets for customer-created confidential applications do not expire automatically; customers should rotate them according to their security policies or when explicitly required. Here is the documentation reference: Adding a Confidential Application

#### 10\. Why is Oracle making this change?

This change is a security enhancement designed to reduce the exposure of OPC Apps OAuth client secrets.

Customers should use customer-owned Integrated Applications and manage their own credentials for custom workloads, rather than relying on credentials for Oracle-provisioned applications.

#### 11\. What should customers do?

Customers should review whether any custom workload, script, integration, job, agent, or third-party tool uses a client secret copied from an Oracle-provisioned application under Oracle Cloud Services. If so, they should create their own confidential application under Integrated Applications and use its OAuth client ID and client secret for their custom integrations.

#### What Next?

If customers have a use case that requires OPC Apps OAuth client secrets for their integrations to work and they can't follow the recommendation, we recommend engaging the appropriate service integration and/or Identity Cloud Service team to review the use case and provide guidance.

Please refer to the Release Notes for additional details about this change.

[Managing IDCS Federated Resources in the OCI Console Is Being Deprecated](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/servicechanges.htm#)

**Date Announced:** March, 18th 2026

**Date in Effect:** April, 30th 2026

**Details:**

This is to inform you of an important update regarding Oracle Cloud Infrastructure (OCI) Identity and Access Management (IAM) support for managing Identity Cloud Service (IDCS) federated resources through the OCI Console.

We will deprecate support for managing IDCS federated users, IDCS federated groups, and other IDCS federated resources (see attached list for deprecated APIs) through the Federation Provider details page of the OCI Console on April, 30th 2026. You can use the alternative options that are already available (see [FAQ](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/servicechanges.htm#idcs-federated-resources-oci-console-deprecation__faq)
). See [List of Deprecated APIs](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/servicechanges.htm#idcs-federated-resources-oci-console-deprecation__list-deprecated-apis)
 for the full list of APIs and OCI Console [IDCS Federation UI](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/servicechanges.htm#idcs-federated-resources-oci-console-deprecation__identity-deprecated-my-idcs-federation)
 page being deprecated.

**FAQs**

**What's Changing?**

IDCS was unified with OCI IAM as OCI identity domains. Before this unification, when your tenancy had an OCI IAM federation with IDCS, you could manage IDCS users and IDCS groups from within the OCI Console by signing in by using the IDCS federation. With this change, to manage users and groups of a federated IDCS, you will need to sign in directly to the corresponding identity domain instead of using the My [IDCS Federation UI](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/servicechanges.htm#idcs-federated-resources-oci-console-deprecation__identity-deprecated-my-idcs-federation)
 in the OCI Console.

**What are the timelines for the deprecation of support for managing IDCS federated resources through the OCI Console?**

The deprecation will be done on April, 30th 2026

**Does this impact me?**

If you have been signing in to your OCI tenancy using an IDCS federation (and NOT signing in using an identity domain) and have been managing users and groups from the Federation > Identity Provider details page, you will no longer be able to do so after the deprecation. Instead, you can sign in directly to the corresponding identity domain or IDCS instance's Admin Console (<IDCS\_URL>/ui/v1/adminconsole) to manage users and groups.

**Who can I contact for support if I have additional questions or concerns?**

We understand that this transition may raise questions or concerns. Don't hesitate to reach out for support. You can contact Oracle Support using the chat agents in the OCI Console or through the [My Oracle Support](https://support.oracle.com/epmos/faces/MosIndex.jspx)
 portal.

**UI Changes**

The following screen will no longer be available.

[![Displays a screenshot of the My IDCS Federation that is used in IAM without Identity Domains.](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Identity/images/identity-my-idcs-federation.png "Click to expand")](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Identity/images/identity-my-idcs-federation.png)

**List of Deprecated APIs**

*   `console-idcs.<REGION>.oraclecloud.com/20181127/IdcsAppRoleGrants`
*   `console-idcs.<REGION>.oraclecloud.com/20181127/IdcsAppRoleGrants/{id}`
*   `console-idcs.<REGION>.oraclecloud.com/20181127/access`
*   `console-idcs.<REGION>.oraclecloud.com/20181127/accesstoken`
*   `console-idcs.<REGION>.oraclecloud.com/20181127/appgrants`
*   `console-idcs.<REGION>.oraclecloud.com/20181127/appgrants/bulk/`
*   `console-idcs.<REGION>.oraclecloud.com/20181127/appgrants/{id}`
*   `console-idcs.<REGION>.oraclecloud.com/20181127/approles`
*   `console-idcs.<REGION>.oraclecloud.com/20181127/approles/(id)`
*   `console-idcs.<REGION>.oraclecloud.com/20181127/apps`
*   `console-idcs.<REGION>.oraclecloud.com/20181127/apps/{id}`
*   `console-idcs.<REGION>.oraclecloud.com/20181127/groups/`
*   `console-idcs.<REGION>.oraclecloud.com/20181127/groups/{groupId}/members/{userId}`
*   `console-idcs.<REGION>.oraclecloud.com/20181127/groups/{id}`
*   `console-idcs.<REGION>.oraclecloud.com/20181127/groups/{id}/members`
*   `console-idcs.<REGION>.oraclecloud.com/20181127/idcs/instanceinfo`
*   `console-idcs.<REGION>.oraclecloud.com/20181127/me`
*   `console-idcs.<REGION>.oraclecloud.com/20181127/users`
*   `console-idcs.<REGION>.oraclecloud.com/20181127/users/{id}`
*   `console-idcs.<REGION>.oraclecloud.com/20181127/users/{id}/resetpassword`
*   `console-idcs.<REGION>.oraclecloud.com/20181127/users/{id}/validatepassword`

[Deprecation of DigiCert G1 Root and Intermediate CA certificates](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/servicechanges.htm#)

*   **Service Change:** Beginning in March 2026, Oracle Cloud Infrastructure Identity and Access Management (OCI IAM) will update the TLS certificates used for `*.identity.oraclecloud.com`. After this change, the service will use certificates issued by the DigiCert Global G2 TLS RSA SHA256 2020 certificate authority.
*   **Date Announced:** December, 2nd 2025
*   **Date in Effect:** March 2026
*   **Details:**
    
    **What's Changing?**
    
    Current certificates chaining to DigiCert TLS RSA SHA256 2020 CA1 (G1 Root) will be replaced with certificates issued under the DigiCert Global G2 TLS RSA SHA256 2020 CA1 (G2 Root). This change ensures IAM remains aligned with modern security best practices and industry standards.
    
    **Why Is This Change Needed?**
    
    DigiCert has announced that end-entity TLS certificates issued from certain G1 roots will no longer be trusted by major browsers, including Google Chrome and Mozilla Firefox. Transitioning to G2-based certificates ensures continued compatibility and robust security. For more details, please refer to the Additional Information section.
    
*   **Does this impact me?**
    
    Most operating systems, libraries, and browsers already trust the DigiCert Global G2 root, so most users don't need to take any action.
    
    However, you may be impacted if:
    
    *   Your systems or applications explicitly imported the previous certificate chain into a trust store, or
    *   You use custom or restricted trust stores that don't currently trust DigiCert Global G2.
*   **What do I need to do?**
    
    *   Review client applications, scripts, devices, and libraries connecting to OCI Identity Login, and ensure that DigiCert Global Root G2 and the relevant intermediate certificates are trusted. If you use standard, up-to-date platforms and libraries, no action is required.
    *   Test your applications to confirm continual connectivity following the certificate update.
*   **Additional Information**
    
    Per the [DigiCert announcement](https://knowledge.digicert.com/general-information/digicert-root-and-intermediate-ca-certificate-updates-2023)
    , Google and Mozilla have announced that, starting April 15, 2026, TLS certificates issued from impacted G1 roots will no longer be trusted by their browsers. As a result, websites and services using these certificates may encounter security warnings or other issues. Any certificate issued in CMC using the default G1 root will need to be reissued with the G2 (SHA-256 Root) option and installed to ensure continued trust and security. Please review your current certificates and plan to update them before the deadline to avoid service disruptions. If you need assistance or further support, please contact Oracle Support and raise a Service Request.
    

[End of Support for TLS\_RSA\_WITH\_AES\_128\_CBC\_SHA Cipher Suite](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/servicechanges.htm#)

**Date Announced:** July, 21, 2025

**Date in Effect:** October 21, 2025

**Details:**

OCI IAM is announcing the deprecation and planned removal of support for the weak TLS\_RSA\_WITH\_AES\_128\_CBC\_SHA SSL cipher suite. Following the effective date, any connections that attempt to negotiate this cipher suite will no longer be accepted.

While TLS\_RSA\_WITH\_AES\_128\_CBC\_SHA was a commonly used cipher suite in the past, it no longer meets current industry best practices and security standards. Its continued use presents several security risks, including lack of Perfect Forward Secrecy (PFS) and a weak hashing algorithm (SHA-1). Removing support for older TLS cipher suites helps optimize security for the IAM service and its users.

**Does this impact me?**

If you use an older web browser (generally browser versions installed prior to 2015), libraries, or devices that leverage older SSL/TLS cipher suites, this could impact your ability to connect to the IAM service.

**What do I need to do?**

To ensure uninterrupted service and maintain the highest level of security, we encourage all users to upgrade their legacy browsers, libraries, or devices that do not support secure TLS configurations. Additionally, please update your client application configurations to use more modern and secure TLS cipher suites if they are not already in use. If you are using TLS 1.2 or 1.3 with secure cipher suites, no action is needed.

[Changes to the Default Sender's Email Address for Email Notifications](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/servicechanges.htm#)

**Date Announced:** March 21, 2025

**Date in Effect:** March 21, 2025

**Details:**

*   The default **Sender's email address** used to send password reset and other email notifications has changed from `no-reply@oracle.com` to `no-reply@identity.oci.oraclecloud.com` for the OC1 realm.
*   For Dedicated regions, the address has changed to `no-reply@identity.oci.`<realm\_domain>. For details on the <realm\_domain>, see [Dedicated Regions](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/General/Concepts/dedicatedregions.htm)
    .

**Does this impact me?** Yes.

**What do I need to do?** To continue receiving email notifications from OCI IAM without interruption, add the new email sender address to your list of allowed senders.

[Change to Custom Sender Email Address Verification for Email Notifications](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/servicechanges.htm#)

**Service Change:** Effective April 3, OCI Identity will no longer support custom email verification using the OCI Console.

**Date Announced:** March 26, 2025

**Date in Effect:** April 3, 2025

**Details:** Customers who need to update the sender email address or change from the default email address for email notifications must use the Verify Sender Domain process and provision DKIMs for each domain in the tenancy in the home and subscribed regions. If you currently have a verified custom sender email address, you can continue to use that verified email address.

**Does this impact me?** Yes, if you change the existing verified custom sender email address.

**What do I need to do?**

You need to configure email authentication settings for SPF and DKIM. See [Configuring Email Authentication Settings for SPF and DKIM](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/Identity/notifications/configure-email-auth-spf-dkim.htm)
.

**Note**  
  
Provisioning DKIM needs to be done for all applicable domains in both the home and subscribed regions.

[AuditEvents APIs and Reports](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/servicechanges.htm#)

**Service Change:** The Oracle Cloud Infrastructure Identity and Access Management Audit Events APIs which you can use with IAM identity domains will be deprecated in **May 2025**. Out of the box reports will continue to be available, but they will be limited to the last 14 days of data.

**Date Announced:** May 15, 2023

**Date in Effect:** May 2025

**Details:** Starting **May 2025**, the IAM APIs for AuditEvents will no longer work with IAM. Out of the box reports will continue to be available, but they will be limited to the last 14 days of data.

**Does this impact me?** If you're currently using IAM APIs for AuditEvents, you will need to use the OCI [Audit](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/Audit/home.htm)
 APIs instead. There is no change if you use reports, but note that Identity Domains reports will be limited to the last 14 days of data.

**What do I need to do?** You can now use OCI [Audit](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/Audit/home.htm)
 APIs. To find out more about retrieving data from OCI Audit, see:

*   [Implement multi cloud security using OCI Audit to capture events from OCI Identity and Access Management](https://docs.oracle.com/en/learn/oci-iam-multicloud-security/#introduction)
    
*   [Generate Identity and Access Management Reports from Oracle Cloud Infrastructure Audit](https://docs.oracle.com/en/learn/generating-iam-reports-from-oci-audit/index.html#introduction)
    

#### Deprecated AuditEvents APIs

The following IAM AuditEvents APIs are deprecated:

*   AuditEvents

#### Reports that continue to be supported but with only 14 days data

The following IAM report templates in the reports APIs will continue to be supported with limited data (14 days):

*   User Login
*   System Log
*   Sync Failure
*   Suspicious Events
*   Notification Delivery
*   AppRole Assignment
*   Application Access

Java Management Service (JMS)
-----------------------------

[Custom log required for JMS Fleet creation](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/servicechanges.htm#)

**Service Change:** Effective July 15, 2022, the CreateFleet API will require the custom log OCID in the `inventoryLog` property.

**Date Announced:** April 15, 2022

**Date in Effect:** July 15, 2022

**Details:** As of March 30, 2022, JMS uses the Oracle Cloud Infrastructure Logging service to store inventory and operation logs. Inventory logs are custom logs that store the Java Runtime inventory and usage related information reported from the hosts by the Management Agent. With this change, the CreateFleet API includes an additional property, `inventoryLog`, to specify the custom log to use.

**What do I need to do?** Existing fleets must be migrated using the UpdateFleet API by July 15, 2022. After July 15, 2022 the `inventoryLog` property of CreateFleet API will be a required parameter. See the [CreateFleet](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/api/#/en/jms/latest/Fleet/CreateFleet)
 and [UpdateFleet](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/api/#/en/jms/latest/Fleet/UpdateFleet)
 operations for details. Agents need to be on 220302.1455 or later.

[Deprecated attributes in LibraryUsage Reference](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/servicechanges.htm#)

**Service Change:** Effective September 24, 2025

**Date Announced:** September 24, 2025

**Date in Effect:** September 24, 2025

**Details:** The following attributes will be deprecated in the [Java Management Service Fleets API Library Usage Reference](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/api/#/en/jms/latest/LibraryUsage/)
:

*   `cvssScore`
*   `cveId`
*   `timeLastCveRefreshed`

**What do I need to do?**If you're an existing customer and you have previously used [Summarize Java Usage](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/api/#/en/jms/latest/LibraryUsage/SummarizeLibraryUsage)
:

*   Stop using the deprecated fields `cvssScore`, `cveId`, and `timeLastCveRefreshed` and instead use the new fields `highestVulnerabilityScore` and vulnerabilities.
*   Stop using `cvssScore enum` for `sortBy` and instead use the new field `highestVulnerabilityScore enum`.

If you're a new customer and have never used our service before, this change doesn't impact you.

Language
--------

[Deprecated Language APIs](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/servicechanges.htm#)

Starting October 26, 2022, the `Detect` Language APIs of version `20221001` were deprecated. From October 10, 2023, onwards the deprecated APIs will be unavailable.

| Deprecated API | Replacement API |
| --- | --- |
| [DetectDominantLanguage](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/api/#%23/en/language/20221001/DetectDominantLanguage/DetectDominantLanguage) | [BatchDetectDominantLanguage](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/api/#%23/en/language/latest/BatchDetectDominantLanguage/BatchDetectDominantLanguage) |
| [DetectLanguageEntities](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/api/#%23/en/language/20221001/DetectLanguageEntities/DetectLanguageEntities) | [BatchDetectLanguageEntities](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/api/#%23/en/language/latest/BatchDetectLanguageEntities/BatchDetectLanguageEntities) |
| [DetectLanguageKeyPhrases](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/api/#%23/en/language/20221001/DetectLanguageKeyPhrases/DetectLanguageKeyPhrases) | [BatchDetectLanguageKeyPhrases](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/api/#%23/en/language/latest/BatchDetectLanguageKeyPhrases/BatchDetectLanguageKeyPhrases) |
