| [DetectLanguageSentiments](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/api/#%23/en/language/20221001/DetectLanguageSentiments/DetectLanguageSentiments) | [BatchDetectLanguageSentiments](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/api/#%23/en/language/latest/BatchDetectLanguageSentiments/BatchDetectLanguageSentiments) |
| [DetectLanguageTextClassification](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/api/#%23/en/language/20221001/DetectLanguageEntities/DetectLanguageEntities) | [BatchDetectLanguageTextClassification](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/api/#%23/en/language/latest/BatchDetectLanguageTextClassification/BatchDetectLanguageTextClassification) |

[Removed Language Classes](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/servicechanges.htm#)

Starting October 26, 2022, the `BatchDetect` Language APIs have a new supported API version of `20221001`. With the introduction of API version `20221001`, the following classes have been removed and replaced with the common class, `com.oracle.bmc.ailanguage.model.TextDocument`.

| Removed Class in Language | Replacement Class |
| --- | --- |
| `com.oracle.bmc.ailanguage.model.EntityDocument` | `com.oracle.bmc.ailanguage.model.TextDocument` |
| `com.oracle.bmc.ailanguage.model.KeyPhraseDocument` |
| `com.oracle.bmc.ailanguage.model.SentimentsDocument` |
| `com.oracle.bmc.ailanguage.model.TextClassificationDocument` |

Network Firewall
----------------

[Deprecated Network Firewall API](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/servicechanges.htm#)

Support for the [20211001 version of the Oracle Network Firewall API](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/api/#/en/network-firewall/20211001/)
 ends on January 30, 2026. We highly recommend upgrading to the 20230501 version to ensure continued functionality and support.

**Sunset Dates for Specific API Operations**

*   `CreateNetworkFirewallPolicy` Friday, November 1, 2024 (00:00:00 GMT)
*   `GetNetworkFirewallPolicy` Wednesday, January 1, 2025 (00:00:00 GMT)
*   `UpdateNetworkFirewallPolicy` Friday, November 1, 2024 (00:00:00 GMT)
*   `DeleteNetworkFirewallPolicy` Saturday, February 1, 2025 (00:00:00 GMT)

To avoid service disruptions, ensure that you migrate configurations to the new API version by the deadlines mentioned.

**How to Upgrade**

For a smooth transition, see the following resources:

*   [New API Version Documentation](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/api/#/en/network-firewall/20230501/)
    
*   [Upgrade a Firewall Policy](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/network-firewall/upgrade.htm)
    
*   See [Getting help](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/GSG/support/getting-help.htm)
     if you need help with the upgrade process or have any questions.

Network Load Balancer
---------------------

[Deprecated Network Load Balancer APIs](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/servicechanges.htm#)

The Oracle Cloud Infrastructure's Network Load Balancer `ListNetworkLoadBalancerProtocol` API is deprecated as of January 12, 2022. Support for `ListNetworkLoadBalancerProtocol` ends on March 1, 2023. See [ListenerDetails](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/api/#/en/networkloadbalancer/latest/datatypes/ListenerDetails)
 for the current list of supported protocol values.

OCI Cache
---------

[Use of Redis Commands CONFIG SET and ACL Restricted](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/servicechanges.htm#)

**Service Change:** OCI Cache is restricting the use of the Redis commands `CONFIG SET` and `ACL` in clusters managed by the service.

**Date Announced:** June 14, 2024

**Date in Effect:** July 14, 2024

**Details:** OCI Cache prevents the use of some Redis commands to ensure performance and stability of the service, see [Unsupported Redis Commands](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/ocicache/restrictedcommands.htm)
. Starting July 14, 2024, the `CONFIG SET` and `ACL` commands will be included in the list of restricted commands for OCI Cache.

**Does this impact me?** If you currently use these commands, you will no longer be able to use them with your OCI Cache clusters after July 14, 2024. We also recommend that you don't use these commands prior to them being restricted as they can cause stability issues with clusters managed by OCI Cache.

Ops Insights
------------

[Ops Insights for MySQL HeatWave End of Life](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/servicechanges.htm#)

**Service Change:** Ops Insights for MySQL HeatWave End of Life

**Date Announced:** January 29, 2026

**Date in Effect:** January 29, 2027

**Details:** MySQL HeatWave support within the OCI Ops Insights service has been deprecated as of January 29, 2026, and new MySQL HeatWave DB systems can no longer be enabled. Additionally, this MySQL functionality will be obsoleted as of January 29, 2027. Note that Ops Insights support for Oracle Databases and Exadata systems is not impacted and will continue to be enhanced.

**Does this impact me?** Yes, if you currently use Ops Insights for MySQL HeatWave to monitor MySQL HeatWave DB systems.

**What do I need to do?**

*   Plan and execute a transition to an alternative monitoring solution before the effective date to avoid disruption.
*   Evaluate and select a replacement that fits your business and technical requirements. Third‑party options such as Prometheus and Grafana dashboards are commonly used for MySQL monitoring. Verify security and compliance with your organization’s requirements and Oracle’s internal guidelines before adoption.
*   For assistance or additional information, submit a Service Request through My Oracle Support.

Oracle Apiary
-------------

[Apiary Cloud Service End of Life](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/servicechanges.htm#)

**Service Change:**End-of-Life (EOL) for Apiary Cloud Service effective October 31, 2026.

**Date Announced:** Oct 28, 2025

**Date in Effect:** Oct 31, 2026

**Details:**

Oracle announces the End of Life (EOL) for Apiary on October 31, 2026. The impacted SKUs are as follows:

*   B88650 - Oracle Apiary - Standard
*   B88651 - Oracle Apiary - Professional

After the end date, Oracle will no longer support, update, or maintain the above services. You will no longer be able to use these services after the EOL date, and all remaining instances will be permanently deleted.

**Does this impact me?** Yes, if you are using the Oracle Apiary Cloud service.

**What do I need to do? :** Customers using API Blueprint format will need to convert to OpenAPI format.

Apiary customers can then choose to migrate OpenAPI to a wide variety of API design and documentation tools like Apimatic, Postman, Swagger Editor, StopLight, Redocly, or Bump.sh.

If you have questions, require support, or need assistance with migration planning, contact your Oracle account team by October 31, 2026.

Oracle API Platform Cloud Service
---------------------------------

[Oracle API Platform Cloud Service End of Life](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/servicechanges.htm#)

**Service Change:**End-of-Life (EOL) for API Platform Cloud Service effective October 31, 2026.

**Date Announced:** Oct 27, 2025

**Date in Effect:** Oct 31, 2026

**Details:**

Oracle announces the End of Life (EOL) for Oracle API Platform Cloud Service on October 31, 2026. The impacted SKUs are as follows:

*   B89652 – Oracle API Platform Cloud Service – Gateway Per Hour
*   B90267 – Oracle API Platform Cloud Service – Gateway Per Hour (Gov SKU)
*   B88822 – Oracle API Platform Cloud Service – Classic – Government – Gateway Per Hour

After the end date, Oracle will no longer support, update, or maintain the above services. You will no longer be able to use these services after the EOL date, and all remaining instances will be permanently deleted.

**Does this impact me?** Yes, if you're using the Oracle API Platform Cloud service.

**What do I need to do? :**

Customers can choose to migrate from API Platform to OCI API Gateway and will need to complete a technical migration.

Customers who require a developer portal functionality of API Platform will migrate to Traefik Labs, available in OCI Marketplace.

If you have questions, require support, or need assistance with migration planning, contact your Oracle account team by October 31, 2026.

Oracle Database Service for Azure
---------------------------------

[Oracle Database Service for Azure End of Life](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/servicechanges.htm#)

**Service Change**: End of Life for Oracle Database Service for Azure

**Date Announced**: July 8, 2026

**Date in Effect**: May 31, 2027

**Details**: On the effective date, Oracle Database Service for Azure will no longer be available.

**Does this impact me?** Yes, if you currently use Oracle Database Service for Azure.

**What do I need to do?** To maintain connectivity between Microsoft Azure and databases running in OCI, migrate to [Oracle Interconnect for Azure](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/multicloud/interconnect-azure.htm)
. Configure or validate your Interconnect connection, update OCI routing to use Interconnect instead of the Oracle Database Service for Azure DRG attachment, and continue managing your databases through the OCI Console. Your databases and data remain unaffected.

Oracle Mobile Hub
-----------------

[Oracle Mobile Hub End of Life](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/servicechanges.htm#)

**Service Change:**End-of-Life (EOL) for Oracle Mobile Hub Cloud Service effective October 31, 2026.

**Date Announced:** Oct 28, 2025

**Date in Effect:** Oct 31, 2026

**Details:**

Oracle announces the End of Life (EOL) for Oracle Mobile Hub (OMH) Cloud Service on October 31, 2026. The impacted SKUs are as follows:

*   B90304 Oracle Mobile Hub Cloud Service
*   B90647 Oracle Mobile Cloud Service – Enterprise
*   B88362 Oracle Mobile Cloud Service – Enterprise – Classic

After the end date, Oracle will no longer support, update, or maintain the above services. You will no longer be able to use these services after the EOL date, and all remaining instances will be permanently deleted.

**Does this impact me?** Yes, if you're using the Oracle Mobile Hub.

**What do I need to do? :**

Customers must plan and execute migration of OMH workloads before the End Date.

If you need a full MBaaS solution, consider Google Firebase as the primary alternative, offering a comprehensive MBaaS ecosystem (push notifications, APIs, identity/auth, storage, SDKs, offline sync, analytics, and A/B testing). AWS Amplify and Azure Mobile Apps may also be considered. Depending on your use of Oracle Mobile Hub, you may be able to use OCI services to meet your needs.

If you have questions, require support, or need assistance with migration planning, please contact your Oracle account team by October 31, 2026.

OS Management
-------------

[OS Management Service End of Life](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/servicechanges.htm#)

**Service Change:** Oracle OS Management service is deprecated.

**Date Announced:** April 23, 2024

**Date in Effect:** April 23, 2025

**Details:** On April 23, 2025, the OS Management service (OSMS) reaches end of life (EOL). Effective now, the service is no longer available to you in regions where you are not already using OSMS, or to new users with new tenancies. The OS Management service is replaced by [OS Management Hub](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/osmh/doc/home.htm)
, which provides an improved user experience with new features, including patch deployments through lifecycle stages, enhanced job scheduling, and reporting capabilities.

**Does this impact me?** The OS Management service will no longer be available to manage Oracle Linux or Microsoft Windows instances after April 23, 2025.

**What do I need to do:** Start using the OS Management Hub service to manage instances in Oracle Cloud Infrastructure (OCI), private data centers, and supported third-party cloud environments. Before the EOL date, we recommend that you migrate your managed instances from the OS Management service to the OS Management Hub service.

**Note**  
  
Other services, including Autonomous Linux, that use the OS Management API will provide separate guidance.

Process Automation
------------------

[Oracle Cloud Infrastructure Process Automation End of Life](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/servicechanges.htm#)

**Service Change:** Oracle Cloud Infrastructure Process Automation End of Life.

**Date Announced:** January 15, 2025

**Date in Effect:** January 23, 2026

**Details:** Effective January 23, 2026 (the “End Date”), Oracle Cloud Infrastructure Process Automation has reached end of life. The affected services are as follows:

*   B95504 - Oracle Cloud Infrastructure - Process Automation - User
*   B95505 - Oracle Cloud Infrastructure - Process Automation - Execution Pack

Search with OpenSearch
----------------------

[Permissions for Networking resources changing from service to user permissions](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/servicechanges.htm#)

**Service Change:** The IAM permissions for Networking resources required to create and work with OpenSearch clusters are changing from service permissions to user permissions.

**Date Announced:** February 20, 2024

**Date in Effect:** September 15, 2024

**Details:** To create and manage clusters in Search with OpenSearch, you must create IAM policies for your tenancy that grants permissions to specific Networking resources. Currently, the required permissions are service permissions, with policy statements such as the following snippet:

    Allow service opensearch to manage <Networking_Resource>...

Search with OpenSearch is transitioning to requiring user permissions to grant access to Networking resources instead of service permissions. During the transition period your tenancy needs to have both types of policies.

**Does this impact me?** All tenancies where users are creating and managing OpenSearch clusters need to have new policies that specify user permissions in addition to the existing policies with service permissions for access to the required Networking resources.

**What do I need to do?** To prepare for this transition, create a policy for Search with OpenSearch in your tenancy that grants the required user permissions to Networking resources. The following policy example includes these permissions:

    Allow group SearchOpenSearchAdmins to manage vnics in compartment <NETWORK_RESOURCES_COMPARTMENT>
    Allow group SearchOpenSearchAdmins to manage vcns in compartment <NETWORK_RESOURCES_COMPARTMENT>
    Allow group SearchOpenSearchAdmins to use subnets in compartment <NETWORK_RESOURCES_COMPARTMENT>
    Allow group SearchOpenSearchAdmins to use network-security-groups in compartment <NETWORK_RESOURCES_COMPARTMENT>
    Allow group SearchOpenSearchAdmins to manage opensearch-family in compartment <CLUSTER_RESOURCES_COMPARTMENT>
                            

You need to keep any existing policies you have for Search with OpenSearch that include service permissions statements for Networking resources until the transition to user permissions statements has been completed. See [Search with OpenSearch IAM Policies](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/search-opensearch/Concepts/ocisearchpermissions.htm)
 for documentation about the permissions required for Search with OpenSearch.

Stack Monitoring
----------------

[OCI Stack Monitoring End of Life](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/servicechanges.htm#)

**Service Change:** OCI Stack Monitoring End of Life

**Date Announced:** January 22, 2026

**Date in Effect:** January 23, 2027

**Details:** OCI Stack Monitoring has been deprecated as of January 22, 2026. If you're an existing OCI Stack Monitoring customer, you can continue to use the service in your existing tenancies and regions until the last date of service, January 23, 2027. For alternative cloud-based monitoring solutions, we recommend using the OCI Database Management service to monitor all Oracle database and Exadata resources. Monitoring for all other resource types are available in other OCI Observability & Management services. Oracle Enterprise Manager can also be used as an alternative customer-managed solution.

**Does this impact me?** Yes, if you currently use OCI Stack Monitoring.

**What do I need to do?** Adopt any of the alternative solutions listed in the following table:

| Solution name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| [OCI Database Management](https://www.oracle.com/manageability/database-management/) | Provides comprehensive monitoring, performance management, tuning, and administration of Oracle databases and Exadata running on-premises, in OCI, or in other clouds. |
| [OCI Log Analytics](https://www.oracle.com/manageability/log-analytics/) | Provides ML-based insights, alerts, and dashboards from logs across an IT environment. |
| [OCI Monitoring](https://www.oracle.com/cloud/cloud-native/monitoring/) | Provides capabilities for storing, aggregating, and visualizing metric data, as well as for evaluating, triggering, and managing metric alarms. |
| [Oracle Enterprise Manager 24ai](https://www.oracle.com/enterprise-manager/) | Provides a customer-managed systems management tool for monitoring and managing applications, application servers, Oracle databases, Exadata systems, and host servers. Deploy Oracle Enterprise Manager on-premises, in OCI, or in other clouds to monitor resources across those environments. |

For more information, see [My Oracle Support (MOS) note KA1304](https://support.oracle.com/support/?anchorId=&documentId=KA1304)
.

Support Management
------------------

[Deprecated attributes: bearertoken, bearertokentype, contacts, idtoken, name](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/servicechanges.htm#)

**Service Change:** The following attributes are deprecated: bearertoken, bearertokentype, contacts, idtoken, name

**Date Announced:** April 20, 2026.

**Date in Effect:** May 20, 2027.

**Details:** To improve the clarity and accuracy of the Support Management API documentation, several unused attributes are being deprecated. These attributes currently provide no functional value for public API usage. Following are the deprecated attributes.

*   `bearertoken` ([CreateIncident](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/api/#/en/incidentmanagement/latest/Incident/CreateIncident)
    )
*   `bearertokentype` ([CreateIncident](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/api/#/en/incidentmanagement/latest/Incident/CreateIncident)
    )
*   `contacts` ([CreateIncident](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/api/#/en/incidentmanagement/latest/Incident/CreateIncident)
    )
*   `idtoken` ([Incident](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/api/#/en/incidentmanagement/latest/Incident)
    )
*   `name` ([ListIncidentResourceTypes](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/api/#/en/incidentmanagement/latest/IncidentResourceType/ListIncidentResourceTypes)
    )

**Does this impact me?** If you have custom scripts that explicitly use deprecated attributes, then you might want to update the scripts.

**What do I need to do?** When using [SDKs and the CLI](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/API/Concepts/sdks.htm)
, update custom scripts to remove deprecated attributes.

[Deprecated csi attribute, reduced max length for description attribute](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/servicechanges.htm#)

**Service Change:** The `csi` attribute ([CreateIncident](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/api/#/en/incidentmanagement/latest/Incident/CreateIncident)
) is deprecated, and the `Max Length` field for the `description` attribute ([CreateTicketDetails](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/api/#/en/incidentmanagement/latest/datatypes/CreateTicketDetails)
) is reduced to 1500.

**Date Announced:** January 27, 2025.

**Date in Effect:** January 27, 2026.

**Details:** With the migration to [My Oracle Cloud Support](https://support.oracle.com/)
, the `csi` attribute is no longer needed, and the `Max Length` field for the `description` attribute is required to be 1500 (was 1800).

**Does this impact me?** If you have custom scripts that explicitly use the `csi` attribute, or that explicitly rely on a `Max Length` greater than 1500 for the `description` attribute, then you might want to update the scripts.

**What do I need to do?** When using [SDKs and the CLI](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/API/Concepts/sdks.htm)
, update custom scripts to remove deprecated attributes and field values.

Vision
------

[Deprecated Vision APIs](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/servicechanges.htm#)

**Service Change:** Oracle Cloud Infrastructure Vision's document analysis APIs were deprecated January 30, 2023. The document analysis functionality is now offered through the [Oracle Cloud Infrastructure Document Understanding service](https://www.oracle.com/artificial-intelligence/document-understanding/)
.

**Date Announced:** January 30, 2023

**Date in Effect:** January 31, 2024

**Details:**

The following APIs were deprecated January 10, 2023:

*   AnalyzeDocument
*   CreateDocumentJob
*   GetDocumentJob
*   CancelDocumentJob

Any output data stored in an Object Storage bucket that resulted from previous Document Jobs will remain accessible after Document API deprecation. From January 31, 2024 onwards, the deprecated APIs will be unavailable in the Oracle Cloud Infrastructure Vision service.

**Does this impact me?** This change impacts customers using the document analysis functionality in the Oracle Cloud Infrastructure Vision service.

**What do I need to do?** Customers using the document analysis functionality in the Oracle Cloud Infrastructure Vision service should instead use the document analysis functionality offered through the [Oracle Cloud Infrastructure Document Understanding service](https://www.oracle.com/artificial-intelligence/document-understanding/)
.

# Private Cloud Appliance

### [Oracle Cloud Infrastructure Documentation](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/home.htm)

All Pages

Private Cloud Appliance
=======================

Use Private Cloud Appliance to deploy Oracle Cloud Infrastructure (OCI) services on-premises to comply with data residency requirements. Operate disconnected from OCI, maintain full control over your infrastructure, and expand its capacity as your resource consumption grows.

[What's new](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/releasenotes/services/private-cloud-appliance/)

Database Tools
==============

Database Tools is a managed service in OCI that enables you to create reusable connections to any Oracle or MySQL database service in OCI.

[What's new](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/releasenotes/services/database-tools/)

# Application Performance Monitoring

### [Oracle Cloud Infrastructure Documentation](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/home.htm)

All Pages

Application Performance Monitoring
==================================

Application Performance Monitoring provides a comprehensive set of features to monitor applications and diagnose performance issues.

[What's new](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/releasenotes/services/apm/)

#### Get Started

[Overview](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/application-performance-monitoring/doc/application-performance-monitoring.html)

[Create policies](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/application-performance-monitoring/doc/perform-oracle-cloud-infrastructure-prerequisite-tasks.html)

[Workshop: Get started with APM](https://apexapps.oracle.com/pls/apex/r/dbpm/livelabs/view-workshop?wid=3350)

[Create an APM domain](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/application-performance-monitoring/doc/create-apm-domain.html)

[Product page](https://www.oracle.com/manageability/application-performance-monitoring/)

#### Visualize Traces and Spans

[View service topology](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/application-performance-monitoring/doc/view-service-topology.html)

[View thread snapshots](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/application-performance-monitoring/doc/view-thread-snapshots.html)

[View GeoMap](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/application-performance-monitoring/doc/view-traces-geomap-view.html)

[Work with dashboards](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/application-performance-monitoring/doc/work-dashboards.html)

#### Developer Tools

[APIs for Application Performance Monitoring](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/application-performance-monitoring/doc/application-performance-monitoring-apis.html)

[SDKs and the CLI](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/API/Concepts/sdks.htm)

[CLI for Application Performance Monitoring](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/tools/oci-cli/3.33.0/oci_cli_docs/cmdref/apm-config.html)

#### Configure Data Sources

[Learn about data sources](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/application-performance-monitoring/doc/application-performance-monitoring-data-sources.html)

[Java agent](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/application-performance-monitoring/doc/provision-and-deploy-apm-java-agents-linux-application-servers.html)

[Browser agent](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/application-performance-monitoring/doc/configure-browser-agent-real-user-monitoring.html)

[Oracle applications](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/application-performance-monitoring/doc/integrate-other-applications.html)

#### Configure Synthetic Monitoring

[Learn about synthetic monitoring](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/application-performance-monitoring/doc/use-synthetic-monitoring.html)

[Tasks and workflows](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/application-performance-monitoring/doc/set-synthetic-monitoring.html)

[Create a script](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/application-performance-monitoring/doc/create-script.html)

[Set up dedicated vantage points](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/application-performance-monitoring/doc/use-dedicated-vantage-points.html)

[Create a monitor](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/application-performance-monitoring/doc/create-monitor.html)

#### Related Services

[Stack Monitoring](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/stack-monitoring/index.html)

[Monitoring](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/Monitoring/home.htm)

#### Community

[Blog posts](https://blogs.oracle.com/observability/category/oem-application-performance-monitoring)

[APM Videos](https://www.youtube.com/playlist?list=PLiuPvpy8QsiV0dnG-YYaJ83FJOG_Q4eIl)

[Observability How-to Videos](https://www.youtube.com/playlist?list=PLiuPvpy8QsiWO40IrhRlbY-TOLepyGXzV)

# Generative AI

### [Oracle Cloud Infrastructure Documentation](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/home.htm)

* * *

All Pages

[Skip to main content](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/generative-ai/home.htm#dcoc-content-body)

Generative AI
=============

OCI Generative AI is Oracle Cloud Infrastructure's **"Enterprise AI" Platform"** for building, deploying, and governing AI applications at scale. Speed up delivery with frontier models, built-in agent development tools, vector stores, connectors, managed context retention and memory, and a hosted runtime that's designed for agent development and orchestration. Apply enterprise controls such as IAM, guardrails, observability, and auditability to ensure safe, governed, and lower-risk production deployments.

[What's new](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/releasenotes/services/generative-ai/ ".")

#### Get Started

[Overview](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/generative-ai/overview.htm "OCI Generative AI is a fully managed Oracle Cloud Infrastructure service for building, deploying, and operating generative AI applications at enterprise scale.")

[Learn about using the models](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/generative-ai/models.htm "Use Enterprise AI Models in OCI Generative AI to access pretrained hosted models, import supported custom models, and deploy models for enterprise inference workloads.")

[Learn about Enterprise AI agents](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/generative-ai/agents.htm "OCI Generative AI provides two main approaches for building enterprise-grade agents, and you can also combine them in a hybrid architecture.")

[Product page](https://www.oracle.com/artificial-intelligence/generative-ai/large-language-models/)

#### Learn About Models

[Pretrained models](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/generative-ai/pretrained-models.htm "OCI Generative AI offers the following pretrained foundational models. Review the key features, regions, on-demand and dedicated AI cluster offerings, deprecation and retirement dates, and benchmarks for the models.")

[Models by region](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/generative-ai/model-endpoint-regions.htm "This page provides a list of regions where OCI Generative AI models are available. Select each model for its details.")

[Import your own models](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/generative-ai/imported-models.htm "You can import open-source and third-party large language large language models from Hugging Face and OCI Object Storage buckets into OCI Generative AI. After importing a model, you can host it on a dedicated AI cluster, create an endpoint, and use it in the Generative AI service.")

[Guardrails](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/generative-ai/guardrails.htm "Guardrails are configurable safety and compliance controls that help manage what the model can accept as input and generate as output. In OCI Generative AI, guardrails support content moderation, prompt injection detection, and personally identifiable information (PII) detection for text inputs into a Generative AI application or text generated by Generative AI.")

[Retiring the models](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/generative-ai/deprecating.htm "OCI Generative AI retires its large language models (LLMs) based on each model's type and serving mode. The LLMs serves the user requests in either an on-demand mode or a dedicated mode. Review the following topics to learn about deprecation and removal times and to decide which serving mode works best for you.")

#### Import Models

[Compatible models](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/generative-ai/imported-models.htm "You can import open-source and third-party large language large language models from Hugging Face and OCI Object Storage buckets into OCI Generative AI. After importing a model, you can host it on a dedicated AI cluster, create an endpoint, and use it in the Generative AI service.")

[Import models from Hugging Face](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/generative-ai/import-model-from-hugging-face.htm "Import compatible open source and third-party models from Hugging Face into OCI Generative AI, host them, create endpoints, and use them like any other model.")

[Import models from Object Storage buckets](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/generative-ai/import-model-from-bucket.htm "Import compatible open source and third-party models with a Hugging Face format into OCI Generative AI, host them, create endpoints, and use them like any other model.")

#### Tutorials

[Create a sandbox user](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/generative-ai/setup-sandbox-user.htm "Create a sandbox user and a compartment for this user in an OCI tenancy. Then give this user access to all OCI resources only in this compartment.")

[Set up API authentication](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/generative-ai/setup-oci-api-auth.htm "To connect a local environment to an OCI tenancy, set up API authentication in a config file.")

[Chat using LangChain](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/generative-ai/setup-oci-langchain.htm "Set up LangChain packages including packages for integration with OCI Generative AI. Then, test the installation by chatting with a model hosted on OCI Generative AI.")

#### Build Agents with Enterprise AI features

[Overview](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/generative-ai/agents.htm "OCI Generative AI provides two main approaches for building enterprise-grade agents, and you can also combine them in a hybrid architecture.")

[QuickStart for building agents](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/generative-ai/get-started-agents.htm "Use the following steps to begin building Enterprise AI Agents with the OCI Responses API.")

[OCI OpenAI-Compatible Endpoints](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/generative-ai/openai-compatible-api.htm "Use OCI Generative AI OpenAI-compatible endpoints to call Enterprise AI models and build Enterprise AI agents through familiar OpenAI-style API. Call these endpoints to reach supported OpenAI request patterns while keeping authentication, execution, and resource management within OCI.")

[OCI Responses API](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/generative-ai/responses-api.htm "Use the OCI Responses API to call supported models, generate model outputs, and build tool-based or multi-step workflows through a single OpenAI-compatible, Open Responses-compliant API. It supports text and image inputs and text outputs. You can use it to build stateful interactions with conversation history, add supported tools such as File Search and Code Interpreter, and connect the model to external systems through Function Calling and MCP Calling.")

[Projects](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/generative-ai/projects.htm "OCI Generative AI projects organize conversations and responses under a shared set of settings. In a project, you define how long data is retained, enable long-term memory to persist context across conversations, and enable short-term memory compaction to optimize how conversation history is processed.")

#### Deploy agents with Enterprise AI features

[Learn about deploying agents](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/generative-ai/deploying-agents.htm "You can deploy agents by using OCI Generative AI Applications, which provide a managed runtime for containerized agent workloads.")

[Create an application](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/generative-ai/create-application.htm "Create an application in the OCI Generative AI service.")

[Create a deployment](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/generative-ai/create-deployment.htm "Create a deployment in OCI Generative AI service to deploy an agent container that can serve client requests for an application.")

[Add an artifact](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/generative-ai/add-artifact.htm "Add an artifact to a deployment in the OCI Generative AI service.")

#### Developer Tools

[Inference API](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/api/#/en/generative-ai-inference/)

[Management API](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/api/#/en/generative-ai/)

[NL2SQL API](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/api/#/en/generative-ai-nl2sql/)

[Inference CLI](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/tools/oci-cli/latest/oci_cli_docs/cmdref/generative-ai-inference.html)

[Management CLI](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/tools/oci-cli/latest/oci_cli_docs/cmdref/generative-ai.html)

[NL2SQL CLI](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/tools/oci-cli/latest/oci_cli_docs/cmdref/generative-ai-data.html)

[SDKs and the CLI](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/API/Concepts/sdks.htm)

[Cloud Shell](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/API/Concepts/devcloudshellintro.htm)

#### API Keys

[OCI API keys](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/generative-ai/api-keys.htm "OCI Generative AI API keys are secure credential tokens used to authenticate callers and authorize access to large language models hosted by the OCI Generative AI service.")

[Create an API key](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/generative-ai/create-api-key.htm "Create an API key to authenticate requests to OCI Generative AI. You can name the key and optionally configure up to two key names with expiration dates and times.")

[Add key permissions](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/generative-ai/add-api-permission.htm "To use an OCI Generative AI API key, grant permission through tenancy-level or compartment-level IAM policies.")

#### Agent tools

[MCP tools](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/generative-ai/get-started-agents.htm#mcp-calling "MCP (Model Context Protocol) tools are executable functions or capabilities that AI models can use to interact with external systems. Use MCP Calling in OCI Generative AI to let a model access executable tools exposed by a remote MCP server during a Responses API request. These tools can provide access to external systems such as API, databases, filesystems, or application endpoints. OCI Generative AI communicates directly with remote MCP servers as part of the request workflow.")

[Code interpreter](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/generative-ai/get-started-agents.htm#code-interpreter "Use Code Interpreter to let the model write and run Python code in an isolated OCI-managed container. Code Interpreter is useful for calculations, data analysis, file processing, chart generation, and other computation-heavy tasks. Code Interpreter supports dynamic file interaction during the life of the container. The model can read files you provide and can also create new files.")

[File search](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/generative-ai/get-started-agents.htm#file-search "When you add the File Search tool to the Responses API, the model can retrieve content from files stored in a vector store during response generation. This is useful when you want responses to reflect the documents you provide rather than relying only on the model’s built-in knowledge.")

[SQL Search (NL2SQL)](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/generative-ai/get-started-agents.htm#nl2sql-tool)

[Function Calling](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/generative-ai/get-started-agents.htm#function-calling "Use Function Calling to let the model request data or actions from your application during a Responses API workflow. This is useful when the model needs information or operations that aren't available in the prompt itself, such as calendar data, internal application state, or the result of a custom operation.")

#### Vector Stores for Enterprise AI features

[Create a vector store](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/generative-ai/create-vector-store.htm "Create a vector store in the OCI Generative AI service.")

[Use the NL2SQL API for structured data](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/generative-ai/nl2sql.htm "Use SQL Search (NL2SQL) to convert natural-language requests into validated SQL for enterprise data in OCI Generative AI.")

[Sync data for unstructured data](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/generative-ai/sync-data-vector-store.htm "To sync data to a vector store, first, create one or more data scync connectors for a vector store in the OCI Generative AI service. Then run the command to perform the sync.")

#### Support

[Support Requests](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/GSG/Tasks/contactingsupport.htm)

[Create a service request](https://support.oracle.com/)

#### Community

[AI at Oracle](https://www.oracle.com/artificial-intelligence/)

[Oracle AI blog](https://blogs.oracle.com/ai-and-datascience/)

[AI solutions hub](https://www.oracle.com/artificial-intelligence/solutions/)

Database Management
===================

Database Management provides a unified console for monitoring, performance management, tuning, and administration of your databases and infrastructure components.

[What's new](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/releasenotes/services/database-management/)

Log Analytics
=============

**Oracle Logging Analytics service is now renamed as Oracle Log Analytics**. Oracle Log Analytics is a cloud solution in Oracle Cloud Infrastructure that lets you index, enrich, aggregate, explore, search, analyze, correlate, visualize, and monitor all log data from your applications and system infrastructure.

[What's new](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/releasenotes/services/log-analytics/)

# Known Issues

### [Oracle Cloud Infrastructure Documentation](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/home.htm)

* * *

All Pages

[Skip to main content](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/knownissues.htm#dcoc-content-body)

Known Issues
============

The following lists describe the known issues with Oracle Cloud Infrastructure.

Announcements
-------------

Currently, there are no known Announcements issues.

API Gateway
-----------

For known issues with API Gateway, see [Known Issues for API Gateway](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/APIGateway/known-issues/apigateway-known-issues.htm)
.

Application Performance Monitoring
----------------------------------

[Browser and Scripted Browser monitors might not run applications that use frames](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/knownissues.htm#)

**Details:** In Synthetic Monitoring, the Browser and Scripted Browser monitors might fail to run against applications that use frames.

**Workaround:** We are aware of the issue and working on a resolution. For Scripted Browser monitors, you can work around this issue by replacing `index=<frame-index>` with either `id=<id-of-frame>` or `name=<name-of-frame>` in the `.side` script.

For example, if this script is the original version:

    {
          "id": "23956f51-8812-40e6-ac91-1d608871ee4c",
          "comment": "",
          "command": "selectFrame",
          "target": "index=0",
          "targets": [\
            ["index=0"]\
          ],
          "value": ""
        }

The following script would be the modified version:

    {
          "id": "23956f51-8812-40e6-ac91-1d608871ee4c",
          "comment": "",
          "command": "selectFrame",
          "target": "id=frame1",
          "targets": [\
            ["id=frame1"]\
          ],
          "value": ""
        }

**Direct link to this issue:**[Browser and Scripted Browser monitors might not run applications that use frames](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/knownissues.htm#APM_scripted_browser_monitors)

[Issues with the authorization policies based on the apm-domains resource tags](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/knownissues.htm#)

**Details:** Authorization policies based on the `apm-domains` resource tags do not work for the Trace Explorer and Synthetic Monitoring APIs, causing authorization failures.

**Workaround:** We are aware of the issue and working on a resolution.

**Direct link to this issue:** [Issues with the authorization policies based on the apm-domains resource tags](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/knownissues.htm#apm_authorizationpolicies)

Artifact Registry
-----------------

For known issues with Artifact Registry, see [Known Issues](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/artifacts/known-issues.htm)
.

Audit
-----

Currently, there are no known Audit issues.

-------------------------

For known issues with Automated CEMLI Execution, see [Known Issues](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/auto-cemli-execution/knownissues_ace.htm)
.

.

.

.

Connector Hub
-------------

.

Console
-------

[Bug in the Firefox browser can cause the Console not to load](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/knownissues.htm#)

**Details:** When you try to access the Console using Firefox, the Console page never loads in the browser. This problem is likely caused by a corrupted Firefox user profile.

**Workaround:** Create a new Firefox user profile as follows:

1.  Ensure that you are on the latest version of Firefox. If not, update to the latest version.
2.  Create a new user profile and remove your old user profile. See Mozilla Support for instructions to create and remove user profiles: [https://support.mozilla.org/en-US/kb/profile-manager-create-and-remove-firefox-profiles](https://support.mozilla.org/en-US/kb/profile-manager-create-and-remove-firefox-profiles)
    .
3.  Open Firefox with the new profile.

Alternatively, you can use one of the other [Supported Browsers](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/GSG/Tasks/signinginIdentityDomain.htm#supported-browsers)
.

**Direct link to this issue:** [Bug in the Firefox browser can cause the Console not to load](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/knownissues.htm#Bug)

Console Dashboards
------------------

For known issues with Console Dashboards, see [Known Issues for Console Dashboards](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/Dashboards/Reference/known-issues.htm)
.

Container Instances
-------------------

For known issues with Container Instances, see [Known Issues for Container Instances](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/container-instances/known-issues.htm)
.

Container Registry
------------------

For known issues with Container Registry, see [Known Issues](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/Registry/known-issues/registry-known-issues.htm)
.

Data Catalog
------------
