December 2022

The following table outlines the new features for Oracle WebLogic Server for OKE Release 22.4.3:

If you are using Oracle WebLogic Server for OKE (Release 21.3.2 or earlier), see the documentation listed in the [Books](https://docs.oracle.com/pls/topic/lookup?ctx=en/cloud/paas/weblogic-container/new&id=weblogic-container-cloud-books)
 page (under Previous Release).

| Feature | Description |
| --- | --- |
| WebLogic Server 14.1.1.0.0 support | You can create non-JRF domains with WebLogic Server version 14.1.1.0.0 running on JDK version 8 or 14.1.1.0.0 running on JDK version 11.<br><br>See the following topics in Using Oracle WebLogic Server for OKE:<br><br>*   [Create a Domain](https://docs.oracle.com/pls/topic/lookup?ctx=en/cloud/paas/weblogic-container/new&id=WLKUG-GUID-DAA854F9-5F6A-4FE1-AE07-2AC64E5A127C)<br>     in Manage WebLogic Domains<br>*   [Create a Domain](https://docs.oracle.com/pls/topic/lookup?ctx=en/cloud/paas/weblogic-container/new&id=WLKUG-GUID-B74CF74E-C0C6-4564-8C41-A0FD1ED120DE)<br>     in Manage WebLogic Domains in Verrazzano |
| Product release version for stack listings | The stack listings for Oracle WebLogic Server for OKE now use the product release version. So when you launch a stack in the Marketplace, you must select the Oracle WebLogic Server for OKE release version from the Version list. |
| Patching utility tool | You can use the patching utility tool to list the latest patches and download the latest patches that are available in the catalog.<br><br>Note: To use these latest features for your existing Oracle WebLogic Server for OCI instances created before release December 14, 2022, you must upgrade the patching utility tool as described in [Upgrade Patching Tool Version](https://docs.oracle.com/pls/topic/lookup?ctx=en/cloud/paas/weblogic-container/new&id=WLKUG-GUID-CD54C88A-3C89-4883-8C56-C5998BA822C7)<br> in Using Oracle WebLogic Server for OKE..<br><br>See [List Patches](https://docs.oracle.com/pls/topic/lookup?ctx=en/cloud/paas/weblogic-container/new&id=WLKUG-GUID-74123781-3262-4047-A818-6E546B63FA72)<br> in Using Oracle WebLogic Server for OKE. |
| Use October PSUs | New domains include the October Patch Set Updates (PSUs) for Oracle WebLogic Server.<br><br>See [Patches Included in Oracle WebLogic Server for OKE](https://docs.oracle.com/pls/topic/lookup?ctx=en/cloud/paas/weblogic-container/new&id=WLKUG-GUID-DA265AF2-C32D-454A-9C62-7F4AE595B0FE)<br> in Using Oracle WebLogic Server for OKE. |

October 2022

The following table outlines the new features for Oracle WebLogic Server for OKE Release 22.4.1:

If you are using Oracle WebLogic Server for OKE (Release 21.3.2 or earlier), see the documentation listed in the [Books](https://docs.oracle.com/pls/topic/lookup?ctx=en/cloud/paas/weblogic-container/new&id=weblogic-container-cloud-books)
 page (under Previous Release).

| Feature | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Private subnet for load balancer | You can now also select a private subnet for the load balancer when you create a stack in Oracle WebLogic Server for OKE. See [Configure the Network](https://docs.oracle.com/pls/topic/lookup?ctx=en/cloud/paas/weblogic-container/new&id=WLKUG-GUID-8A153B88-9723-48B2-85E2-37B1FFF07E17)<br> and [Configure the Load Balancer](https://docs.oracle.com/pls/topic/lookup?ctx=en/cloud/paas/weblogic-container/new&id=WLKUG-GUID-7FE195EB-4187-42DE-8875-C8D6A352677B)<br> in Using Oracle WebLogic Server for OKE. |
| Provide new registry user name and token for pipeline jobs | When you deploy or undeploy a sample application, update a domain, and apply patches to a WebLogic Server installation, you can specify the OCIR credentials different than that you specified when creating a stack.<br><br>See the following topics in Using Oracle WebLogic Server for OKE:<br><br>*   [Deploy a Sample Application](https://docs.oracle.com/pls/topic/lookup?ctx=en/cloud/paas/weblogic-container/new&id=WLKUG-GUID-5D9EF81D-26DF-4CEF-84AB-CBC2DBB519FB)<br>    <br>*   [Update a Domain Configuration](https://docs.oracle.com/pls/topic/lookup?ctx=en/cloud/paas/weblogic-container/new&id=WLKUG-GUID-97721721-4953-46C6-BA36-4570236D6BCA)<br>    <br>*   [Apply a WebLogic Server Patch](https://docs.oracle.com/pls/topic/lookup?ctx=en/cloud/paas/weblogic-container/new&id=WLKUG-GUID-2CC776FD-BEEF-4458-A73D-742BBE24BE5F) |
| Use October PSUs | New domains include the October Patch Set Updates (PSUs) for Oracle WebLogic Server.<br><br>See [Patches Included in Oracle WebLogic Server for OKE](https://docs.oracle.com/pls/topic/lookup?ctx=en/cloud/paas/weblogic-container/new&id=WLKUG-GUID-DA265AF2-C32D-454A-9C62-7F4AE595B0FE)<br> in Using Oracle WebLogic Server for OKE. |

The following table outlines the new features for Oracle WebLogic Server for OKE Release 22.3.1:

 page (under Previous Release).

| Feature | Description |

| Application Configuration | When creating a WebLogic Domain in Verrazzano, you can use the create application configuration job to select a component, which allows to generate the basic application configuration with an IngressTrait and a MetricsTrait. See [Create a Stack](https://docs.oracle.com/pls/topic/lookup?ctx=en/cloud/paas/weblogic-container/new&id=WLKUG-GUID-8207EE2C-4CC0-4130-8A55-B813ADC7B9A0)<br> in Using Oracle WebLogic Server for OKE. |

June 2022

The following table outlines the new features for Oracle WebLogic Server for OKE Release 22.2.3:

 page (under Previous Release).

| Feature | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Support to select secrets | When creating a stack, you can now select the compartment where the secret is located and the secret that you want to use. See [Create a Stack](https://docs.oracle.com/pls/topic/lookup?ctx=en/cloud/paas/weblogic-container/new&id=WLKUG-GUID-C4F7CEBC-B1E7-4F43-8911-F18C62B61805)<br> in Using Oracle WebLogic Server for OKE. |
| Flexible and Standard3-based (Intel) shapes | Oracle WebLogic Server for OKE supports `BM.Standard3.64`, `VM.Standard.E3.Flex`, `VM.Standard.E4.Flex`, `VM.Standard3.Flex`, and `VM.Optimized3.Flex` shapes for the compute instances.<br><br>See the following topics in Using Oracle WebLogic Server for OKE:<br><br>*   [Compute](https://docs.oracle.com/pls/topic/lookup?ctx=en/cloud/paas/weblogic-container/new&id=WLKUG-GUID-376D3BB1-5C83-49A2-8997-BCBB1E5EBB62)<br>    <br>*   [Create a Stack](https://docs.oracle.com/pls/topic/lookup?ctx=en/cloud/paas/weblogic-container/new&id=WLKUG-GUID-C4F7CEBC-B1E7-4F43-8911-F18C62B61805)<br>    <br>*   [Create a Domain](https://docs.oracle.com/pls/topic/lookup?ctx=en/cloud/paas/weblogic-container/new&id=WLKUG-GUID-DAA854F9-5F6A-4FE1-AE07-2AC64E5A127C) |
| Use April PSUs | New domains include the April Patch Set Updates (PSUs) for Oracle WebLogic Server.<br><br>See [Patches Included in Oracle WebLogic Server for OKE](https://docs.oracle.com/pls/topic/lookup?ctx=en/cloud/paas/weblogic-container/new&id=WLKUG-GUID-DA265AF2-C32D-454A-9C62-7F4AE595B0FE)<br> in Using Oracle WebLogic Server for OKE. |

January 2022

The following table outlines the new features for Oracle WebLogic Server for OKE Release 22.1.1:

 page (under Previous Release).

| Feature | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Create database with database connect string for a JRF-enabled domain with OCIDB | For a JRF-enabled domain with OCIDB, you can create a datasource using the database connection string for an existing network only. In this case, Oracle WebLogic Server for OKE creates a single instance data source. However, you can update the data sources for Oracle WebLogic Suite and Oracle WebLogic Server Enterprise Edition. See [Configure Provision with JRF](https://docs.oracle.com/pls/topic/lookup?ctx=en/cloud/paas/weblogic-container/new&id=WLKUG-GUID-0137AF46-3E5D-415E-832F-6189D9772E8E)<br>. |

 page (under Previous Release).

| Feature | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Reuse WebLogic node pool | You can create a WebLogic node pool when you create a stack, and reuse this node pool when you create a domain using the Jenkins job.<br><br>See Configure the Container Cluster in [Create a Stack](https://docs.oracle.com/pls/topic/lookup?ctx=en/cloud/paas/weblogic-container/new&id=WLKUG-GUID-C4F7CEBC-B1E7-4F43-8911-F18C62B61805)<br> and [Create a Domain](https://docs.oracle.com/pls/topic/lookup?ctx=en/cloud/paas/weblogic-container/new&id=WLKUG-GUID-DAA854F9-5F6A-4FE1-AE07-2AC64E5A127C)<br>. |

 page (under Previous Release).

| Feature | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Use October PSUs | New domains include the October Patch Set Updates (PSUs) for Oracle WebLogic Server.<br><br>See [Patches Included in Oracle WebLogic Server for OKE](https://docs.oracle.com/pls/topic/lookup?ctx=en/cloud/paas/weblogic-container/new&id=WLKUG-GUID-DA265AF2-C32D-454A-9C62-7F4AE595B0FE)<br>. |

September 2021

The following table outlines the new features for Oracle WebLogic Server for OKE Release 21.3.3:

If you are using Oracle WebLogic Server for OKE (Release 21.3.2 or earlier), see the documentation listed in the [Books](https://docs.oracle.com/pls/topic/lookup?ctx=en/cloud/paas/weblogic-container/new&id=weblogic-container-cloud-books)
 page (under Previous Release).

| Feature | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Multi-domain Support | Multi-domain enables you to create multiple domains in a single Kubernetes cluster. You can create domains in the existing Kubernetes cluster using Jenkins CI/CD pipeline jobs. See [Manage WebLogic Domains](https://docs.oracle.com/pls/topic/lookup?ctx=en/cloud/paas/weblogic-container/new&id=WLKUG-GUID-03555250-F171-48C3-9D42-E6F4BD71B4E5)<br>. |
| Automatic Patching | You can use `Automatic Patching` Jenkins job to automatically schedule patching for the required domains. See [Automatic Patching](https://docs.oracle.com/pls/topic/lookup?ctx=en/cloud/paas/weblogic-container/new&id=WLKUG-GUID-E0D5C979-90DA-435E-A3FB-8635799CD6F6)<br>. |
| Bring Your Own Container Image | You can create custom base images or upload your own preferred base image, which you can use to create a Oracle WebLogic Server for OKE domain. See [Create a Custom Base Image](https://docs.oracle.com/pls/topic/lookup?ctx=en/cloud/paas/weblogic-container/new&id=WLKUG-GUID-D5A34A42-DBEA-49E3-8218-8C44BEE2A018)<br>. |
| Use July PSUs | New domains include the July Patch Set Updates (PSUs) for Oracle WebLogic Server.<br><br>See [Patches Included in Oracle WebLogic Server for OKE](https://docs.oracle.com/pls/topic/lookup?ctx=en/cloud/paas/weblogic-container/new&id=WLKUG-GUID-DA265AF2-C32D-454A-9C62-7F4AE595B0FE)<br>. |

August 2021

The following table outlines the new features for Oracle WebLogic Server for OKE Release 21.3.2:

| Feature | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Ingress controller namespace | The ingress controller namespace is changed from `ingress-nginx` to `wlsoke-ingress-nginx`. This namespace change is to avoid conflicts with the Verrazzano ingress controller namespace, if Verrazzano is installed later. |
| Use July PSUs | New domains include the July Patch Set Updates (PSUs) for Web Services Manager (WSM).<br><br>See [Patches Included in Oracle WebLogic Server for OKE](https://docs.oracle.com/pls/topic/lookup?ctx=en/cloud/paas/weblogic-container/new&id=WLKMD-GUID-DA265AF2-C32D-454A-9C62-7F4AE595B0FE)<br>. |

July 2021

The following table outlines the new features for Oracle WebLogic Server for OKE Release 21.3.1:

| Feature | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Security Checkup Tool | Oracle WebLogic Administration Server console includes a security checkup tool for security warnings. See [Security Checkup Tool](https://docs.oracle.com/pls/topic/lookup?ctx=en/cloud/paas/weblogic-container/new&id=WLKMD-GUID-4BEDDC7B-EB35-4C4C-82A2-E47BE88293A3)<br> in Using Oracle WebLogic Server for Oracle Cloud Infrastructure Container Engine for Kubernetes. |
| Load balancer with a reserved public IP | You can now provison a load balancer with a reserved public IP. See [Configure the Network](https://docs.oracle.com/pls/topic/lookup?ctx=en/cloud/paas/weblogic-container/new&id=WLKMD-GUID-8A153B88-9723-48B2-85E2-37B1FFF07E17)<br> in Using Oracle WebLogic Server for Oracle Cloud Infrastructure Container Engine for Kubernetes. |
| Use July PSUs | New domains include the July Patch Set Updates (PSUs) for Oracle WebLogic Server.<br><br>See [Patches Included in Oracle WebLogic Server for OKE](https://docs.oracle.com/pls/topic/lookup?ctx=en/cloud/paas/weblogic-container/new&id=WLKMD-GUID-DA265AF2-C32D-454A-9C62-7F4AE595B0FE)<br>. |

June 2021

The following table outlines the new features for Oracle WebLogic Server for OKE Release 21.2.3:

| Feature | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Update the Repository Schema Utility Password using Secrets | If you are using Model in Image and modified the Repository Schema Utility (RCU) password, then you must update the schema password in the domain.<br><br>See [Update the Repository Schema Utility Password using Secrets](https://docs.oracle.com/pls/topic/lookup?ctx=en/cloud/paas/weblogic-container/new&id=WLKMD-GUID-8408FCE1-9B26-4BC9-8E18-65905A29A351)<br> in Using Oracle WebLogic Server for Oracle Cloud Infrastructure Container Engine for Kubernetes |
| Managed SSH Sessions | Managed SSH Sessions for Bastion Service is now supported in instances created using Oracle WebLogic Server for OKE. |
| Use April PSUs | New domains include the April Patch Set Updates (PSUs) for Oracle WebLogic Server.<br><br>See [Patches Included in Oracle WebLogic Server for OKE](https://docs.oracle.com/pls/topic/lookup?ctx=en/cloud/paas/weblogic-container/new&id=WLKMD-GUID-DA265AF2-C32D-454A-9C62-7F4AE595B0FE)<br>. |

May 2021

The following table outlines the new features for Oracle WebLogic Server for OKE Release 21.2.2:

| Feature | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Model in Image | Oracle WebLogic Server for OKE now supports Model in Image to create WebLogic domain home configuration from Docker images. See [Model in Image](https://docs.oracle.com/pls/topic/lookup?ctx=en/cloud/paas/weblogic-container/new&id=WLKMD-GUID-C979B624-848B-4D0A-9F0F-C6F3CB4DB3B7)<br>. |
| Support for private endpoint configuration of Kubernetes cluster | You can configure the Kubernetes API endpoint of a cluster for an existing network with a new subnet or an existing subnet.<br><br>See [Configure the Network](https://docs.oracle.com/pls/topic/lookup?ctx=en/cloud/paas/weblogic-container/new&id=WLKMD-GUID-8A153B88-9723-48B2-85E2-37B1FFF07E17)<br> in Using Oracle WebLogic Server for Oracle Cloud Infrastructure Container Engine for Kubernetes. |
| Use April PSUs | New domains include the April Patch Set Updates (PSUs) for Oracle WebLogic Server.<br><br>See [Patches Included in Oracle WebLogic Server for OKE](https://docs.oracle.com/pls/topic/lookup?ctx=en/cloud/paas/weblogic-container/new&id=WLKMD-GUID-DA265AF2-C32D-454A-9C62-7F4AE595B0FE)<br>. |

April 2021

The following table outlines the new feature for Oracle WebLogic Server for OKE Release 21.2.1:

| Feature | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Use April PSUs | New domains include the April Patch Set Updates (PSUs) for Oracle WebLogic Server.<br><br>See [Patches Included in Oracle WebLogic Server for OKE](https://docs.oracle.com/pls/topic/lookup?ctx=en/cloud/paas/weblogic-container/new&id=WLKMD-GUID-DA265AF2-C32D-454A-9C62-7F4AE595B0FE)<br>. |

March 2021

The following table outlines the new feature for Oracle WebLogic Server for OKE Release 21.1.3:

| Feature | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Kubernetes configuration with existing cluster | You can provision an Oracle WebLogic Server for OKE domain using an existing cluster for an existing network setup. See [Configure the Container Cluster](https://docs.oracle.com/pls/topic/lookup?ctx=en/cloud/paas/weblogic-container/new&id=WLKMD-GUID-A0EDC635-7DB1-42C4-A241-E986EBFBFD8A)<br> in Using Oracle WebLogic Server for Oracle Cloud Infrastructure Container Engine for Kubernetes. |
| Policies for OS Management service | For compute instances created using the OS Management (OSMS) service, the policies required to access OSMS have been added. See [Create Policies for the Dynamic Group](https://docs.oracle.com/pls/topic/lookup?ctx=en/cloud/paas/weblogic-container/new&id=WLKMD-GUID-ABB89CFB-A6F1-4915-A16E-7ADDC4192F57)<br> in Using Oracle WebLogic Server for Oracle Cloud Infrastructure Container Engine for Kubernetes. |
| Flexible load balancer shape | For both private and public load balancers, you can now create a flexible shape with a minimum and maximum bandwidth size.<br><br>See Configure the Network in the [Create a Basic Domain](https://docs.oracle.com/pls/topic/lookup?ctx=en/cloud/paas/weblogic-container/new&id=WLKMD-GUID-C4F7CEBC-B1E7-4F43-8911-F18C62B61805)<br> and [Create a JRF-Enabled Domain](https://docs.oracle.com/pls/topic/lookup?ctx=en/cloud/paas/weblogic-container/new&id=WLKMD-GUID-39BA9CEE-997D-4E41-8C35-07FC01C33722)<br> sections in Using Oracle WebLogic Server for Oracle Cloud Infrastructure Container Engine for Kubernetes. |
| Use January PSUs | New domains include the January Patch Set Updates (PSUs) for Oracle WebLogic Server.<br><br>See [Patches Included in Oracle WebLogic Server for OKE](https://docs.oracle.com/pls/topic/lookup?ctx=en/cloud/paas/weblogic-container/new&id=WLKMD-GUID-DA265AF2-C32D-454A-9C62-7F4AE595B0FE)<br>. |

February 2021

The following table outlines the new feature for Oracle WebLogic Server for OKE Release 21.1.2:

| Feature | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Terraform Scripts | You can access the Terraform Scripts of an Oracle WebLogic Server for Oracle Cloud Infrastructure to perform Oracle Cloud Infrastructure tasks using the Oracle Cloud Infrastructure (OCI) Command Line Interface.(CLI). See [Terraform Scripts in Oracle WebLogic Server for OKE](https://docs.oracle.com/pls/topic/lookup?ctx=en/cloud/paas/weblogic-container/new&id=WLKTS-GUID-868D9729-D90B-48A6-84E7-00C5C8AA5B9B)<br>. |
| WebLogic Server authentication using Oracle Identity Cloud Service | Use Oracle WebLogic Server for OKE to create a domain that integrates the Oracle WebLogic Server security realm with Oracle Identity Cloud Service. As a result, users that access the administration console or your Java applications are authenticated against Oracle Identity Cloud Service. See these topics in Using Oracle WebLogic Server for Oracle Cloud Infrastructure Container Engine for Kubernetes:<br><br>*   [About the Components of Oracle WebLogic Server for OKE](https://docs.oracle.com/pls/topic/lookup?ctx=en/cloud/paas/weblogic-container/new&id=WLKMD-GUID-D2C79FB8-EC82-41B2-9D39-5FC9D71D1242)<br>    <br>*   [Before You Begin with Oracle WebLogic Server for OKE](https://docs.oracle.com/pls/topic/lookup?ctx=en/cloud/paas/weblogic-container/new&id=WLKMD-GUID-68139E1B-ACF4-46E8-8AAB-EA6951AF0248)<br>    <br>*   [Create a Basic Domain](https://docs.oracle.com/pls/topic/lookup?ctx=en/cloud/paas/weblogic-container/new&id=WLKMD-GUID-C4F7CEBC-B1E7-4F43-8911-F18C62B61805) |
| Utilities to manage servers | You can now use scripts to manage the lifecycle operations on the WebLogic pods. See [Start and Stop Servers](https://docs.oracle.com/pls/topic/lookup?ctx=en/cloud/paas/weblogic-container/new&id=WLKMD-GUID-3F412AE8-5380-4D89-8649-3ED96E39B973)<br> in Using Oracle WebLogic Server for Oracle Cloud Infrastructure Container Engine for Kubernetes. |
| Resource name prefix character length | The length of the resource name prefix name is extended to 16 characters. |
| Use January PSUs | New domains include the January Patch Set Updates (PSUs) for Oracle WebLogic Server.<br><br>See [Patches Included in Oracle WebLogic Server for OKE](https://docs.oracle.com/pls/topic/lookup?ctx=en/cloud/paas/weblogic-container/new&id=WLKMD-GUID-DA265AF2-C32D-454A-9C62-7F4AE595B0FE)<br>. |

January 2021

The following table outlines the new feature for Oracle WebLogic Server for OKE Release 21.1.1:

| Feature | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Patching tool utility | You can use the patching tool utility to download the patches for the WebLogic compute instance and the bastion instance if you do not have access to the support portal to download the required patches. See [Download Patches Using the Patching Tool Utility](https://docs.oracle.com/pls/topic/lookup?ctx=en/cloud/paas/weblogic-container/new&id=WLKMD-GUID-11E0FB5C-0B53-4153-9073-EE1EF5FB377C)<br> in Using Oracle WebLogic Server for Oracle Cloud Infrastructure Container Engine for Kubernetes. |
| Use January PSUs | New domains include the January Patch Set Updates (PSUs) for Oracle WebLogic Server.<br><br>See [Patches Included in Oracle WebLogic Server for OKE](https://docs.oracle.com/pls/topic/lookup?ctx=en/cloud/paas/weblogic-container/new&id=WLKMD-GUID-DA265AF2-C32D-454A-9C62-7F4AE595B0FE)<br>. |

December 2020

The following table outlines the new feature for Oracle WebLogic Server for OKE Release 20.4.3:

| Feature | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Session Persistence | Configure session persistence when deploying Java EE applications to a WebLogic cluster. See [Session Persistence Considerations](https://docs.oracle.com/pls/topic/lookup?ctx=en/cloud/paas/weblogic-container/new&id=WLKMD-GUID-C0470F60-220E-4E2D-840E-509C3409BF6D)<br> in Using Oracle WebLogic Server for Oracle Cloud Infrastructure Container Engine for Kubernetes. |
| Cloud Shell scripts to validate existing network setup | You can run scripts from the Cloud Shell to validate the existing network setup if you run into issues during provisioning. See [Validate Existing Network Setup](https://docs.oracle.com/pls/topic/lookup?ctx=en/cloud/paas/weblogic-container/new&id=WLKMD-GUID-F3EA1798-F276-4188-ACA0-B7858A1D6B1E)<br> in Using Oracle WebLogic Server for Oracle Cloud Infrastructure Container Engine for Kubernetes. |
| Bastion configuration is optional | You can configure a bastion compute instance on a public subnet to provide access to the Container Cluster worker nodes and the administration instances that are provisioned in private subnets. See [Configure the Compute Instances](https://docs.oracle.com/pls/topic/lookup?ctx=en/cloud/paas/weblogic-container/new&id=WLKUG-GUID-F10087ED-CB16-4C49-A377-4786875DA554)<br> and [Configure the Network](https://docs.oracle.com/pls/topic/lookup?ctx=en/cloud/paas/weblogic-container/new&id=WLKMD-GUID-8A153B88-9723-48B2-85E2-37B1FFF07E17)<br> in Using Oracle WebLogic Server for Oracle Cloud Infrastructure Container Engine for Kubernetes. |
| Subnet in a separate compartment | You can select a subnet compartment that is different than the VCN compartment when you're using an existing subnet. See [Configure the Network](https://docs.oracle.com/pls/topic/lookup?ctx=en/cloud/paas/weblogic-container/new&id=WLKMD-GUID-8A153B88-9723-48B2-85E2-37B1FFF07E17)<br> in Using Oracle WebLogic Server for Oracle Cloud Infrastructure Container Engine for Kubernetes. |
| Use October PSUs | New domains include the October Patch Set Updates (PSUs) for Oracle WebLogic Server.<br><br>See [Patches Included in Oracle WebLogic Server for OKE](https://docs.oracle.com/pls/topic/lookup?ctx=en/cloud/paas/weblogic-container/new&id=WLKMD-GUID-DA265AF2-C32D-454A-9C62-7F4AE595B0FE)<br>. |

November 2020

The following table outlines the new feature for Oracle WebLogic Server for OKE Release 20.4.2:

| Feature | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Kubernetes Secret Encryption | When you create a domain, you can encrypt the Kubernetes secrets at rest in `etcd` by using the master encryption key in the OCI vault service.<br><br>See Configure the Kubernetes Cluster when you [Create a Basic Domain](https://docs.oracle.com/pls/topic/lookup?ctx=en/cloud/paas/weblogic-container/new&id=WLKMD-GUID-C4F7CEBC-B1E7-4F43-8911-F18C62B61805)<br> or [Create a JRF-Enabled Domain](https://docs.oracle.com/pls/topic/lookup?ctx=en/cloud/paas/weblogic-container/new&id=WLKMD-GUID-39BA9CEE-997D-4E41-8C35-07FC01C33722)<br> in Using Oracle WebLogic Server for Oracle Cloud Infrastructure Container Engine for Kubernetes. |
| Root policy creation is optional | When you create a domain, by default the OCI Policies check box is selected and Oracle WebLogic Server for OKE creates a dynamic group and relevant root-level (tenancy) policies. You need to create a dynamic group only when a user creates a domain and does not select OCI Policies.<br><br>If you're an administrator, see [Create a Dynamic Group](https://docs.oracle.com/pls/topic/lookup?ctx=en/cloud/paas/weblogic-container/new&id=WLKMD-GUID-D696FB45-1105-44D8-87A0-0D5C2FFA50C7)<br> and [Create Root Policies](https://docs.oracle.com/pls/topic/lookup?ctx=en/cloud/paas/weblogic-container/new&id=WLKMD-GUID-CCE511FB-7A6D-4625-9A32-253F9DDCA393)<br> in Using Oracle WebLogic Server for Oracle Cloud Infrastructure Container Engine for Kubernetes. |
| Added compartment policies | If you are not an Oracle Cloud Infrastructure administrator, you must be given management access to resources in the compartment in which you want to create a domain using Oracle WebLogic Server for OKE.<br><br>If you're an administrator, see [Create Compartment Polices](https://docs.oracle.com/pls/topic/lookup?ctx=en/cloud/paas/weblogic-container/new&id=WLKGS-GUID-A05B6E62-B4B9-4A8E-9AD5-7F8A43A207C8)<br> in Using Oracle WebLogic Server for Oracle Cloud Infrastructure Container Engine for Kubernetes. |
| Dynamic group and root-level policy | When you create a domain, Oracle WebLogic Server for OKE creates one dynamic group and one root-level (tenancy) policy. This root-level policy has multiple statements that are scoped to compartment IDs, resource IDs, or both.<br><br>See [Identity Resources for Dynamic Group and Root Policies](https://docs.oracle.com/pls/topic/lookup?ctx=en/cloud/paas/weblogic-container/new&id=WLKMD-GUID-58E299A9-D52B-4957-A0DA-51235C867318)<br>. |
| Mount target in different compartment than the stack for existing network | When you configure the File System to provision Oracle WebLogic Server for OKE using an existing subnet, the mount target can be created in a different compartment than the stack. See [Configure the File System](https://docs.oracle.com/pls/topic/lookup?ctx=en/cloud/paas/weblogic-container/new&id=WLKMD-GUID-D944A0F4-E7F9-4D63-8ED0-1C2324AD5645)<br>. |
| Use October PSUs | New domains include the October Patch Set Updates (PSUs) for Oracle WebLogic Server.<br><br>See [Patches Included in Oracle WebLogic Server for OKE](https://docs.oracle.com/pls/topic/lookup?ctx=en/cloud/paas/weblogic-container/new&id=WLKMD-GUID-DA265AF2-C32D-454A-9C62-7F4AE595B0FE)<br>. |

Documentation Accessibility

For information about Oracle's commitment to accessibility, visit the Oracle Accessibility Program website at [http://www.oracle.com/pls/topic/lookup?ctx=acc&id=docacc](https://docs.oracle.com/pls/topic/lookup?ctx=acc&id=docacc)
.

Access to Oracle Support

Oracle customers that have purchased support have access to electronic support through My Oracle Support. For information, visit [http://www.oracle.com/pls/topic/lookup?ctx=acc&id=info](https://docs.oracle.com/pls/topic/lookup?ctx=acc&id=info)
 or visit [http://www.oracle.com/pls/topic/lookup?ctx=acc&id=trs](https://docs.oracle.com/pls/topic/lookup?ctx=acc&id=trs)
 if you are hearing impaired.

Oracle AI Database Documentation
================================

Oracle provides a range of industry-leading on-premises and cloud-based solutions to meet the data management requirements from small and medium sized businesses to large global enterprises.

Oracle AI Database
------------------

### [Oracle AI Database](https://docs.oracle.com/en/database/oracle/oracle-database/index.html)

Oracle AI Database is the world’s most popular database. [Oracle AI Database 26ai](https://docs.oracle.com/en/database/oracle/oracle-database/26/index.html)
, the next Generation Oracle AI Database, is now available as a Base Database Service delivering the most complete and simple converged database for developers looking to build new microservice, graph, document, and relational applications. Oracle AI Database 26ai Free is also available for free as a simple download for ease of use.

*   [Oracle AI Database 26ai](https://docs.oracle.com/en/database/oracle/oracle-database/26/index.html)
    
*   [Oracle Database 21c](https://docs.oracle.com/en/database/oracle/oracle-database/21/index.html)
    
*   [Oracle Database 19c](https://docs.oracle.com/en/database/oracle/oracle-database/19/index.html)
    
*   [Oracle Database 18c](https://docs.oracle.com/en/database/oracle/oracle-database/18/index.html)
    

### [Oracle AI Database Cloud Services](https://docs.cloud.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Database/Concepts/databaseoverview.htm)

Oracle Cloud provides several services to create and manage Oracle AI Database deployments quickly and easily.

Related Products
----------------

Oracle also offers a comprehensive set of products related to Oracle AI Database to meet your database needs. Select a category below to access the documentation for the related products in each category.
